Trump Hormuz deadline day (8PM ET). No major new strikes reported on UAE territory overnight. Ruwais operations remain suspended. OPEC+ announces 206k bbl/d output increase.

MoD: intercepted 12 BM, 2 CM, 19 UAVs on Apr 6. Cumulative: 519 BM, 26 CM, 2,210 drones. Ghanaian national injured by shrapnel in Abu Dhabi. Du telecom building in Fujairah hit by drone. Mussaffah debris incident. Borouge petrochemical fires from debris.

TWO-WEEK CEASEFIRE IN EFFECT. No new Iranian strikes reported. Khor Fakkan port resumes limited ops. Ruwais refinery restart assessment underway. Borouge fires from Apr 7 debris fully contained. MoD cumulative: 531 BM, 28 CM, 2,232 drones.

CEASEFIRE DAY 2. No new Iranian strikes. Iran re-closed Strait of Hormuz citing Israeli Lebanon strikes. Ruwais refinery restart delayed pending Hormuz reopening. Khor Fakkan port ops paused again. ADNOC assessing cumulative damage across Habshan and Ruwais facilities. MoD cumulative: 531 BM, 28 CM, 2,232 drones.

CEASEFIRE DAY 3. No new Iranian strikes. Vance-led US delegation departs for Islamabad talks. ADNOC CEO says Hormuz 'not open' — access restricted and controlled. Ruwais refinery restart remains on hold. Khor Fakkan port limited to outbound domestic cargo only. MoD cumulative: 531 BM, 28 CM, 2,232 drones.

CEASEFIRE DAY 4. No new Iranian strikes. Islamabad talks commence — Vance-led US delegation and Iranian Deputy FM Takht-Ravanchi meet Saturday morning. ADNOC CEO reiterates Hormuz 'not open' — only 2 tankers transited since ceasefire. Ruwais refinery restart remains on hold pending Hormuz access. Khor Fakkan port limited to outbound domestic cargo. MoD cumulative: 531 BM, 28 CM, 2,232 drones.

CEASEFIRE DAY 5. No new Iranian strikes. Islamabad talks enter overnight marathon — trilateral session (US-Iran-Pakistan) began past 2 AM local. Hormuz remains effectively closed; only IRGC-approved civilian vessels transiting under escort. ADNOC says Khor Fakkan outbound-only ops continue. DFC $40B reinsurance backstop drawing shipowner interest. War-risk premiums dipping but still elevated.

CEASEFIRE DAY 6. No new Iranian strikes. US naval blockade of Hormuz begins 10am ET per Trump order. Al Taweelah aluminum plant damage assessment ongoing. Dubai stocks fall 2.1% pre-market on blockade news.

CEASEFIRE DAY 7. No new Iranian strikes. US naval blockade of Iranian ports takes effect 10 AM ET — CENTCOM clarifies non-Iranian port traffic through Hormuz unimpeded. ADNOC Khor Fakkan outbound-only ops continue. Dubai Financial Market drops 3.2% at open on blockade escalation fears. IRGC warns approaching military vessels will be treated as ceasefire violation. Al Taweelah aluminum plant still offline.

CEASEFIRE DAY 8. No new Iranian strikes. US naval blockade of Iran enters Day 3. UAE reopens Abu Dhabi International Airport for limited commercial flights. Emirates resumes select routes to Europe and Asia. Second round of US-Iran talks under discussion in Islamabad.

CEASEFIRE DAY 9. No new Iranian strikes. US naval blockade Day 4 ongoing. Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir in Tehran carrying new US message; second round of talks expected within days. Emirates repair crews continue infrastructure restoration at Al Taweelah.

Ceasefire Day 27 (DE FACTO BROKEN) / WPR 60-day deadline +3. Sharp escalation overnight as Project Freedom commences operationally: UAE MoD logs 9 BM tracks intercepted (4 west of Al Dhafra, 2 northwest of Sila, 1 over Liwa corridor, 2 outliers toward Fujairah) by PAC-3/SM-3/SAMP-T; 17 UAVs downed by NASAMS/Skyguard across Fujairah/Khor Fakkan/Liwa/Al Dhafra approaches; 1 cruise missile intercepted over Gulf of Oman littoral by SM-2 from CG-69. Tempo nearly doubled vs Day 65 (4 BM / 9 UAV) tracking Iranian 'practical action' rhetoric becoming kinetic post-IRGC declaration that Project Freedom is a ceasefire violation. No casualties or infrastructure damage. MbZ chaired emergency Federal National Defence Council session at 0530 reviewing Jask warship strike (US destroyer hit by 2 ASCMs near Jask Island, forced to turn back per IRGC/Fars). Houthi spokesman repeats UAE-specific threat over Hormuz support. ADNOC running Ruwais at 102% nameplate; Fujairah crude flow nominal but underwriter pressure mounting. CENTCOM PAC-3/SM-3 resupply through Al Dhafra resumed (4th sortie this week). Cumulative regional Iran BM tally 1,492 since Feb 28 (UAE intercepted 558 BM / 2,300 UAV / 27 cruise per AD JOC). Etihad cargo nominal; DXB T3 normal; ADNOC LNG Das spot lifts unaffected. Source: WAM/UAE MoD daily readout, AD JOC, Al Jazeera live blog, CENTCOM PAO.

Ceasefire Day 28 (DE FACTO BROKEN, Day 2 of overt kinetic exchange). UAE MoD daily readout: 7 BM tracks intercepted overnight (3 west of Al Dhafra by PAC-3, 2 over Liwa by SAMP-T, 2 outliers north of Sila by SM-3 from CG-69), 14 UAVs downed (NASAMS/Skyguard) across Fujairah/Khor Fakkan/Liwa/Al Dhafra approaches, 2 cruise missiles intercepted over Gulf of Oman littoral by SM-2 from DDG-class. One UAV penetrated outer ring near Fujairah Oil Terminal at 0247 local — minor fire at Tank Farm 7 (extinguished within 90 min, 1 worker treated for smoke inhalation, no production impact); first confirmed infrastructure hit on UAE soil since pre-ceasefire. ADNOC reports Ruwais running 99% nameplate; Fujairah crude/products loading nominal but underwriter Lloyd's Joint War Committee re-listed UAE territorial waters as 'enhanced risk' overnight (rate +0.35%). MbZ chaired second emergency Federal National Defence Council session in 24h; statement condemns 'reckless escalation' and reiterates UAE will defend airspace 'with full coalition support.' CENTCOM forward-deployed PAC-3 reload sortie #5 this week landed Al Dhafra 0930. Cumulative regional Iran BM tally now ~1,499 since Feb 28 (UAE intercepted 565 BM / 2,314 UAV / 29 cruise per AD JOC). Etihad cargo nominal; DXB T3 nominal; ADNOC LNG Das spot lifts unaffected. Source: WAM/UAE MoD, AD JOC, Al Jazeera live blog, CENTCOM PAO, Lloyd's JWC bulletin.

Ceasefire Day 29 (DE FACTO BROKEN, Day 3 of overt kinetic exchange / Project Freedom Day 3 — PAUSED at ~1900Z by US POTUS announcement). UAE MoD daily readout: 4 BM tracks intercepted overnight (2 west of Al Dhafra by PAC-3, 1 north of Sila by SM-3 from CG-69, 1 outlier on Liwa axis by SAMP-T), 9 UAVs downed (NASAMS/Skyguard) across Fujairah/Khor Fakkan/Liwa approaches, 1 cruise missile intercepted east of Fujairah by SeaRAM. Tempo down ~40% vs Day 67 — Iranian volley appears partially throttled in last 6 hours of overnight window after Beijing FM-level Wang Yi/Araghchi readout and Trump 'Project Freedom paused' Truth Social post. Fujairah Tank Farm 7 fire fully extinguished 1130Z; ADNOC says 4 of 7 berths reopened to escorted traffic, full Fujairah throughput remains <50% pre-war. UAE FM Sheikh Abdullah ibn Zayed delivers terse statement: 'We welcome any pause that produces verified de-escalation. Until Iranian fires stop, our defenses remain at maximum readiness.' President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed convenes Supreme National Security Council; UAE keeps DEFCON-equivalent posture at elevated tier. Iran President Pezeshkian publicly rebukes IRGC ('utter madness') over the unauthorized May 4-5 strike series — first daylight signal of internal Tehran command schism.

Ceasefire Day 2. No new inbound. Ras Laffan LNG output still down ~17%. QatarEnergy force majeure remains on long-term contracts. Doha hosting preparatory diplomatic consultations ahead of Islamabad talks Friday. Qatar Emir spoke with Pakistan PM Sharif.

Ceasefire Day 3. No inbound. Ras Laffan LNG output still down ~17%. QatarEnergy FM on long-term contracts unchanged. Qatar Emir coordinating with Pakistan PM Sharif ahead of Islamabad talks today. Doha diplomatic channels active.

Ceasefire Day 4. No inbound. Ras Laffan LNG output still down ~17%. QatarEnergy force majeure on long-term contracts unchanged. Qatar Emir in contact with Pakistan PM Sharif as Islamabad talks open. Doha diplomatic channels active supporting Pakistani mediation.

Ceasefire Day 5. No inbound. Ras Laffan LNG output still reduced ~17%. QatarEnergy force majeure on long-term contracts remains in effect. LNG carriers have aborted Hormuz transits due to IRGC restrictions. ~800 freighters remain stuck in Persian Gulf holding pattern.

Ceasefire Day 6. No inbound. Ras Laffan LNG output still reduced ~17%. Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar urges both sides to uphold ceasefire after Islamabad talks collapse. Qatar FM consulting Tehran on next steps.

Ceasefire Day 7. No inbound threats. Ras Laffan LNG output still reduced ~17% from earlier damage. Qatar FM Al Thani in contact with Tehran and Washington urging resumption of talks. China FM Wang Yi calls for preserving 'extremely fragile' ceasefire. Asian LNG spot prices surge 12% on blockade fears.

Ceasefire Day 8. No inbound threats. Ras Laffan LNG output remains reduced (~40% capacity). Qatar mediating alongside Pakistan on potential second round of Islamabad talks. Doha hosts emergency GCC energy ministers meeting on Hormuz transit.
Ceasefire Day 9. No inbound threats. Ras Laffan LNG complex repair ongoing; reduced global LNG output continues. Qatar mediating alongside Pakistan on ceasefire extension talks.

Asian LNG spot premium >145% pre-war on Brent climbing through $108/bbl. Qatar MFA continues mediation contacts with Iran via Muscat channel; Pakistani envoy Sadiq remains in Doha coordinating with Omani envoy. Ceasefire Day 22. No engagements reported overnight. Ras Laffan LNG aggregate output ~48% pre-war; QatarEnergy Train 5 restart slipped again to May 2 pending power-supply revalidation. Asian LNG spot >$25/MMBtu (~150% pre-war) on Brent settling $111.26 Apr 28 (+3% D/D). Qatar floated Doha as Round 3 venue after Trump Apr 25 cancellation of Witkoff/Kushner Islamabad trip. Post-Mar 10 reporting gap to formal MoD daily readout now 50 days.

Ceasefire Day 23. No engagements reported overnight. Ras Laffan LNG aggregate output ~48% pre-war; QatarEnergy Train 5 restart still slipped to May 2 pending power-supply revalidation. Asian LNG spot >$26/MMBtu (~155% pre-war) on Brent settling $118.03 Apr 29 (+6% D/D) — panic-buying behavior reported across Pacific basin per S&P Global Platts. Doha confirms Round 3 venue offer formally tabled to both Tehran and Washington after Trump Apr 25 cancellation of Witkoff/Kushner Islamabad trip. Qatar MFA continues coordinating with Pakistani envoy Sadiq and Omani envoy on Iranian red-lines transit. Post-Mar 10 reporting gap to formal MoD daily readout now 51 days.

Ceasefire Day 24. No engagements reported overnight; Qatar MoD silence streak now 52 days post-Mar 10. Ceasefire Day 25. No engagements reported overnight; Qatar MoD silence streak now 53 days post-Mar 10. Ras Laffan LNG aggregate output ~48% pre-war; QatarEnergy Train 5 restart slipped a third time (May 4 target) pending power-supply revalidation. Asian LNG spot >$26.50/MMBtu (~158% pre-war) on Brent settling $120+. Doha hosts second-track Pakistani-mediated channel; PM Mohammed bin Abdulrahman convened ministerial review on WPR-deadline diplomatic offramp options. QatarEnergy commercial extending force-majeure on May-loading Asian LNG cargoes through H2 May. Source: QatarEnergy notices, QNA, Reuters LNG.

Ceasefire Day 26. No engagements reported overnight; Qatar MoD silence streak now 54 days post-Mar 10. Ras Laffan LNG aggregate output ~48% pre-war; QatarEnergy Train 5 restart held to May 4 target with no slippage announced. Asian LNG spot ~$26.30/MMBtu (~157% pre-war) with Brent settling in $108-114 band on Iran 14-point counterproposal review. Doha hosts Pakistani-mediated second-track channel; PM Mohammed bin Abdulrahman convened weekend ministerial session to review Iran's 30-day-vs-US-2-month timeline divergence in counterproposal (Fars/Tasnim leak). QatarEnergy commercial extends force-majeure on May-loading Asian LNG cargoes through May 16 (was H2 May). Source: QatarEnergy notices, QNA, Reuters LNG.

Ceasefire Day 27 (DE FACTO BROKEN). Qatar MoD silence streak 55 days post-Mar 10. Ras Laffan LNG aggregate output ~48% pre-war; QatarEnergy Train 5 restart slipped from May 4 to May 11 target citing 'security review' tied to Project Freedom kickoff. Asian LNG spot rallied to ~$28.40/MMBtu (~170% pre-war) on Hormuz warship strike news. PM Mohammed bin Abdulrahman convened emergency 0700 ministerial; Doha reactivates Pakistani-mediated backchannel after Trump publicly rejected Iran 14-point counterproposal as 'unacceptable' overnight. QatarEnergy commercial extends force majeure on May-loading Asian LNG cargoes through May 23 (was May 16). Doha International nominal; Hamad Port lift queue stable but underwriters reissuing breach notices. Source: QatarEnergy notices, QNA, Reuters LNG, Times of Israel.

Ceasefire Day 28 (DE FACTO BROKEN). Qatar MoD silence streak 56 days post-Mar 10. Ras Laffan LNG aggregate output ~46% pre-war (slight slip vs Day 66 on harbor-pilotage tightening); QatarEnergy Train 5 restart held to May 11 target, 'security review' continuing. Asian LNG spot ~$29.10/MMBtu (~174% pre-war) on overnight Hormuz boat exchange and Fujairah tank-farm fire. PM Mohammed bin Abdulrahman convened 0700 ministerial; Doha confirms Pakistani backchannel 'remains open but increasingly strained' after Iranian FM Araghchi's 'Project Freedom is Project Deadlock' statement and Trump's overnight 'unacceptable' rejection of Iran 14-point counterproposal. QatarEnergy commercial extends force majeure on May-loading Asian LNG cargoes through May 30 (was May 23). Doha Hamad Port nominal; lift queue stable but underwriters reissuing breach notices. Cum: 414 drones, 226 missiles. Camp Canada attack details released. Several drones hit Kuwait Intl Airport — material damage, 0 injuries. Source: Kuwait Times/KUNA.
Airport radar damaged again. Extended closure; flights diverted. No new BMs recorded in Mar 13-23 lull (confirmed by KUNA/Wikipedia). Drone attacks continued.

Ceasefire in effect. Overnight curfew (midnight-6AM) lifted. Ali Al Salem AFB: 15 Americans wounded Apr 7 receiving treatment. KPC damage assessment continues. Kuwait Airport fuel tanks fire extinguished.

Ceasefire Day 2. No new attacks. Ali Al Salem AFB wounded Americans stable. KPC restoring power station and desalination plant operations damaged Apr 8. Kuwait Airport fuel tanks area secured. Parliament emergency session on reconstruction funding.

Ceasefire Day 3. No attacks. KPC restoration of power station and desalination plant from Apr 8 damage continues. Ali Al Salem AFB wounded Americans stable. Parliament emergency session on $2.4B reconstruction funding package.

Ceasefire Day 4. No attacks. KPC power station and desalination plant restoration continues. Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery fires extinguished; damage assessment underway. Parliament approves $2.4B reconstruction funding package. Ali Al Salem wounded stable.

Ceasefire Day 5. No attacks. IRGC denied responsibility for Apr 10 drone incident near Ali Al Salem; blamed Israel/US. KPC infrastructure restoration ongoing. Apr 6 Arash-2 kamikaze drone attack on Ali Al Salem injured 15 US personnel.
Ceasefire Day 6. No attacks. Kuwait Emir issues statement calling for renewed diplomacy. KPC reports oil exports via southern terminals remain disrupted by Hormuz restrictions.

Ceasefire Day 7. No new incidents. State Department evacuation order for non-emergency personnel in effect. Kuwait Petroleum Corp stockpiling crude in onshore storage as exports via Gulf remain constrained.
Ceasefire Day 8. No new incidents. KPC spot export suspension extended. Al Zour refinery remains evacuated. Kuwait Premier Market index down 15.5% from pre-war levels.
Ceasefire Day 9. No new incidents. KPC force majeure on Asian crude shipments remains in effect. Mina al-Ahmadi refinery repairs continue.
KUNA states airspace remains clear, no hostile targets detected today.

Ceasefire Day 11 / Blockade Day 6. No new incidents. KPC force majeure on Asian crude shipments active. Mina al-Ahmadi Unit 4 on partial duty; Al Zour repair track 6-8 weeks. Kuwait Premier Market index +0.9% intraday on ceasefire-extension optimism; retreated on Rubio snapback-sanctions warning.
Ceasefire Day 12. MoD silent on numerics; cadence reliability remains 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput recovered to ~82% pre-war. Emir confirms continued mediation posture with Oman.

Ceasefire Day 13. MoD still silent on numerics; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput at ~84% pre-war, +2pp vs Day 51. Emir reaffirms shared mediation posture with Oman; no Kuwaiti combat sorties attributed.

Ceasefire Day 14. MoD continues silence on daily numerics; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput edges to ~85% pre-war. Emir reiterates shared mediation posture with Oman; National Assembly extends emergency budget authority 30 days.

Ceasefire Day 15. Kuwait MoD maintains silence on daily numerics; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput remains near pre-war baseline. Emir/Oman joint mediation posture holds. Continuation entry pending National Assembly statement.

Ceasefire Day 16. Kuwait MoD maintains silence on daily numerics; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline. Emir/Oman joint mediation posture holds. Continuation entry pending National Assembly statement.

Ceasefire Day 17. Kuwait MoD silence continues on daily numerics; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline. Emir convened National Security Bureau session on Hormuz contingency shipping reroute options via Saudi East-West pipeline. Oman joint mediation posture holds.

Ceasefire Day 18. Kuwait MoD silence continues; cadence reliability 0.65. Mina al-Ahmadi exports near pre-war baseline. Emir convened second National Security Bureau session of week on Hormuz reroute via Saudi East-West pipeline; KPC commercial team in Riyadh.

Ceasefire Day 19. Quiet day for Kuwaiti air-defense activations. Government communique called Round 2 collapse 'a deeply concerning setback' and reiterated joint mediation offer with Oman. KPC May-loading lift schedule for Asia partially reinstated.

Ceasefire Day 20. Kuwait MoD silence continues on daily numerics; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline. Emir held third National Security Bureau session of week on Hormuz reroute via Saudi East-West pipeline; KPC commercial team finalizing May-loading lift adjustments with Asian buyers. Joint mediation posture with Oman and Qatar reaffirmed after Round 2 collapse.

Ceasefire Day 21. Kuwait MoD silence continues on daily numerics; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline. Emir held National Security Bureau session on Hormuz reroute via Saudi East-West pipeline; KPC commercial team finalized May-loading lift adjustments with Asian buyers (premium ~$3.20/bbl over Dubai benchmark). Joint mediation posture with Oman and Qatar reaffirmed; Kuwait MFA welcomed Araghchi-Putin St. Petersburg engagement as constructive.

Ceasefire Day 22. Kuwait MoD silence continues; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline. Emir held National Security Bureau session on Trump Apr 28 'state of collapse' rhetoric and prospects for Round 3 talks restart. KPC commercial team finalized May-loading lift adjustments with Asian buyers (premium widened to ~$3.45/bbl over Dubai benchmark). Joint mediation posture with Oman and Qatar reaffirmed; Kuwait MFA welcomed Araghchi-Putin St. Petersburg readout and noted Russian FM Lavrov 'diplomatic-only resolution' framing as constructive.

Ceasefire Day 23. Kuwait MoD silence continues; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline. Emir held second National Security Bureau session this week on Trump Apr 29 blockade extension and prospects for Round 3 talks restart in Doha. KPC commercial team finalizing accelerated May-loading lift adjustments with Asian buyers (premium widened to ~$3.95/bbl over Dubai benchmark). Joint mediation posture with Oman and Qatar reaffirmed; Kuwait MFA welcomed Putin offer to store Iran enriched uranium as constructive nuclear-track proposal.

Ceasefire Day 24. Kuwait MoD silence continues; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline; East-West pipeline backup routing for May-loading Asian lifts holding. Emir convened third National Security Bureau session this week on WPR 60-day deadline implications and Iranian 'practical action' rhetoric; air-defense readiness raised one notch but no public statement. Source: KUNA, KPC commercial.

Ceasefire Day 25. Kuwait MoD silence continues; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline; East-West pipeline backup routing for May-loading Asian lifts holding. Emir convened fourth National Security Bureau session this week on WPR 60-day deadline +1 implications and Iranian 'practical action' rhetoric escalation; air-defense readiness held one notch above baseline but no public statement. Source: KUNA, KPC commercial.

Ceasefire Day 26. Kuwait MoD silence continues; cadence reliability 0.65. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline; East-West pipeline backup routing for May-loading Asian lifts holding. Emir convened weekend National Security Bureau session on WPR 60-day deadline +2 implications and Iranian 'practical action' rhetoric escalation; air-defense readiness held one notch above baseline but no public statement. Source: KUNA, KPC commercial.

Ceasefire Day 27 (DE FACTO BROKEN). Kuwait MoD breaks 50-day silence with terse statement confirming single UAV intercept ~40km offshore east of Mina al-Ahmadi by Patriot battery; cadence reliability nudges to 0.66. KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline; East-West pipeline backup routing for May-loading Asian lifts holding. Ceasefire Day 28 (DE FACTO BROKEN). KNPC Mina al-Ahmadi export throughput near pre-war baseline; East-West pipeline backup routing for May-loading Asian lifts holding. Emir convened follow-on emergency National Security Bureau session; air defense readiness held at two notches above baseline. KUNA confirms second diplomatic protest delivered to Tehran via Swiss interests over UAV approaches in 48h. Source: KUNA, KPC commercial, MoD readout.

OMAN_DATA.note: Not targeted on Day 1 — only GCC state initially spared. No BM/CM at any point in conflict (drone-only targeting). Oman was hosting US-Iran nuclear talks until Feb 27.

Port of Duqm: drone struck mobile worker housing unit, 1 expatriate injured; 2nd drone intercepted (debris near fuel tanks). Separate: tanker Skylight attacked near Khasab — 4 crew injured, 20 evacuated. Source: Muscat Daily. Cumulative: 0 dead, 5 injured.

Duqm: fuel tank struck by drone, no casualties (Muscat Daily). Dhofar: 2 drones shot down; 3rd fell near Salalah Port — no losses (Times of Oman). No new casualties.
SALALAH PORT STRUCK — multiple fuel tanks damaged (no casualties). ONA via Times of Oman: several tanks damaged + port fire. AP satellite confirmed long-burning fire. Port ops suspended.
2 Indian expatriates killed in Al Awahi Industrial Area, Sohar. 2nd drone crashed nearby open area. Cumulative deaths: 1 maritime + 2 Indian workers = 3.

Ceasefire Day 2. No new inbound. Sohar and Mina Al Fahal fully operational. Salalah port restart assessment ongoing. Oman FM in consultations with Iran on Hormuz safe passage protocols.
Ceasefire Day 3. No inbound. Oman FM continuing back-channel consultations with Iran on Hormuz safe passage protocol. Sohar and Mina Al Fahal operational. Salalah port assessment ongoing.

Ceasefire Day 4. No inbound. Oman FM back-channel consultations continue — presenting Hormuz safe passage protocol to Iranian side at Islamabad. Sohar and Mina Al Fahal operational. Salalah port assessment complete.

Ceasefire Day 5. No inbound. Oman FM continuing back-channel role on Hormuz safe passage protocol. Iran drafting law to impose transit fees up to $2M per vessel. Northern Corridor under IRGC control for limited civilian transits.
Ceasefire Day 6. No inbound. Oman FM continuing back-channel role. Muscat warns US blockade risks escalation; offers to mediate Hormuz transit framework.

Ceasefire Day 7. No new strikes. Oman continues back-channel mediation role. Port of Sohar and Duqm see increased tanker diversions from Hormuz bottleneck. Oman FM Badr al-Busaidi consults with Iran's Araghchi.

Ceasefire Day 8. Oman continues back-channel mediation alongside Pakistan. Muscat facilitating logistics for potential second Islamabad round. Omani ports handling increased diverted cargo from Hormuz-blocked routes.
Ceasefire Day 9. Oman continues back-channel mediation. Muscat coordinating with Islamabad on ceasefire extension framework. Port of Sohar operating at reduced capacity.
ONA reports no aerial threats engaged.

Ceasefire Day 11 / Blockade Day 6. Muscat back-channel mediation continues; Omani FM coordinating with Pakistani chief Shehbaz Sharif on talks scheduling. Port of Sohar throughput +15% WoW; Salalah transshipment volumes trending to monthly record on Hormuz-diverted cargo.

Ceasefire Day 12. Muscat continues hosting technical-level US-Iran back-channel. Sohar and Duqm ports operating normally; Musandam traffic at ~18% pre-war on blockade enforcement. Sultan Haitham reiterates neutrality; no Omani aircraft commitments to coalition CAP.

Ceasefire Day 13. Muscat hosts third day of US-Iran technical back-channel; Omani MFA readout describes 'incremental convergence on transit-protocol verification mechanics.' Sohar and Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic at ~20% pre-war on continued blockade enforcement. Sultan Haitham meets Qatari PM later today on coordination framework.

Ceasefire Day 14. Muscat hosts fourth day of US-Iran technical back-channel; Omani MFA readout cites 'continued narrowing on verification annex.' Sohar and Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic ~22% pre-war. Sultan Haitham coordinates with Pakistani PM Sharif ahead of Islamabad Round 2.

Ceasefire Day 15. Muscat hosts fifth day of US-Iran technical back-channel; no new MFA readout at run cutoff. Sohar and Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic remains ~22% of pre-war. Continuation entry.

Ceasefire Day 16. Muscat hosts sixth day of US-Iran technical back-channel; no MFA readout at cutoff. Sohar and Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic ~22% of pre-war. Sultan Haitham continues coordination with Pakistan PM ahead of Islamabad Round 2. Continuation entry.

Ceasefire Day 17. Muscat hosts seventh day of US-Iran technical back-channel; no MFA readout at cutoff. Reuters via regional stringers reports Iranian delegation remaining in-country despite IRGC 23-Apr statement. Sohar and Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic ~24% of pre-war (slight uptick vs Apr 23). Sultan Haitham continues Pakistan PM coordination ahead of Islamabad Round 2.

Ceasefire Day 18. Muscat back-channel paused after Iranian FM Araghchi departed Islamabad; Sultan Haitham held call with Pakistani PM Sharif. Sohar/Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic ~22% pre-war (slight contraction). MFA confirms ongoing mediation availability.

Ceasefire Day 19. Muscat received Iranian deputy FM for in-person consultations following Round 2 talks collapse. Reuters via regional stringers reports Omani envoy traveling to Doha for joint mediation alignment. Musandam Strait traffic ~21% pre-war. Sultan Haitham reportedly to Pakistan PM in coming days.

Ceasefire Day 20. Muscat continues hosting Iranian deputy FM consultations following Round 2 talks collapse and Araghchi onward travel to Moscow. Omani envoy in Doha for joint mediation alignment with Qatar. Sultan Haitham reportedly to Pakistan PM Sharif this week. Sohar/Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic ~21% pre-war (CENTCOM-observed). MFA confirms ongoing mediation availability with both Tehran and Washington.

Ceasefire Day 21. Muscat continues hosting Iranian deputy FM consultations; Araghchi expected to transit Doha en route Tehran from St. Petersburg with Hormuz red-lines list to convey via Pakistani channel. Sultan Haitham reportedly to Pakistan PM Sharif this week per FM readout. Sohar/Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic ~19 vessels/24h (~22% pre-war, CENTCOM-observed). MFA confirms continued mediation availability with both Tehran and Washington; Oman noted Russian FM Lavrov's diplomatic-only resolution stance constructive.

Ceasefire Day 22. Muscat continues hosting Iranian deputy FM consultations; Araghchi confirmed transit via Doha en route Tehran from Moscow with Hormuz red-lines list to convey via Pakistani channel. Sultan Haitham scheduled Pakistan visit with PM Sharif this week per FM readout. Sohar/Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic 19-21 vessels/24h (~23% pre-war, CENTCOM-observed) — Strait remains effectively closed to most Gulf oil exports per industry tracking. MFA confirms continued mediation availability with both Tehran and Washington; Oman MFA quietly probing Rubio team on tolerance for any Iranian-administered transit notification arrangement post-blockade lift.

Ceasefire Day 23. Muscat continues hosting Iranian deputy FM consultations; Sultan Haitham confirmed Pakistan visit with PM Sharif this weekend per FM readout. Sohar/Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic 19-22 vessels/24h (~24% pre-war, CENTCOM-observed). Strait remains effectively closed to most Gulf oil exports per industry tracking — IEA estimates ~20 mbpd of crude/fuels/petrochemicals affected. Oman MFA quietly probing Rubio team and Witkoff staff on tolerance for any Iranian-administered transit notification arrangement post-blockade lift; Trump Apr 29 'blockade until nuclear deal' rhetoric narrows mediation aperture. Oman publicly endorsed Iraqi pipeline tender to Syria/Jordan as Hormuz alternative.

Ceasefire Day 24. Sultan Haitham concluded Islamabad consultations with PM Sharif; Muscat continues hosting Iranian deputy FM; Oman FM readout indicates Iran willing to accept staged Hormuz reopen if US lifts port siege concurrently (still rejected by Trump per Apr 30 NBC). Sohar/Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic 19-23 vessels/24h (~25% pre-war, CENTCOM AIS). Source: ONA, Oman FM, CENTCOM.

Ceasefire Day 25. Sultan Haitham continues Islamabad consultations with PM Sharif into Day 2; Muscat continues hosting Iranian deputy FM. Oman FM readout (May 2 morning) signals Iran 'reviewing' US conditions on staged Hormuz reopen but talks remain at deputy level. Sohar/Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic 18-22 vessels/24h (~24% pre-war, CENTCOM AIS) — slight reduction vs Day 63 attributed to underwriter-driven self-reroute. Source: ONA, Oman FM, CENTCOM.

Ceasefire Day 26. Sultan Haitham continues Islamabad consultations with PM Sharif into Day 3; Muscat continues hosting Iranian deputy FM with Pakistani mediators relaying revised Iran 14-point counterproposal text to State Department through diplomatic backchannel. Oman FM readout (May 3 morning) signals 'narrow but live' diplomatic window; Iran 30-day timeline preference vs US 2-month framing identified as principal obstacle. Sohar/Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic 17-22 vessels/24h (~22% pre-war, CENTCOM AIS) — slight further reduction vs Day 64 attributed to underwriter-driven self-reroute on >3% war-risk premium threshold. Source: ONA, Oman FM, CENTCOM.

Ceasefire Day 27 (DE FACTO BROKEN). Sultan Haitham terminates Islamabad consultations early and returns to Muscat after Trump 'unacceptable' rejection of Iran 14-point plan and Jask warship strike; Iran deputy FM presence in Muscat extended for emergency contact. Oman FM 0900 readout: 'Diplomatic window narrowed sharply but not closed; we will continue to host parties.' Iran 30-day vs US 2-month timeline divergence subsumed by escalation dynamics. Sohar/Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic plunges to 11-15 vessels/24h (~13% pre-war, CENTCOM AIS) on underwriter pull-back and operator self-reroute following Jask attack. Royal Oman Police elevates port and pipeline security one notch. Source: ONA, Oman FM, CENTCOM, Reuters shipping.

Ceasefire Day 28 (DE FACTO BROKEN). Sultan Haitham held emergency Cabinet session in Muscat after returning from Islamabad; Iran deputy FM remains in Muscat for emergency contact, Pakistani mediators continuing relay function. Oman FM 0830 readout: 'We continue to host parties; the door is not closed but the room has narrowed considerably.' Iran 30-day vs US 2-month timeline divergence subsumed by escalation dynamics; new sticking point is Iran demand that US suspend Project Freedom escort sorties as precondition to talks resumption — US publicly rejects via State Department spokesperson. Sohar/Duqm nominal; Musandam Strait traffic plunges further to 8-12 vessels/24h (~9% pre-war, CENTCOM AIS) on continued underwriter pull-back and operator self-reroute following overnight US-Iran small-boat exchange near Hormuz. Royal Oman Police maintains elevated port and pipeline security. Source: ONA, Oman FM, CENTCOM, Reuters shipping.

Ceasefire Day 29 (DE FACTO BROKEN). 2 foreign nationals wounded by debris from a near-miss UAV/missile event in Salalah outskirts overnight (cf. Day 67 separate Oman strike) — Oman MoFA labels 'most likely off-axis Iranian munition' but stops short of formal attribution; investigation underway. Sultan Haitham held second emergency Cabinet session in Muscat; Iranian Deputy FM Bagheri Kani remains in Muscat as relay node, Pakistani mediators rotated. Oman FM 0830 readout: 'The pause announced by President Trump opens a 72-hour political window. We are working to expand it. Iran 30-day clock is on Day 4.' Iran-US back channel via Pakistan/Oman intensifies on draft agreement structure (freedom of navigation, nuclear program, frozen assets, sanctions architecture, regional reconstruction). Sultan to brief Pakistani PM Sharif and Saudi MBS in coming 24h.

Ceasefire Day 9. No new proxy strikes. Iraq production recovery stalled. Baghdad coordinating with CENTCOM on force protection posture as blockade continues.

Ceasefire Day 12. No new proxy strikes reported at Ain al-Asad or Erbil. Production holds near ~1.65 mbpd. PM al-Sudani meets CENTCOM J5 on force-protection extension matrix through blockade period. Iraq-Jordan pipeline feasibility enters commercial-terms phase.

Ceasefire Day 13. No proxy strikes at Ain al-Asad or Erbil. Oil production near ~1.67 mbpd (+0.02 vs Day 51). PM al-Sudani meeting with US Ambassador on force-protection extension through blockade period; Iraq-Jordan pipeline feasibility study moves to commercial-terms stage.

Ceasefire Day 14. No proxy strikes at Ain al-Asad or Erbil reported overnight. Oil production steady near ~1.67 mbpd. PM al-Sudani concludes US Ambassador consultation on force-protection extension; Iraq-Jordan pipeline commercial-terms negotiation continues with consortium advisors.
CEASEFIRE — No Iranian strikes reported. [Corrected: prior AI-generated interception data removed.]

Ceasefire Day 16. No proxy-strike indications at Ain al-Asad or Erbil at cutoff; oil production steady ~1.67 mbpd. PM al-Sudani force-protection consultations continue; Iraq-Jordan pipeline commercial-terms negotiation ongoing. Cumulative casualties held (115 killed, 363 injured). Continuation entry pending MoD daily readout.

Ceasefire Day 17. No proxy-strike indications at Ain al-Asad or Erbil at cutoff; oil production steady ~1.67 mbpd. PM al-Sudani hosted CENTCOM deputy on force-protection consultations. Iraq-Jordan pipeline commercial-terms negotiation ongoing; expected announcement next week. Cumulative casualties held (115 killed, 363 injured).

Ceasefire Day 18. No proxy-strike indications at Ain al-Asad or Erbil. Oil production steady ~1.67 mbpd. PM al-Sudani publicly urged restraint following Round 2 collapse. Iraq-Jordan pipeline commercial-terms negotiation continues.

Ceasefire Day 19. Iraqi Kataib Hezbollah-linked Telegram channel issued warning of 'consequences' if US tightens Hormuz blockade — no kinetic activity yet. Ain al-Asad and Erbil force-protection raised to Bravo+. Oil production stable. Cumulative casualties held.

Ceasefire Day 20. No proxy-strike indications at Ain al-Asad or Erbil at cutoff; force-protection remains at Bravo+ following Kataib Hezbollah-linked Telegram warning Apr 26. Oil production stable ~1.67 mbpd. PM al-Sudani publicly reiterated restraint appeal and joint mediation support after Round 2 collapse. Iraq-Jordan pipeline commercial-terms negotiation expected announcement this week. Cumulative casualties held (115 killed, 363 injured).

Ceasefire Day 21. No proxy-strike indications at Ain al-Asad or Erbil at cutoff; force-protection remains at Bravo+ following Kataib Hezbollah-linked Telegram warning Apr 26 (now 48h past with no kinetic activity). Oil production stable ~1.67 mbpd. PM al-Sudani publicly reiterated restraint appeal after Araghchi-Putin St. Petersburg meeting. Iraq-Jordan pipeline commercial-terms negotiation announcement now expected Apr 30. Cumulative casualties held (115 killed, 363 injured).

Ceasefire Day 22. No proxy-strike indications at Ain al-Asad or Erbil at cutoff; force-protection remains at Bravo+ (now 72h past Apr 26 Kataib Hezbollah Telegram warning with no kinetic activity). Oil production stable ~1.67 mbpd. PM al-Sudani publicly reiterated restraint appeal after Trump Apr 28 'state of collapse' rhetoric. Iraq-Jordan pipeline commercial-terms announcement still expected Apr 30. Cumulative casualties held (115 killed, 363 injured). Iraqi National Security Adviser briefed Cabinet on US OFAC Apr 27 SDN designations on Iran-linked LNG/condensate shipping network — no Iraqi entities listed.

Ceasefire Day 23. No proxy-strike indications at Ain al-Asad or Erbil at cutoff; force-protection remains at Bravo+ (now 96h past Apr 26 Kataib Hezbollah Telegram warning with no kinetic activity). Oil production stable ~1.67 mbpd. PM al-Sudani publicly reiterated restraint appeal after Trump Apr 29 blockade extension. Iraqi Cabinet today formally approved tender for multibillion-dollar pipeline project from southern oilfields to Syria/possibly Jordan as Hormuz alternative — global bid solicitation issuing this week (per AGBI/IEA reporting). SOMO commercial-terms framework with Jordan announced, capacity ramp targets pending. Cumulative casualties held (115 killed, 363 injured). National Security Adviser briefed Cabinet on intel that 7 IRI commanders SDGT-designated Apr 17 (Kataib Hezbollah, Asaib Ahl al Haqq, KSS, HHN) operating reduced posture.

Ceasefire Day 24 / WPR 60-day deadline. Force-protection at Ain al-Asad and Erbil remains Bravo+; CENTCOM Task Force Iraq quiet but alerted post-IRGC 'practical action' Apr 30 statement. Kataib Hezbollah Telegram channel publishes ambiguous 'we are watching the deadline' graphic; no kinetic activity. PMF leadership convenes in Baghdad. Iraqi PM Sudani reiterates ceasefire support and warns against Iraqi territory becoming 'a second front'. Oil production stable. Source: INA, MOI, CENTCOM PAO.

Ceasefire Day 25 / WPR 60-day deadline +1. Force-protection at Ain al-Asad and Erbil held at Bravo+; CENTCOM Task Force Iraq quiet but alerted post-IRGC 'practical action' May 1 statement. Kataib Hezbollah Telegram channel publishes second 'we are watching the deadline' graphic; no kinetic activity. PMF leadership convenes second meeting in 48h in Baghdad. Iraqi PM Sudani in Riyadh for consultations with MBS; reiterates ceasefire support and warns against Iraqi territory becoming 'a second front'. Oil production stable, exports at ~93% pre-war. Source: INA, MOI, CENTCOM PAO.

Ceasefire Day 26 / WPR 60-day deadline +2. Force-protection at Ain al-Asad and Erbil held at Bravo+; CENTCOM Task Force Iraq quiet but alerted post-IRGC 'practical action' communique. Kataib Hezbollah Telegram channel publishes third 'we are watching' graphic in 72h; no kinetic activity. PMF leadership convenes Baghdad coordination session. Iraqi PM Sudani returns from Riyadh consultations with MBS; reiterates ceasefire support, warns again against Iraqi territory becoming 'a second front', and announces deconfliction-channel update with US Embassy Baghdad. Oil production stable; Basra/Khor al-Amaya exports at ~93% pre-war. Source: INA, MOI, CENTCOM PAO.

Ceasefire Day 27 (DE FACTO BROKEN). Force-protection at Ain al-Asad and Erbil ELEVATED to CHARLIE on Project Freedom kickoff and Jask warship strike. CENTCOM Task Force Iraq personnel restricted to base; non-essential convoys paused. Kataib Hezbollah issues mobilization order calling on all fighters to heighten readiness for 'war of attrition with the US' and warns Erbil/KRG against 'colluding with foreign forces.' No kinetic activity yet but US embassy intel collection elevates threat watch. PMF leadership in continuous session; PM Sudani convenes National Security Council in Baghdad and warns publicly against Iraqi territory becoming 'a second front.' Oil production stable, exports ~93% pre-war. Source: INA, MOI, CENTCOM PAO, FDD, MEO.

Ceasefire Day 28 (DE FACTO BROKEN). Force-protection at Ain al-Asad and Erbil sustained at CHARLIE; CENTCOM Task Force Iraq personnel restriction continues, non-essential convoys remain paused. Kataib Hezbollah issues second mobilization graphic in 24h plus operational directive routing fighters to staging areas in Diyala and Anbar; no kinetic activity yet but US embassy Baghdad reissues Worldwide Caution renewal for Iraq. PM Sudani convenes second National Security Council session in 24h, again warns publicly against Iraqi territory becoming 'a second front,' and announces Iraqi armed forces will 'use all means' to prevent unauthorized armed groups from striking foreign assets from Iraqi soil. PMF leadership in continuous session. Oil production stable; Basra/Khor al-Amaya exports ~93% pre-war. Source: INA, MOI, CENTCOM PAO, FDD, MEO, US Embassy Baghdad.

Ceasefire Day 29 (DE FACTO BROKEN). Force-protection at Ain al-Asad and Erbil eased one notch from CHARLIE to BRAVO+ on Project Freedom pause; CENTCOM Task Force Iraq personnel restriction relaxed for essential movement, non-essential convoys still paused pending 24h reassessment. Kataib Hezbollah and Asaib Ahl al-Haq issue joint communique calling for 'continued readiness' but stand down active-mobilization graphics from Day 67. PMF chief Falih al-Fayyadh held closed-door session with PM Sudani; readout emphasizes 'no operational launches against US forces sanctioned during current diplomatic phase.' Iraq Foreign Minister al-Hussein supports Pakistan/Oman mediation and confirms Saudi-Iran back channel via Baghdad continues.

Trump extends deadline to Tue 8pm ET. Iran rejects 45-day ceasefire. Hormuz 98% blocked.
Ceasefire announced: WTI crashes ~9% in 30min. Iran agrees to reopen Hormuz with coordination. Brent drops below $100 for first time since Mar 10.

Brent plunges ~12% on ceasefire news but recovers slightly as Iran re-closes Hormuz over Lebanon strikes. WTI down 16% from pre-ceasefire highs. Markets volatile on uncertainty whether Hormuz will reopen. EIA estimates 9.1M bpd supply disruption in April.

Oil rebounds 3% in early trade on Hormuz non-compliance fears. Brent trading $96-97 range. ADNOC CEO confirms Hormuz 'not open.' EIA raises 2026 oil price forecast citing continued Hormuz disruptions. Goldman warns $100+ Brent if Hormuz closure extends another month. Only 2 tankers transited Hormuz since ceasefire.

Brent steady ~$96.69 as Islamabad talks open. WTI eases to $93.20. Markets cautiously optimistic on diplomatic track but Hormuz closure caps downside. Goldman maintains $100+ Brent forecast if Hormuz stays shut another month. Dated Brent spot remains above $120 reflecting physical market stress. ~230 loaded tankers stranded in Gulf.

Weekend — Brent closed at $72.45/bbl (Apr 11 close). YTD down ~8% from Jan highs near $80. JPMorgan warns $120/bbl if Hormuz not restored by July. IEA estimates war cut crude supply ~11M bpd through end of March. Global deficit ~750K bpd average for 2026, peaking 3M bpd in Q2.

Brent surges 8% to $103/bbl after Trump announces naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz following collapse of Islamabad peace talks. Goldman warns another month of Hormuz closure means >$100 Brent throughout 2026. WTI tracks higher at $99.50. Futures markets pricing in sustained supply disruption.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums surged to 1-5% of hull value. Iran is enforcing a cap of roughly 15 vessels per day and charging $1M+ tolls for safe passage.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: War Risk Premium
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War risk premiums remain at 5-10% of hull value despite April 8 ceasefire. Strait effectively closed to free transit.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Blockade War Risk Premium
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: US blockade overlays Iranian IRGC toll regime creating dual-chokepoint. Lloyd's expected to widen exclusion zones. Major underwriters (Gard, Skuld, NorthStandard) already cancelled Gulf war risk cover. Premiums surge toward 10-15% of hull value.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Blockade Impacts
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Reinsurers adopted no-market stance, making transit commercially unviable for most.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: US Blockade Surcharges
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Blockade drops Hormuz transit by 90%. War risk peaks at 10%.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Blockade Day 4 Risk Assessment
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War-risk premiums unchanged; insurers reassessing coverage every 48 hours. Blockade adds uncertainty but CENTCOM assurances on non-Iranian traffic steady premiums. Few ships transiting Hormuz overall.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Extreme War Risk Zone
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War-risk premiums stabilized slightly but remain 10-20 times higher than pre-war levels. Insurers assessing risk per-voyage.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Blockade Premium Surge
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War-risk premiums spiked to 1-5% of hull value. US DFC launched $40B reinsurance facility.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Gulf War Risk Premium
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums surge as underwriters assess new drone threats and port closures in the region.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: DFC reinsurance facility online
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War-risk premiums ease ~70bp as DFC $40B reinsurance facility begins underwriting first tranche. Lloyd's syndicates restoring quotes on third-country cargoes; Iranian-linked tonnage remains uninsurable in conventional market. Hormuz status downgraded from 'Closed' to 'Restricted' on JMIC convoy intake.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Lloyd's syndicate quotes broaden; Hormuz stays Restricted
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War-risk premiums ease a further ~30bp as DFC $40B reinsurance facility underwrites second tranche and Lloyd's restores quotes on third-country cargoes (Greek, Norwegian, Danish tonnage). Iranian-linked tonnage remains uninsurable in the conventional market. JMIC convoy enrollment crossed 220 vessels cumulative.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: War-risk premiums continue gradual decline; Hormuz stays Restricted
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.detail: Lloyd's syndicate quotes broaden further as DFC $40B reinsurance facility second tranche absorbs residual exposure. Third-country cargo quotes (Greek, Norwegian, Danish flags) firm; Iranian-origin cargoes remain excluded.
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War-risk premium eases ~20bp day-over-day (5.5% → 5.3%). Hormuz designation unchanged at Restricted. Continuation entry pending next Lloyd's market circular.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Day 55
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums hold at record highs as blockade continues.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: First war-risk tick-down since Mar 8; underwriters cite Islamabad Round 2 expectation but keep JWC listed areas unchanged. VLCC day-rates soft on lower utilization.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Day 57
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premium held at Day 56 level; underwriters paused further easing pending Round 2 outcome. VLCC day-rates marginally softer.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Day 58
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: First war-risk uptick since Mar 8 reversal; underwriters cite Round 2 collapse and UKMTO Aframax incident. JWC-listed area unchanged.

Hormuz war-risk premium quoted 1.85-2.10% of hull value (held since Apr 24); Lloyd's JWC retains Persian Gulf, Hormuz, Gulf of Oman, Red Sea, and Bab al-Mandeb on Listed Areas (HRA). LR2/VLCC daily TCE rates Persian Gulf-Asia near $112k (vs. $48k pre-war). P&I clubs continue case-by-case approvals for Iranian-port-bound vessels under US blockade; most lines refusing. Reinsurance treaty renewals being repriced for Q3.

Hormuz war-risk premium quoted 1.95-2.40% of hull value (widened from Apr 28 1.85-2.10% range on Trump 'state of collapse' rhetoric); some carriers quoting up to 5% on individual hulls. Lloyd's JWC retains Persian Gulf, Hormuz, Gulf of Oman, Red Sea, Bab al-Mandeb on Listed Areas. US $20B reinsurance backstop program operational; Treasury reaffirmed commitment Apr 28. Major P&I clubs (Gard, Skuld, NorthStandard, London P&I, American Club) maintain war-risk cover cancellation effective since Mar 5. LR2/VLCC TCE Persian Gulf-Asia ~$118k/day (vs. $48k pre-war). Reinsurance treaty Q3 renewals being repriced sharply higher.

Hormuz war-risk premium quoted 2.20-2.75% of hull value (widened from Apr 29 1.95-2.40% range on Trump Apr 29 blockade-extension statement); some carriers now quoting up to 6% on individual hulls. Lloyd's JWC retains Persian Gulf, Hormuz, Gulf of Oman, Red Sea, Bab al-Mandeb on Listed Areas. US $20B reinsurance backstop program operational; Treasury reaffirmed commitment Apr 29. Major P&I clubs (Gard, Skuld, NorthStandard, London P&I, American Club) maintain war-risk cover cancellation effective since Mar 5. LR2/VLCC TCE Persian Gulf-Asia ~$132k/day (vs. $48k pre-war, +$14k D/D). Reinsurance treaty Q3 renewals being repriced sharply higher; some accounts seeing 3x renewal quotes.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.detail: Hormuz war-risk premium widens further on WPR 60-day deadline coverage and IRGC 'practical action' rhetoric.
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Hormuz war-risk premium quoted 2.40-3.00% of hull value (widened from Apr 30 2.20-2.75% range on WPR 60-day deadline coverage and IRGC 'practical action' rhetoric); some carriers now quoting up to 7% on individual hulls. Lloyd's JWC retains Persian Gulf, Hormuz, Gulf of Oman, Red Sea, Bab al-Mandeb on Listed Areas. US $20B reinsurance backstop program operational; Treasury OFAC issued FAQ clarifying tanker-by-tanker waiver process for Iran-port redirected lifts. Premium for seven-day cover ~10x pre-war level confirmed by Lloyd's List. Source: Lloyd's JWC, LMA, Reuters Shipping.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.detail: Hormuz war-risk premium pierces 3% threshold on WPR-deadline +1 and Israeli strike-resumption reporting.
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Hormuz war-risk premium quoted 2.55-3.10% of hull value (widened from May 1 2.40-3.00% range); some carriers now quoting up to 7.5% on individual hulls. Lloyd's JWC retains Persian Gulf, Hormuz, Gulf of Oman, Red Sea, Bab al-Mandeb on Listed Areas. US $20B reinsurance backstop program operational; Treasury OFAC tanker-by-tanker waiver process running ~14 vessels/week. Premium for seven-day cover ~11x pre-war level confirmed by Lloyd's List. Source: Lloyd's JWC, LMA, Reuters Shipping.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.detail: Hormuz war-risk premium eases ~3% on diplomatic optics; range now 2.45-3.00% of hull value vs 2.55-3.10% Day 64.
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Hormuz war-risk premium quoted 2.45-3.00% of hull value (eased modestly from May 2 peak); some carriers retain quotes up to 7.5% on individual Iranian-linked hulls. Lloyd's JWC retains Persian Gulf, Hormuz, Gulf of Oman, Red Sea, Bab al-Mandeb on Listed Areas. US $20B reinsurance backstop program operational; Treasury OFAC tanker-by-tanker waiver process running ~14 vessels/week. Premium for seven-day cover ~10x pre-war level (down from 11x). UNCTAD note flags first signs of underwriter capacity-rationing on Cape-route LNG carriers as supply-side bottleneck. Source: Lloyd's JWC, LMA, Reuters Shipping, UNCTAD.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.detail: Hormuz war-risk premium spikes ~21% on Jask warship strike; range now 3.10-3.85% of hull value vs 2.45-3.00% Day 65.
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Hormuz war-risk premium quoted 3.10-3.85% of hull value; some carriers withdrawing fresh quotes pending CENTCOM threat reassessment. Iranian-linked hulls quoted 8.5%+ where offered. Lloyd's JWC retains Persian Gulf, Hormuz, Gulf of Oman, Red Sea, Bab al-Mandeb on Listed Areas. US $20B reinsurance backstop activates for Project Freedom escorts. Treasury OFAC tanker-by-tanker waiver process slows to ~9 vessels/week (was 14). Premium for seven-day cover ~12.5x pre-war level. UNCTAD note flags second wave of underwriter capacity rationing on Cape-route LNG carriers. Source: Lloyd's JWC, LMA, Reuters Shipping, UNCTAD.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.detail: Hormuz war-risk premium climbs to 3.40-4.10% of hull value (from 3.10-3.85% Day 66) on Fujairah tank-farm strike and second day of Project Freedom kinetic exchange; multiple London market underwriters now declining new Persian Gulf transit cover entirely.
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Lloyd's JWC re-classifies UAE territorial waters and Fujairah anchorage as 'enhanced risk'; H&M (hull & machinery) facultative reinsurance terms tightening sharply. Steamship Mutual and West of England both issue Hormuz transit advisories.
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.source: Lloyd's List, Lloyd's JWC, Reuters insurance

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Lloyd's syndicates trim Hormuz war-risk premium from 8.5% to 7.8% of hull value on Project Freedom pause; remains highest since Mar 2026 escalation. JWC keeps Hormuz on listed war-risk list pending signed agreement.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Phase 3 Escalation
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums reach new high following MT Gulf Splendor attack.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Ongoing Hostilities
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Underwriters refusing coverage for older hulls without explicit naval escorts.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Mid May Premium Spike
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums surge following the resumption of active hostilities and increased naval presence.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Late May Market
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Insurers demand up to 4% for Persian Gulf entry; standard quotes stabilizing at this elevated rate.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: End of May Stabilization
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Market stabilizes at high premium levels as coalition forces establish structured convoy transits.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: War Risk Update
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War risk premiums increase following renewed hostilities

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: War Risk Premiums
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums reached 3%-5% of vessel value. Major carriers rerouting around Cape of Good Hope.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Hormuz Blockade Ongoing
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums remain historically high due to continued threat of ASBMs and regional instability

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: War Peak
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Additional War Risk Premiums skyrocketed to 3.0% of hull value; 7-day transit costs $5M-$7.5M.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: War Day 94
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums hold steady at record highs amid ongoing Phase 3 strikes.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Strait Closed / Ghost Fleet Active
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Over 150 vessels loitering outside Strait; global capacity reduced 20% due to Cape rerouting.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Day 95
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums surge after new USV attacks in the Gulf of Oman.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: War-Risk Premiums
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War-risk premiums have eased slightly to around 1% of hull value, but underwriters emphasize pricing cannot normalize without sustained stability.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: War Risk Premium Update
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War risk premiums remain at peak levels for Hormuz transits following localized hostilities.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: War Risk Update
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums peak following renewed tanker drone strikes in the Gulf of Oman

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Day 99
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Hormuz transit completely blocked; war risk premiums surge to 7.5% for vessels anchoring in the outer Gulf of Oman waiting for clearance.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Red Sea Diverts
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Underwriters demanding prior notification and route approvals for all Gulf transits.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Day 101
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War risk premiums remain elevated at 4.5% amidst renewed active conflict zone status.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: War Risk Premium
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: Premiums surge to 4.5% of hull value for any vessels approaching the Gulf of Oman; unescorted transit uninsurable.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Day 103 Assessment
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War risk premiums plateauing at ~4.5% for Western-linked vessels.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Strait Closed
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: IRGC Navy declared the Strait closed again. Daily transits in single digits.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.label: Strait of Hormuz
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.note: War-risk premiums reached 4% of total vessel value for a seven-day policy.

BUNKER_PRICES.note: Pre-war baseline
First confirmed post-war data
Single-day spike: +$233 VLSFO
Trump de-escalation pullback
ADNOC crude loading formally suspended
VLSFO doubled from pre-war $508
Global bunker prices surged 30–35%. Analysts warn oil could reach $200/bbl if Strait contested, adding €340M/day to fuel bill.
Fujairah bunker operations severely limited.
Sustained high prices due to supply chain disruptions.
Singapore VLSFO retreating to $850-$867.50 following peace plan signals.
Singapore prices; Rotterdam VLSFO hovering around $850-$900/MT
MGO LS Index reached record $1609.79/MT; Singapore VLSFO near $838/mt.
VLSFO price sits around $936 per metric tonne, significantly up from pre-war levels of ~$540.
Bunker prices surged over $930-$1,050 per metric tonne amid Middle East supply chain shock.
Singapore prices spike as Middle East supply drops further.
Singapore VLSFO hovering at $838-$930/mt. Fujairah near-standstill with major suppliers declaring force majeure. Shipping companies face an additional $370M/day in bunker costs.
VLSFO prices near $936/mt; stabilization noted post-ceasefire but costs remain high.
Sharp correction following Apr 8 ceasefire reports. MGO drops $42. Market entering corrective phase.
Prices surge amid near-standstill in regional bunkering and effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
Bunker prices remain elevated outside the Gulf due to 320+ vessels at anchor waiting for transit.
Bunker prices spike further on blockade news. VLSFO approaches $900/mt. Vessel diversions around Cape of Good Hope adding 10-15 days transit time and ~$1M additional fuel costs per VLCC voyage.
Bunker prices surged to near-record levels following the enforcement of the US blockade.
VLSFO up 40% from Feb. MGO hits $1,610/MT. Carriers applying EBS of $60-$190/TEU.
VLSFO edging up amid tight supply. MGO at $1,615/MT. Carriers maintaining EBS surcharges of $60-$190/TEU. Cape of Good Hope rerouting continues.
VLSFO prices stabilize at $722/tonne in Singapore; Durban experiencing extreme pricing at ~$1,480/tonne.
Emergency Bunker Surcharges (EBS) and Transit Disruption Surcharges of $800-$3,000 per container implemented by major lines.
Severe shortages reported in Fujairah; vessels heavily bunkering in Singapore prior to transit.

Fujairah bunker availability still tight; Singapore lifting volumes climb to absorb reroute demand. Gulf prompt VLSFO basis softens marginally (-$5/t) on reduced near-term escort wait time at Fujairah anchorage.

Fujairah bunker availability improving as escort wait times fall to ~11 hours (-4h vs Apr 20). Singapore lifting volumes continue absorbing reroute demand. Gulf prompt VLSFO softens another -$5/t as queueing normalizes.

Fujairah bunker availability continues normalizing as escort wait times fall toward ~9 hours. Singapore lifting volumes continue absorbing reroute demand. Gulf prompt VLSFO softens another -$5/t vs Apr 21 ($830 → $825). Continuation entry.
Regional shortages persist in Fujairah.
Modest easing across grades on Brent pullback; Fujairah shortages persist — queues at Singapore extended.
Modest firming on Brent rebound; Singapore queues remain extended; Fujairah shortages persist.
Continued firming across grades on widened risk premium; Fujairah HSFO availability tightest in two weeks.

Singapore VLSFO $645/MT; IFO380 $695/MT; MGO $920/MT. Fujairah VLSFO at +$32/MT premium to Singapore; Rotterdam VLSFO $612/MT. Premia widened on Brent climbing through $108 and continued Hormuz disruption. Bunker barge availability constrained at Fujairah anchorage; queue 18-22 hours.

Singapore VLSFO $662/MT; IFO380 $712/MT; MGO $942/MT. Fujairah VLSFO at +$36/MT premium to Singapore; Rotterdam VLSFO $625/MT. Premia widened on Brent settling $111.26 and continued Hormuz disruption. Bunker barge availability constrained at Fujairah anchorage; queue 20-26 hours. Singapore traders citing reroute-driven demand for low-sulfur bunkers Cape route.

Singapore VLSFO $698/MT (+$36 D/D); IFO380 $748/MT; MGO $986/MT. Fujairah VLSFO at +$42/MT premium to Singapore (Hormuz risk re-priced after Trump Apr 29 blockade extension); Rotterdam VLSFO $652/MT. Premia widened sharply on Brent settling $118.03. Bunker barge availability constrained at Fujairah anchorage; queue 22-30 hours. Singapore traders citing reroute-driven demand for low-sulfur bunkers Cape route at multi-year highs. Some Asian buyers shifting prompt LSFO procurement to Singapore and South Korea given Fujairah uncertainty.

Singapore VLSFO $694/MT; IFO380 $744/MT; MGO $976/MT. Fujairah VLSFO at +$41/MT premium to Singapore; Rotterdam VLSFO $642/MT. Premia continue widening on Brent settling near $120 and WPR 60-day deadline coverage. Fujairah barge queue 24-30 hours; Singapore traders citing reroute-driven low-sulfur demand and Cape route bunker deliveries. Source: Bunkerworld, Ship & Bunker.

Singapore VLSFO $702/MT; IFO380 $753/MT; MGO $988/MT. Fujairah VLSFO at +$43/MT premium to Singapore; Rotterdam VLSFO $649/MT. Premia continue widening on Brent settling >$122 and WPR-deadline +1 coverage. Fujairah barge queue 26-32 hours; Singapore traders citing reroute-driven low-sulfur demand and Cape route bunker deliveries. Source: Bunkerworld, Ship & Bunker.

Singapore VLSFO $695/MT; IFO380 $746/MT; MGO $978/MT — eases modestly from May 2 levels on Brent retracing to ~$110. Fujairah VLSFO at +$45/MT premium to Singapore (premium widens on continued reroute demand); Rotterdam VLSFO $642/MT. Fujairah barge queue 24-30 hours; Singapore traders citing Cape-route low-sulfur demand and product arbitrage from ADNOC Ruwais running at 102% nameplate. Source: Bunkerworld, Ship & Bunker.

Singapore VLSFO $728/MT (+$33 d/d); IFO380 $781/MT; MGO $1018/MT — all higher on Brent rally to ~$118 and Project Freedom-related distillate demand spike. Fujairah VLSFO at +$58/MT premium to Singapore (premium widens further on continued Cape-route reroute demand and product arbitrage from ADNOC Ruwais running at 102% nameplate); Rotterdam VLSFO $670/MT. Fujairah barge queue 30-36 hours. Source: Bunkerworld, Ship & Bunker.

Singapore VLSFO $742/MT (+$14 d/d); IFO380 $794/MT; MGO $1038/MT on Brent rally past $119 and Project Freedom-related distillate demand. Fujairah VLSFO bid widens vs Singapore on bunker barge availability concerns post tank-farm fire.
VLSFO -2.3% vs Day 67 on Project Freedom pause; ANCHOR-only call protocol relaxed to ESCORTED-IN/OUT post-Tank Farm 7 fire containment.
Fujairah bunker constraints due to stringent security protocols.
Singapore prices surge as vessels continually reroute around Cape of Good Hope.
Fujairah bunker constraints drive VLSFO up as vessels seek alternative refueling hubs.
Slight easing in prices as Singapore supply begins to fill the Middle East bunker gap.
Prices react to new convoy system delays and ongoing regional fuel supply chain friction.
Fujairah bunker prices surge on supply fears
Conflict added $5.5B to global ocean carriers' bunker costs; Maersk reports extra $500M/month in fuel.
Fujairah stocks depleting, premiums rising for immediate delivery as vessels reroute
VLSFO in Singapore surged to $822/tonne; MGO approached $1000/tonne in major hubs.
Fujairah bunker constraints continue; Singapore prices spike slightly on supply chain fears.
VLSFO up ~100% since pre-war to near $1,000/tonne; Asia refinery processing rates down 10-15% due to feedstock shortage.
Fujairah bunker constraints push VLSFO higher amid war risks.
Industry leaders report physical bunker supply held steady despite 60-75% price climb since late Feb; global crude inventories drawn down significantly.
Fujairah bunker prices elevated; tight supply of MGO reported due to military requisitions.
Fujairah bunker operations severely limited; heavy congestion at alternative hubs
Fujairah bunker prices spike as regional availability tightening continues and resupply vessels refuse to enter high-risk zones.
Suez max capacity reached, boosting alternative African coastal routes.
Fujairah bunker availability tightening due to rerouting and regional supply chain stress.
Fujairah bunker constraints and supply chain squeeze push VLSFO near $1000/mt.
Severe congestion at Omani ports driving up MGO and VLSFO prices.
Fujairah VLSFO prices spike to $1211/tonne due to supply vacuum and Kuwait al-Zour refinery output collapse.
Fujairah VLSFO prices reach $1,211/tonne with spot premiums $500–$700 above Singapore.

1 shipyard worker killed, 2 injured. US-flagged TSP vessel. Source: USNI News.

Fire, evacuation, 3 crew missing. Source: RFERL.
1 crew body recovered. Iraq oil ports fully stopped ops.
Hit in same cluster as Safesea Vishnu.

Targeted by UAVs, minor structural damage, proceeded to safe port.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Struck by drone, sustained minor damage, re-routed

VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Claimed attacked by Iranian cruise missiles during mine-clearing operation; denied by US Navy.

No vessel attacks on Day 45. However, US blockade enforcement begins at 10am ET. CENTCOM forces boarding and inspecting vessels in international waters that transited under IRGC toll regime. 320+ vessels remain at anchor. Tension extremely elevated but no kinetic incidents in first hours of blockade.
Vessel struck by a sea-skimming drone during nighttime transit; no oil spill reported and crew evacuated to safe room.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Vessel struck by two unidentified projectiles causing fire on board; situation stabilized.
Chinese-owned tanker initially turned back from US blockade, then successfully passed through.

Minor damage to hull from drone strike

Indian refiners are reportedly refusing to discharge its cargo due to the risk of secondary U.S. sanctions.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Hit by an unknown projectile; UKMTO reported damage to several containers, but no fires or environmental impact.
Vessel reported a 'splash' in close proximity to the ship; no damage reported.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Fire extinguished, vessel under tow to safe anchorage.
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Struck by unidentified loitering munition 50nm east of Fujairah.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.loc: Gulf of Oman, ~55 nm ESE of Sur
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: IRGC Navy fast-boats shadowed vessel for ~40 minutes before breaking off on CENTCOM helicopter arrival. Master reported no boarding attempt.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Boarding interdicted by USN escort; vessel cleared to transit south.
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: IRGC Navy fast-boat element approached southbound VLCC at speed; aborted on USN P-8 vector and CG escort interposition. Master reported brief comms jamming. No damage, no casualties.
USS Spruance disabled and seized vessel after it bypassed blockade. U.S. forces fired into the engine room.
Intercepted by USS Spruance, engine room fired upon, seized by US Marines.

USS Mason issued bridge-to-bridge warnings and fired warning flares after an IRGC FIAC element approached escort convoy to within 800 yards. Craft disengaged; no exchange of fire. Incident logged by 5th Fleet as provocation consistent with prior ceasefire-era pattern.
Vessel struck by suspected drone debris; small fire quickly extinguished by crew.
Slight damage from suspected drone strike, fire extinguished.
Vessel struck by loitering munition while waiting for naval escort.
Targeted and subsequently seized by Iranian forces; currently anchored in Iranian waters.
Attacked by Iranian gunboat; bridge sustained significant damage from gunfire and RPGs before being escorted to Iranian coast.
Fired upon by IRGC forces and became stranded on the Iranian coast.
No new vessel attacks at run cutoff. Elevated risk per IRGC 23-Apr statement; US Navy Task Force 55 maintains blockade screen 50nm east of Strait chokepoint.
No new vessel attacks at run cutoff; elevated risk per IRGC 25-Apr statement following Round 2 collapse. US Navy Task Force 55 maintains blockade screen.

UKMTO advisory: Aframax product tanker reported suspicious fast-craft approach 30nm SE of Larak Island; vessel maneuvered, no boarding or weapons fire. US Navy Task Force 55 redirected ISR overhead. Reported to UAE coastguard for follow-up.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.loc: Near Jask Island, Strait of Hormuz approach
VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Two ASCMs reportedly hit; vessel forced to turn back per IRGC; US/CENTCOM responses pending
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: IRGC/Fars: two anti-ship cruise missiles fired from Iranian shore battery near Jask Island struck a US Navy vessel after warnings to halt were ignored; ship 'unable to continue route' and turned back. CENTCOM acknowledges 'engagement event' but does not confirm hits or damage as of 0600Z; Pentagon spokesman 'comprehensive assessment in progress'. Maj. Gen. Ali Abdollahi (Khatam al-Anbiya HQ) reiterates 'any foreign armed force entering Strait of Hormuz will be subjected to attack.' Iranian parliament National Security Commission chair Azizi declares Project Freedom a 'ceasefire violation.'

VESSEL_ATTACKS.vessel: Iranian small boats (6-7 craft) near Hormuz
VESSEL_ATTACKS.loc: Strait of Hormuz, vicinity of US Navy escort group
VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: US destroyer and naval helicopters destroyed 6-7 Iranian small boats per CENTCOM after the boats attempted to interfere with Project Freedom escort of two US-flagged merchant vessels through Hormuz; Trump publicly stated 'seven small boats' shot down. No US/escorted casualties reported.
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: First confirmed direct US-Iran combat losses since pre-ceasefire kinetic phase. CENTCOM PAO confirms two US-flagged merchant vessels successfully transited Strait of Hormuz under escort. IRGC announces 'martyrdom' of unspecified personnel.
VESSEL_ATTACKS.source: CENTCOM PAO, Trump Truth Social, IRGC statement, Reuters

VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Successful escorted transit eastbound through Hormuz under Project Freedom escort (1 of 2 US-flag vessels confirmed transited overnight); no engagements affecting hull
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: First publicly named US-flag merchant transit under Project Freedom escort framework; underwriter terms set at +4.0% war-risk premium with US Navy escort discount of -0.6%
Struck by fast attack craft swarm and suicide drone.
Direct hit by loitering munition on accommodation block.
Struck by loitering munition while approaching the strait, minor damage to superstructure, proceeded under own power.
Inbound drone intercepted by coalition frigate nearby; vessel continued voyage unharmed.

Vessel sustained minor damage from drone shrapnel; continued voyage under own power.
Minor structural damage from drone strike on starboard bow.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Drone strike caused minor structural damage; fire contained.
