INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: 7 of 12 IG P&I Clubs issued war-risk cancellation notices effective midnight Mar 5. Covered Iranian waters and full Gulf region.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: JWC emergency session — Listed Areas expanded
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Lloyd's JWC expanded to full Gulf: Bahrain, Djibouti, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar added. 48-hr cancellation clause invoked. Neil Roberts, JWC Sec., confirmed.
Mitsui Sumitomo halted all new war-risk underwriting near Iran, Israel and neighboring nations.
Coverage excluded from Gulf and Iranian waters as of midnight Mar 5. AWRP for standard MR tanker: $40,000/7-day HRA stay; younger MRs $80-120k/7 days.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Fitch: credit negative for US P&C insurers
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Fitch flagged credit-negative for US P&C insurers with Gulf marine war exposure. Jefferies: $1.75bn losses from ~7 vessels as of Mar 5.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Lloyd's List: double-digit millions per Hormuz trip
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Lloyd's underwriter: 10% minimum for VLCC. Hypothetical 5-yr VLCC on US charter: $10-14m per Hormuz voyage. Historical Tanker War peak was 5%.
US International DFC committed $20bn rolling reinsurance covering hull, machinery war risk and cargo for Gulf transit.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Lloyd's CEO: cover must stay available
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Lloyd's CEO Tiernan: critical cover stays available. LMA: ~1,000 vessels in Gulf worth >$25bn. Cover case-by-case at ~5% of vessel value.
Coverage costs reached 5% of ship value, 5x early war levels

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Chubb Named Main US Gulf Insurer
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: $20B plan with DFC to help Persian Gulf shipping resume
Over 1,000% increase since conflict began. Rates for transiting Gulf are 1%-3% of a vessel's hull value.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Major P&I clubs excluded Iranian and adjacent waters
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Gard, Skuld, and NorthStandard formally excluded these waters from standard war-risk policies.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: U.S. Government intervention in insurance market
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: President Trump ordered DFC to provide political risk insurance and guarantees at subsidized rates to ensure continued energy flow, as private insurers retreat.
War-risk insurance premiums reached 3.5% to 10% of vessel value. Many underwriters have completely withdrawn appetite for the Persian Gulf region.
Major syndicates withdraw hull coverage for Persian Gulf transit.
Additional premiums announced for Red Sea approaches.
Premiums for Gulf transits quoted as high as 7.5% of hull value. Cargo insurance ranges 1% to 2.5%.
Lloyd's Syndicates and P&I Clubs issuing 7-day cancellation notices for Gulf transits, effectively making some US/Israel-tied vessels uninsurable.
Lloyd's syndicates raise Gulf of Oman premiums to 8.5%
Major insurers pause new quotes for Persian Gulf transit

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: War Risk Premium Spike and Cancellations
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: War-risk premiums spiked to 3.5%-10% of vessel value. Major insurers (Gard, Skuld, London P&I Club) issued cancellation notices for war risk coverage in the Gulf.
War risk premiums for Persian Gulf transits surged to 1-3%, up from 0.1-0.25% pre-conflict.
Iran targeting ADCOP pipeline to prevent bypassing Hormuz.
Many insurers have issued notices of cancellation to renegotiate terms, and some have withdrawn from the region entirely, though coverage remains technically available.
DFC directed to provide political risk insurance to stabilize trade in the Gulf.
Western underwriters withdrawing automatic coverage for Persian Gulf; JWC expands Listed Areas. Cargo war risk up to 10-20%.
Premiums for Hormuz transits surged from under 1% to 3.5%-10% of vessel hull value, costing tens of millions per voyage.
Many P&I clubs struggling to secure reinsurance, leaving some vessels unable to obtain coverage for Hormuz crossings.
The Trump administration doubled its maritime insurance guarantee to $40 billion through the International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) to lure ships back.
JWC maintains Hormuz as absolute exclusion zone. No quotes provided for any Persian Gulf ports.
U.S. government doubled reinsurance guarantees for ships transiting the Strait to $40 billion, joined by AIG, Berkshire Hathaway, and Travelers.
Major syndicates at Lloyd's announce complete suspension of new policies for vessels entering the Arabian Sea north of 15°N.
Three major London P&I clubs issue 72-hour cancellation notices for Gulf war-risk.
Reinsurers halt backing for any vessel not paying the unofficial Iranian 'toll'.
The U.S. Development Finance Corporation backed a $20 billion reinsurance facility to stabilize the market. Commercial insurers still avoiding the Upper Gulf.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Transit fee proposed and selective uninsurability
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Iran considering up to $2 million transit fee per ship for safe passage. Selective uninsurability for US/Israeli linked vessels continues.
Lloyd's List reports insurers are wary that reimbursing safe passage tolls paid to Iran could violate sanctions due to the IRGC being a designated terrorist organization.
U.S. DFC deployed a $20 billion Maritime Reinsurance program to stabilize the market.
phase: 3. NorthStandard added a specialized 'Iran Conflict layer' to its GlobeView platform for real-time threat assessments.
phase: 3. U.S. government doubled its Gulf reinsurance program to $40 billion to provide a safety net for underwriters.
Premiums surge; insurers completely withdraw coverage for vessels with US or Israel ties.
U.S. International Development Finance Corporation launched a $20 billion maritime reinsurance plan to provide coverage for U.S. and allied shipping that commercial insurers refuse to cover.
7 of 12 International Group P&I Clubs issued 72-hour cancellation notices for war-risk coverage in the Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman.
India establishes $1.5 billion sovereign guarantee fund to support its insurers. US offers TRIA coverage to encourage energy shipping.
Major insurers (Gard, Skuld, NorthStandard) cancelled war risk cover for the Gulf; premiums surged to 1% of ship value where available.

US naval blockade adds second layer of restriction atop IRGC toll regime. Lloyd's market expected to further widen exclusion zones. War risk premiums could reach 10-15% of hull value for any Gulf transit. Reinsurers pulling back from Middle East marine cover.
Lloyd's Market Association Joint War Committee expanded the listed area to encompass the broader Arabian Sea down to 10 degrees North.
War-risk insurance premiums softened to 0.35%-0.45% during the ceasefire but remain highly volatile following the US naval blockade order for the Strait of Hormuz.
Major reinsurers adopted a 'no-market' stance due to the blockade and mine risks, prompting carriers like Hapag-Lloyd to formally refuse passage.
War risk premiums for transiting the Strait of Hormuz surged to 3.0%-5.0% due to US Naval blockade and seizure risks.
Premiums reached up to 10% of vessel value. Insurers issuing Notice of Cancellation to hike rates.
Carriers implement war risk surcharges up to $3,000 per 40ft container for Middle East destinations.
Shipowners invoking London Blocking and Trapping Addendum due to US naval blockade and trapping in ports.
US government providing up to $40 billion in reinsurance guarantees to support neutral shipping avoiding Iranian ports.
Lloyd's market increased war risk premiums for Gulf of Oman transits to 3.5% of hull value.
Major syndicates freeze all remaining active war risk policies for vessels near Hormuz
U.S. government steps in with $40B in reinsurance guarantees for neutral vessels
The US International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) provides up to $40 billion in reinsurance guarantees to encourage shipping, limited primarily to US-linked insurers.
All 12 members of International Group of P&I Clubs maintain cancellation of war risk extensions.
Underwriters expand the war risk surcharge zone to include all of the Arabian Sea following recent escalating drone threats.
U.S. International Development Finance Corporation established a $40 billion reinsurance facility to provide political risk insurance for ships aligned with geoeconomic priorities.
IRGC reportedly charging an ad hoc toll of up to $2 million per ship for alleged 'mine clearance' in northerly transit lanes near Larak Island.
The Joint War Committee (JWC) of the London insurance market has expanded the 'Listed Areas' for hull war, piracy, and terrorism to include the entire Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Gulf.
A consortium of London insurers led by Beazley launched a $1 billion extra war risk facility to provide additional capacity for vessels transiting the high-risk zone.
Indian government approved the 'BMI pool' with a sovereign guarantee of ₹12,980 crore (approx. $1.55 billion) to provide affordable hull, cargo, and war risk insurance.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Launch of $1 billion marine war consortium
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: A $1 billion marine war consortium led by Beazley was launched at Lloyd’s to provide specialized capacity for vessels attempting to resume Hormuz transits.
Major syndicates systematically suspend quoting new coverage for vessels entering the Persian Gulf.
Key reinsurers trigger 48-hour cancellation clauses for Middle East block policies citing unquantifiable risk.

The Joint War Committee (JWC) expanded Listed Areas to include Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, and the UAE. Almost any movement in the region now requires specialized, high-cost notification and additional premiums despite the recent ceasefire.
Global reinsurers exclude Strait of Hormuz from collateral lists. Domestic companies citing inability to move ships.
Premiums up to 3-7.5% of hull value (10% for US-linked ships). $200M tanker insurance jumped to nearly $10M per voyage.

DFC announces second tranche ($18B additional capacity) now underwriting tanker, container, and bulker war-risk cover for third-country vessels transiting JMIC convoys. Premium-to-hull ratios down to 5.5% (from 5.8% Apr 20). US-linked ships remain at ~10%. Tanker insurance for a $200M VLCC now quoting ~$9.2M per voyage (vs ~$10M Apr 20).

DFC announces second tranche ($18B additional capacity) now underwriting tanker, container, and bulker war-risk cover for third-country vessels transiting JMIC convoys. Premium-to-hull ratios down to 5.5% (from 5.8% Apr 20). US-linked ships remain at ~10%. Tanker insurance for a $200M VLCC now quoting ~$9.2M per voyage (vs ~$10M Apr 20).
London insurance market underwriters maintain maximum war risk premiums for all Gulf and Red Sea transits.
Lloyd's Market Association expands the designated High Risk Area further into the Arabian Sea.
Major syndicates refuse to underwrite calls to smaller regional ports outside protected zones.
Rates have surged to 1%–5% of a ship's total hull value per transit in the Gulf. Major insurers issued notices of cancellation for standard cover.
Iran reportedly began charging 'safe corridor fees' of approximately $1 million to $2 million per ship for vessels allowed to pass.
JWC extends high-risk area to cover entire Gulf of Oman and northern Arabian Sea.
Major syndicates issue 7-day notice of cancellation for vessels lacking naval escort.
Joint War Committee expands high-risk Listed Areas further into the Arabian Sea to longitude 65°E, increasing affected voyage zones.
Major syndicates begin refusing underwriting for vessels with direct Israeli or US ownership operating east of Fujairah.
Sanctions placed on over 50 companies and 19 specific vessels involved in Iranian petroleum shipments
Iran unveiled its own maritime insurance program to provide coverage for vessels transiting the strait under Iranian oversight
Joint War Committee expands listed areas further into the Arabian Sea
US government reportedly considering a $20 billion reinsurance program to revive shipping through the region.
War-risk premiums for the Persian Gulf skyrocketed to 3%-5% of vessel value amid ongoing blockades.
JWC maintains entire Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman as highest risk category for hull war, piracy, and terrorism
Joint War Committee advises expanding breach area bounds deeper into the Arabian Sea.
Underwriters invoking 7-day cancellation clauses to strip standard coverage and renegotiate breach premiums.
Lloyd's Joint War Committee extends designated high-risk area further into the Arabian Sea due to increased long-range drone capabilities demonstrated in recent attacks.
Major London underwriters suspend quoting for all wet cargo entering the Persian Gulf following the MT Desert Falcon attack.
Major P&I clubs issue joint advisory warning against transits in the northern Arabian Sea without military escort, linking coverage to compliance.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: U.S. DFC mobilized for political risk insurance
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Major insurers maintain war risk cancellation for Persian Gulf; U.S. DFC steps in to offer discounted political risk insurance to stabilize allied commercial shipping.
US-backed maritime reinsurance facility wrote no cover due to lack of large-scale escort regime.
Two major syndicates issue notice of cancellation for all Persian Gulf transits, citing extreme USV risks.
Joint War Committee expands high-risk designated areas; base premium jumps to 5.5% of hull value.
Iran promoted state-backed initiative allowing shipping companies to settle insurance payments via Bitcoin to bypass sanctions and maintain transit.
Marine insurers estimate a minimum of 60 to 90 days of incident-free operations will be required post-peace agreement before standard coverage terms are reinstated.
The $20 billion DFC maritime reinsurance program has seen limited private sector uptake due to ongoing safety concerns for crews.
U.S. government deploying up to $20 billion in maritime reinsurance under TRIA to counter cancellation of war-risk cover by major mutual P&I clubs.
Offshore Support Vessels (OSVs) operating in the Persian Gulf saw a tenfold increase in premiums, paying up to $100,000 for two weeks of coverage, as P&I Clubs restrict Hormuz policies.
Iran's Ministry of Economy unveiled a state-backed digital insurance pool titled 'Hormuz Safe' following the exodus of Western commercial marine insurers.
Major European P&I clubs issue coordinated notice expanding the high-risk exclusion zone 50nm further into the Arabian Sea.
Major syndicates withdraw all coverage for vessels calling at any southern Iranian or contested Omani ports.
JWC issues circular maintaining entire Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, and northern Arabian Sea as highest-tier listed areas
U.S. $40B government-backed insurance facility under Project Freedom paused; zero business written due to ongoing physical danger.
Insurers warn state-backed GPS jamming may trigger war exclusions in standard corporate cyber policies.
Joint War Committee expands High Risk Area to include the entire Arabian Sea up to the coast of Mumbai due to long-range strikes
War risk premiums for Oman Gulf transits increased to 7.5% of hull value following fresh drone intercepts.
Three major London syndicates announce temporary halt on writing new hull war risk policies for the Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman.
Lloyd's Market Association warns of $25B-$40B exposure if 2,000 stranded vessels remain trapped in the Gulf for 12 months.
US OFAC designates UAE and Chinese front companies for shipping disguised Iranian LPG.
Major International Group P&I clubs issue a joint circular declaring the upper Gulf of Oman a prohibitive risk zone, effectively halting new hull and machinery coverage for the area.
Joint War Committee begins consultative review on expanding high-risk zones further south into the Indian Ocean due to extended drone ranges.
P&I clubs warn members of potential unbudgeted additional calls if Hormuz transit delays and escort requirements persist into Q3.
Emergency premiums implemented for all tankers entering or exiting the Persian Gulf. Hull coverage limits capped.
Insurance rates jumped 50-100%; DFC deployed $20B reinsurance program.
Transit premiums surged to 1.5%-3% for standard vessels and 3%-5% for US/UK/Israeli-linked ships, pushing single VLCC transit costs to $1.8M-$3.6M.
Joint War Committee expands high-risk designated zone further south in the Arabian Sea, affecting regional underwriting.
London market underwriters implement hard floor on Gulf war risk premiums at 4.0% of hull value.
Joint War Committee issues circular expanding the high-risk exclusionary zone to encompass the entire Arabian Sea up to 15 degrees North.
Joint War Committee explicitly expands the Listed Area high-risk zone to cover the entire Arabian Sea down to 10°N latitude.
Lloyd's Market Association syndicates halt quotes for vessels calling at Bandar Abbas.
Lloyd's Market Association signals potential baseline AWRP adjustments following recent Gulf of Oman interceptions.
India's Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool expands cover limits by $200M to accommodate diverted crude shipments.
Joint War Committee maintains the current Gulf Listed Areas without expansion, citing stabilized but high threat levels.
Joint War Committee expands High Risk Area to include extended Indian Ocean coordinates up to 65E.
Major P&I Clubs terminate standard war-risk extensions in the Gulf. Premiums surge to 3-8% of vessel value.
Premiums reached 4% of hull value for Hormuz transits due to active Iranian targeting and loss of fortuity. Some vessels are going dark to avoid costs.
US International Development Finance Corporation established a $40 billion reinsurance facility as insurer of last resort.
JWC issues revised guidelines pushing war risk premiums up to 3.5% of hull value after the MT Horizon Spark attack.
War-risk premiums stabilized at 1%, but underwriters strictly quote 'no-go' for transits lacking international sanction clearance.

CONTAINER_RATES.note: CMA CGM $3,000/FEU emergency surcharge. Most carriers suspended Gulf bookings.
+$485/FEU (+20%) since conflict onset. Hapag-Lloyd $70-75/TEU surcharge eff. ~Mar 23.
+$583/FEU (+16%). Most impacted route after Dubai direct.

CONTAINER_RATES.note: +$191/FEU (+10%). Diverted Atlantic Basin demand.
+19% WoW week of Mar 12. Source: Trans.INFO/Drewry Mar 14.
Rates spiked due to war surcharges and diversions
1% weekly increase, global fuel surcharges applied
Spot rates jumped 26% in the last month. 147 container ships sheltering in Arabian Gulf.
Rates rose 22%. 147 container ships sheltering in Arabian Gulf.
Rates plateau as rerouting becomes standard.
Early April spot rates hit $2,200-$2,700 (20-25% increase). Middle East/Red Sea route war surcharges spiked by nearly 200%.
Middle East surcharges driving rates up by nearly 200%. Far East to Europe up 20-25%.
Rates surged to over $7,000 per FEU by late March.
Jumped 12% in late March due to conflict and rerouting.
Major shipping firms continue Hormuz suspension. Iran selective reopening: China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, Thailand, Philippines flagged ships allowed.
Major carriers (MSC, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd) implement Emergency Fuel Surcharges of $60 to $190 per TEU.
119 container vessels (inc. 17 ULCVs) in holding pattern; port call frequency collapsed by 50%.
Rates surged to $3,500 by early April.
Rates reached $6,500 - $7,000 by early April.

CONTAINER_RATES.route: Far East to US West Coast
CONTAINER_RATES.note: Spot rates reached $2,430/FEU by early April (up 29%).
Spot rates heading toward $6,000 per FEU, an increase of over 270%.
MSC introduced $800 surcharge per container; CMA CGM imposed $3,000 per 40ft war-risk surcharge.
Rates to the region spiked by nearly 200% over the last month; carriers applied emergency fees up to $3,000/FEU.
Rates up roughly 40% to $2,645/FEU.
Rates spiked nearly 200%. War Risk Surcharges reach $3,000 per FEU.
Average spot rates heading toward $6,000 per FEU, an increase of over 270% compared to late February.
Rates climbed to approximately $2,430 per FEU, representing a 29-37% increase since the start of the conflict.
Rates climb again amid the $1M-$2M Iranian safe corridor fee
Approximately 119 container ships, including 17 ULCVs, remain in holding patterns or loitering inside the Arabian Gulf, unable to safely exit.
Spot rates surged toward $6,000 per FEU, a 270% increase compared to late February levels, as reported by Xeneta mid-April.
Rates rose 37% by mid-April due to 'ripple effect' congestion at major Asian transshipment hubs like Singapore and Port Klang.
Drewry WCI reported spot rates between $3,552 and $3,671 per FEU as of mid-April.

CONTAINER_RATES.route: Far East to US East Coast
CONTAINER_RATES.note: Spot rates for 40-foot containers reached approximately $3,382 in early April.
Rates plateauing after consecutive weekly increases following Cape of Good Hope rerouting.
May Phase 3 rates eclipsed March peaks, topping $7,500 per FEU due to renewed blockades.
Prices jumped to nearly $4,400 per FEU in May.

Rates surged to $2,900-$4,400/FEU by late May due to Cape of Good Hope rerouting.
May late-month spot rates exceed $8,200 due to entrenched Cape of Good Hope routing.
SCFI doubled since February. Carriers passing $300M-$500M monthly extra fuel costs via Emergency Bunker Surcharges.
MSC revised EFS rates up to $810 per 40ft reefer container.
Many carriers refusing entry to the Persian Gulf, leading to severe cargo backlogs, container equipment shortages, and pull-forward of peak-season demand.
Spot container rates remain elevated at ~150% above pre-war levels due to mandatory Cape of Good Hope rerouting.
Prices hold at extreme highs as the 3,800 nautical mile Cape detour becomes standard operating procedure for major alliances.
June 4: Drewry WCI surged 31% to $4,565 per FEU.
Drewry WCI reached $3,344 on June 4, up 80% since February.
Spot rates rose 98% to $5,505.

US tanker set ablaze at Mina Salman, Bahrain.
UAE's main non-Hormuz port. Traffic reduced 40% by drone incidents.
Indian crew member killed. Tanker struck off Muscat.
Partial commercial reopening. Emirates resumed limited schedule.
DXB back to ~60% capacity — then suspended again on Day 11.
9 of 35 drones penetrated UAE defenses. Flights grounded again.
Primary Gulf ship bunkering suspended. Global shipping costs spike.
Entire airline fleet moved to Saudi after Muharraq depot fire.
Bahrain GP cancelled — estimated $400M economic loss.
Fire from debris. Hub serves 15% of global ship refueling.
Both Hormuz and Red Sea effectively closed

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: IRGC Navy prevented three container ships from transiting Strait of Hormuz
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Disruption of major shipping route, forced vessels to turn around.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Saint Kitts and Nevis-flagged container ship SELEN expelled from Strait of Hormuz
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Disruption of shipping, highlighted new requirements for 'mandatory permits'.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Container ship from earlier attack remained aflame
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Persistent danger to abandoned or damaged hulls in the region.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: MV Pacific Zircon struck by explosive USV off Bandar Abbas
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: 3rd confirmed tanker attack in Strait since Jan 2026. VLCC freight rates surged 15%. US Navy escorted 4 tankers through Strait on Mar 30.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: War-risk premiums reach 3.5-7.5% of hull value; 150+ vessels diverted or anchored
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: $9M insurance cost per transit for $300M tanker. Crew safety concerns now primary barrier to transit, not insurance availability.
Iran demanding fees from non-hostile vessels (Chinese, Turkish, Pakistani) permitted to pass Strait of Hormuz.
Shipping industry absorbing an additional €340 million ($370 million) per day in fuel costs globally due to rerouting.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Hormuz 98% blocked. 150+ tankers anchored. IRGC fast attack craft fire on tankers. Khor Fakkan port targeted.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Largest oil supply disruption in history. SPR drawdowns accelerating.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iran FM announces safe passage through Hormuz for 2 weeks if vessels coordinate with Iranian armed forces. 8 tankers transited Monday (up from <2/day in March). War-risk insurance still at 5-10% hull value. $2M Iranian transit fee per vessel proposed. US $20B reinsurance program active.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Ceasefire Agreement for Strait of Hormuz
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: U.S. and Iran reached a tentative two-week ceasefire to allow safe passage and extraction of approximately 800 vessels trapped in the Persian Gulf.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: 2,000 ships and 20,000 seafarers stranded. Major carriers like Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd maintain suspension of normal operations.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Global supply chain delays, 8 million bpd regional crude stranded

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: HORMUZ RE-CLOSED. Iran halts safe passage commitment after Israeli Lebanon strikes. Only a handful of vessels transited Apr 8 before closure. Tehran demands crypto tolls from ship owners. Lloyd's List reports tanker traffic remains near zero. Red Sea alternative route congested.

HORMUZ RE-CLOSED. Iran halts safe passage after Israeli Lebanon strikes. Only handful of vessels transited Apr 8 before closure. Tehran demands crypto tolls. Tanker traffic near zero. Red Sea alternative congested.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Most Western-linked tonnage continues rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope until the security situation is fully verified.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Most Western-linked tonnage continues rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope until the security situation is fully verified.
Brent crude plummeted to $92-94/bbl following ceasefire, but 250,000 TEU capacity remains displaced.
Iran demands tolls up to $2M for safe passage; ceasefire breaches reported including a pipeline attack.

Only 2 tankers transited Hormuz since ceasefire. Iran conditioning passage on toll payments up to $2M. NEC Director Hassett says even one tanker is a 'big deal.' 13M bpd of production remains shut in across Middle East. Ship owners unwilling to transit without clear safe passage guarantees.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Iran Traffic Separation Scheme and Tolls
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: phase: 3. Iran requires vessels to coordinate directly with IRGC Navy. Reports of an unofficial 'Tehran toll booth' demanding up to $2M per transit.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Iran charging $1M-$2M per ship for passage via Larak Island. 2,000-3,200 vessels stranded west of Hormuz.
Forced routing north of Larak Island
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: The IRGC issued a directive requiring all vessels to transit through Iranian territorial waters, specifically north of Larak Island, to avoid alleged naval mines.
Reports indicate Iran is attempting to institutionalize a toll-collection regime, proposing fees as high as $2 million per transit. The U.S. issued direct warnings against the practice.
The Suez Canal Authority introduced 15% discounts for large container ships in an attempt to lure traffic back amid widespread diversions around the Cape of Good Hope.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Hormuz transit remains near-zero — only 2 tankers since ceasefire. Iran demanding permission/toll ($1M+) for passage. IMO reports ~2,000 ships stranded including 230 loaded tankers. Insurance premiums remain at war-risk levels.

Hormuz transit remains near-zero — only 2 tankers since ceasefire. Iran demanding permission and toll ($1M+) for passage. IMO reports ~2,000 ships stranded including 230 loaded tankers. Insurance premiums remain at war-risk levels.

Hormuz transit remains near-zero — only 2 tankers since ceasefire. Iran demanding permission and toll (1M+) for passage. IMO reports ~2,000 ships stranded including 230 loaded tankers. Insurance premiums remain at war-risk levels.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Kuwait officially accused Iran and its proxies of launching drone attacks against vital facilities on Thursday evening.
U.S. Navy destroyers entered the Strait of Hormuz for mine clearance. Iran condemned the action as a ceasefire violation and threatened to attack.
Iran is demanding pre-approval and charging safe passage 'tolls' exceeding $1 million per ship, establishing a de facto commercial blockade.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Hormuz transits remain near-zero. ~800 freighters stuck in Persian Gulf holding pattern. Pre-war average ~130-135 ships/day reduced by ~95%. IRGC controls Northern Corridor for limited civilian passages only. Iran drafting transit fee law ($2M per vessel). US DFC offering $40B revolving reinsurance for hull/cargo/liability.

Hormuz transits near-zero. ~800 freighters stuck in Gulf holding pattern. Pre-war ~130-135 ships/day, now reduced ~95%. IRGC controls Northern Corridor for limited civilian passages. Iran drafting transit fee law ($2M/vessel). US DFC $40B revolving reinsurance for hull/cargo/liability active.
Hormuz near-zero transits. ~800 freighters stuck. Pre-war 130+ ships/day reduced ~95%. IRGC Northern Corridor limited. Iran transit fee law drafted. DFC 40B reinsurance active.
Hormuz near-zero transits. ~800 freighters stuck. Pre-war 130+ ships/day reduced ~95%. IRGC Northern Corridor limited. Iran transit fee law drafted. DFC $40B reinsurance active.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iran has begun charging tolls of over $1 million per ship for passage through the Strait of Hormuz.
USS Frank E. Peterson and USS Michael Murphy transited Strait of Hormuz to clear mines.
U.S. Navy ordered total blockade of Strait of Hormuz to interdict vessels paying Iranian protection fees.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Transit dropped 90%, 1,000 ships in holding pattern.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: CENTCOM forces begin implementing blockade of all maritime traffic entering/exiting Iranian ports at 10am ET per presidential proclamation. US Navy to intercept vessels in international waters that paid tolls to Iran. 320+ vessels already at anchor awaiting transit. Second layer of restriction atop IRGC toll regime.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: U.S. Naval Blockade of Strait of Hormuz
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: U.S. Navy ordered to clear mines and block Iranian oil exports following the collapse of peace talks in Islamabad.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: US blockade enforcement begins; Hormuz transits at historic lows
SHIPPING_EVENTS.details: CENTCOM enforces blockade on all vessels entering/exiting Iranian ports starting 10 AM ET. Non-Iranian port transits through Hormuz permitted. Windward data: only 17 vessels crossed Hormuz on Apr 12 (down from ~130 daily pre-war). Lloyd's List: shipping 'immediately halted' after blockade announcement. Tanker diversions to Oman's Sohar and Duqm ports increasing. Saudi East-West pipeline at max capacity.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: U.S. Central Command initiated and enforced a formal blockade of all vessels entering or leaving Iranian ports in the region.
Over 730 cargo and tanker vessels remain trapped or anchored in the Gulf, unable to transit due to the blockade and lack of insurance.
US Naval blockade of Iranian ports goes into effect. Traffic drops drastically with only two vessels exiting the Strait of Hormuz.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: US naval blockade Day 3; Hormuz transit down >90%
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: US blockade targets ships calling at Iranian ports. At least 3 vessels including 2 US-sanctioned tankers transited Hormuz on first full day. JMIC Bahrain reports daily traffic down >90%. Iran calls blockade 'piracy.' UK-led 40-nation coalition planning freedom of navigation operations. IEA characterizes as largest supply disruption in oil market history.
CENTCOM reports 100% interdiction rate redirecting merchant vessels from Iranian ports.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Six merchant vessels attempting to leave or enter Iranian ports intercepted and forced to turn back.
Dark fleet tankers attempting transits using AIS spoofing forced to return to Iranian ports by US Navy.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: USS Spruance Redirects Iranian Cargo Vessel
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Iranian-flagged cargo vessel attempting to evade US blockade after leaving Bandar Abbas was intercepted and redirected by guided-missile destroyer USS Spruance (DDG-111). Separate tanker observed departing Iranian port despite blockade per MarineTraffic data. CENTCOM states non-Iranian port traffic unimpeded.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Three major shipping lines paused all Hormuz transits pending security review.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Salalah and Jebel Ali report maximum capacity, vessels forced to anchor offshore

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz officially drops by more than 90 percent
Iran reportedly begins charging a $2 million 'safe corridor fee' per ship for non-Western vessels allowed to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Further increases operational costs for non-Western vessels (Turkish, Indian, Chinese-flagged) transiting the Strait.
Summit to discuss a multinational plan to restore freedom of navigation.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: France and the UK co-chaired a 40-nation summit to discuss a multinational plan to restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, envisioning a defensive mission involving mine-clearing vessels and naval escorts.
U.S. issued an ultimatum to Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours or face further strikes on critical infrastructure, which Iran rejected.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Several major naval escorts suspended operations temporarily due to increased mine risks, stranding 30+ tankers.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Daily traffic through the Strait of Hormuz reduced from ~140 ships to fewer than 15 due to U.S. blockade and Iranian transit restrictions.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Severe reduction in global shipping lane capacity.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Over 150 vessels loitering or anchored near the Strait of Hormuz, hesitant to transit due to blockade and insurance cancellations.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Major supply chain delays and congestion outside the Gulf.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: U.S. CENTCOM reported that at least 19 vessels attempting to enter or depart Iranian ports were intercepted and forced to turn around.

Iranian Foreign Minister declared the Strait open, but the IRGC and Parliament Speaker immediately countermanded this, stating the waterway remains closed to any vessel related to belligerent countries.
Iran officially announced the re-closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to the continued U.S. naval blockade, effectively ending the ceasefire at sea.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Transit blocked for most commercial traffic
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iran reversed its decision to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, citing the continuation of a U.S. naval blockade on its ports, returning control to its previous blocked state.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Market confusion and delayed transit resumptions.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iranian Foreign Minister announced the strait was open, but IRGC imposed strict conditions and effectively maintained the blockade.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Total paralysis of commercial shipping in the region.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: IRGC warns that any vessel approaching the strait will be considered as cooperating with the enemy and targeted.

CENTCOM confirms 3 tankers turned back Apr 18-19 under blockade protocols; JMIC Bahrain logs 4 safe southbound transits with escort. Araghchi statement on 'Hormuz open' contradicted by on-water reality.

Joint Maritime Information Center logs 7 safe southbound transits and 2 northbound (cleared cargoes) under blockade convoy regime in 24h window. CENTCOM flags one near-miss boarding (MT Amber Pacific, see VESSEL_ATTACKS). No transit denials issued for third-country flagged tonnage carrying non-Iranian cargo.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Asia-EU containers via Cape adding ~14 days vs Suez baseline
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Maersk and CMA CGM extend Cape rerouting on Asia-Europe loops through May. Container spot rates Shanghai-Rotterdam hold at ~$5,800/FEU (+118% YoY). Linerex bulker rerouting now ~28% of Gulf-origin cargoes.

Joint Maritime Information Center logs 7 safe southbound transits and 2 northbound (cleared cargoes) under blockade convoy regime in 24h window. CENTCOM flags one near-miss boarding (MT Amber Pacific, see VESSEL_ATTACKS). No transit denials issued for third-country flagged tonnage carrying non-Iranian cargo.
First seizure of an Iranian-flagged vessel by the U.S. Navy during the conflict.
Iran attempting to formalize control by demanding $2 million USD per vessel for priority transit through Strait of Hormuz.
Transit dropped from 130 to 3 vessels per day. Military analysts estimate clearing mines for safe commercial navigation could take six months.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: JMIC Convoy Intake Resumes at +7 Vessels
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Joint Maritime Information Center convoy intake recovers to 7 vessels in 24h window (vs 3 in prior 12h on Apr 20) as escort mix adds FGS Hamburg and HMS Duncan to US destroyers. Fujairah anchorage wait time down to ~11h.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Mandatory combined maritime forces escorts are causing a significant 4-day transit backlog at key chokepoints.

Combined Maritime Forces escort queue wait times at Hormuz chokepoint continue to ease as coordination patterns mature; backlog estimated ~3 days vs the 4-day peak on Apr 21. Queue wait times for Fujairah bunker pickups near 9-10 hours (-1 to -2h vs prior day). Traffic throughput still elevated-risk vs pre-war baseline; continuation entry pending UKMTO advisory refresh.
Zero commercial transits through Strait of Hormuz recorded for the past 48 hours.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Severe delays in regional supply chains
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Naval escorts grouping vessels for transit through lower risk zones is causing massive staging delays.

Hormuz closure enters Day 11. Zero commercial transits through the Strait of Hormuz recorded for the prior 24-hour period. Cape of Good Hope rerouting remains the default backhaul path for VLCCs. Continuation entry pending IMO bulletin.
Approximately 2,000 ships and 20,000 mariners remain stranded within the Persian Gulf due to the strait closure and U.S. blockade.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Brent crude peaks at $126/barrel; global supply chain crisis.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: AIS aggregators (Kpler, MarineTraffic) report Strait of Hormuz transit volume holding ~5% of pre-war 178-vessel/day baseline. CENTCOM Apr 30 readout: 42 vessels redirected since Apr 13 blockade; 41 Iranian-affiliated tankers carrying ~69M bbl held off Bandar Abbas/Kharg/Jask. Cape of Good Hope reroute adds 14-18 days transit Asia-Europe. Source: Kpler, CENTCOM, MarineTraffic.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Hormuz traffic remains -95% vs pre-war; Cape reroute saturating
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: AIS aggregators (Kpler, MarineTraffic) report Strait of Hormuz transit volume holding ~5% of pre-war 178-vessel/day baseline. CENTCOM May 1 readout: 44 vessels redirected since Apr 13 blockade; 41 Iranian-affiliated tankers carrying ~69M bbl held off Bandar Abbas/Kharg/Jask. Cape of Good Hope reroute adds 14-18 days transit Asia-Europe; Suez/Bab al-Mandeb southbound transits +12% w/w as cargo shifts off Hormuz arbitrage. Source: Kpler, CENTCOM, MarineTraffic.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Hormuz traffic ~5% pre-war; CENTCOM blockade tally 48 Iranian vessels turned around in 20 days
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: AIS aggregators (Kpler, MarineTraffic) confirm Strait of Hormuz transit volume holding ~5% of pre-war 178-vessel/day baseline. CENTCOM May 2 PAO readout: 48 Iranian-affiliated vessels turned around since Apr 13 blockade onset (running 20 days); 41 Iranian-affiliated tankers carrying ~69M bbl held off Bandar Abbas/Kharg/Jask awaiting waiver. Cape of Good Hope reroute adding 14-18 days transit Asia-Europe; Suez/Bab al-Mandeb southbound transits +13% w/w. UNCTAD notes first signs of LNG-carrier tonnage shortage on Cape route as binding constraint independent of days-at-sea. Source: Kpler, CENTCOM, MarineTraffic, UNCTAD.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Project Freedom escort mission begins; Hormuz traffic falls to ~3% pre-war
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: CENTCOM kickoff Project Freedom 0500Z: destroyers, 100+ aircraft, unmanned platforms, ~15,000 personnel committed to escort neutral-flag commercial shipping out of Strait of Hormuz. AIS aggregators (Kpler, MarineTraffic) report Hormuz transit volume falls to ~5 vessels/24h (~3% of pre-war 178/day baseline) on Iranian threats and underwriter pull-back. CENTCOM May 4 readout: 50 vessels redirected since Apr 13 blockade; 41 Iranian-affiliated tankers (~69M bbl) held off Bandar Abbas/Kharg/Jask. Cape of Good Hope reroute saturating; Suez/Bab al-Mandeb southbound transits +18% w/w. Source: Kpler, CENTCOM, MarineTraffic, ABC, Times of Israel.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Project Freedom Day 2 — first US-flag merchant pair completes Hormuz transit; Iranian small-boat swarm destroyed
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: CENTCOM confirms two US-flagged merchant vessels (one Suezmax, one product tanker) completed eastbound Hormuz transit overnight under armed escort by USS Mason (DDG-87) and helicopter overwatch. Iranian IRGC Navy small-boat swarm of 6-7 craft destroyed during attempted interdiction; one Iranian cruise missile and several UAVs intercepted by escort group SM-2/RAM. Hormuz aggregate traffic Day 67 estimated 15-20 vessels/24h (~3-4% pre-war), of which ~6 under US escort.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.source: CENTCOM PAO, MARAD, Reuters, Lloyd's List

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: IMO confirms ~20,000 seafarers stranded on ~2,000 vessels in/around Hormuz
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: International Maritime Organization Secretary-General issued public statement on humanitarian dimension; renewed call for safe passage corridors. UAE/Oman maritime authorities coordinating welfare logistics.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Two US-flag merchants completed second escorted Hormuz transit overnight before pause announcement
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Maersk-operated US-flag MV Liberty Star and MV Pacific Sentinel completed eastbound transit at ~0630Z under USS Mason DDG-87 escort; no incidents. Trump pause announcement followed at ~1900Z.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: IMO confirms approximately 20,000 seafarers stranded on circa 2,000 vessels in/around Hormuz (Day 68)
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: International Maritime Organization Secretary-General issued public statement on humanitarian dimension; renewed call for safe passage corridors. UAE/Oman maritime authorities coordinating welfare logistics. Number unchanged from prior reporting — pause has not yet translated to vessel release.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Humanitarian / logistical bottleneck persists despite pause

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Two US-flag merchants completed second escorted Hormuz transit overnight before Project Freedom pause
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Maersk-operated US-flag MV Liberty Star and MV Pacific Sentinel completed eastbound transit at ~0630Z under USS Mason DDG-87 escort; no incidents. Trump pause announcement followed at ~1900Z effectively suspending further escorted transits.
UAE and US 5th Fleet mandate convoy formations for all liquid bulk transits in the Gulf.
Vessel backlog at Jebel Ali reaches 14 days due to enhanced naval inspections and reduced pilot availability.
Maersk and MSC announce total rerouting of all Middle East bound cargo via Cape of Good Hope, officially suspending Persian Gulf calls.
Port of Fujairah reports a massive 40% drop in vessel calls for bunkering as ships entirely avoid the region due to resumed hostilities.
Coalition naval forces initiate a structured convoy system for essential energy shipments transiting the Strait of Hormuz to mitigate attacks.
CENTCOM reported redirecting 109 commercial ships and disabling 4 for non-compliance
Reports of unofficial MoU framework to restore commercial shipping in 30 days if U.S. lifts blockade
Naval escorts for commercial vessels reduced due to increased operational tempo elsewhere
US and Iran negotiating a 60-day MoU for unrestricted shipping and mine removal in exchange for lifting the US naval blockade.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Severe delays at South African and West African bunker ports
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Vessels experiencing 4-7 day wait times for bunkering operations in Durban and Walvis Bay
Coalition forces mandate active naval escorts for all LNG and VLCC traffic exiting the Strait.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Severe cost increase for regional transit.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iran attempting to implement transit tolls of over $1 million per ship for Strait passage.
Coalition forces implement strict new convoy formation requirements, increasing transit wait times at the Fujairah anchorage up to 72 hours.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Severe delays in regional maritime supply chain.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Over 50 tankers idling outside Fujairah port limits as owners refuse to authorize Hormuz transits.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Transit delays increased by 48 hours for convoy formation.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Combined Maritime Forces implemented strict new convoy formation protocols for all commercial vessels entering the Gulf of Oman.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: IEA declares largest oil supply disruption in history
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: The IEA formally classified the crisis as the largest oil supply disruption in history. QatarEnergy maintains force majeure on LNG exports.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Markets react to rumors of a framework deal.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Reports indicate growing speculation of a framework deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for ending the U.S. naval blockade.
70 commercial vessels passed through Strait in dark mode under CENTCOM guidance due to insurance withdrawal.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: High operational risk and reliance on naval guidance.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iran claims sovereignty and demands compliance over paying Western war-risk premiums.
Daily transits drop 40% below pre-war averages as independent tanker operators halt Gulf loadings pending security guarantees.
Bunker barge delays stretch to 72 hours at Fujairah due to heightened naval security checks and bottlenecking.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Severe cargo backlogs and container equipment shortages
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Container operators re-evaluating routes as Umm Qasr port, previously considered safe, proved vulnerable to missile strikes, forcing rerouting away from the Gulf.
An estimated 99 million barrels of crude and 37 million barrels of oil products remain stranded on board 247 vessels within the Gulf. May exports dropped to 0.98 mb/d.
U.S. forces have disabled or rerouted at least 126 blockade-eligible ships since the blockade was initiated in April.
Enforcement of U.S. naval blockade has forced clusters of sanctioned, Iran-flagged cargo ships to anchor indefinitely outside Indian and Pakistani ports.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: 121 commercial ships redirected and 5 disabled since mid-April blockade.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Maersk, CMA CGM, and Hapag-Lloyd suspended all residual Persian Gulf transits, citing unsustainable risks and $500M monthly increases in operating bills.
Commercial naval escort operations temporarily suspended in the western Gulf of Oman due to elevated anti-ship missile threats.
IRGC Navy fast-attack craft aggressively intercepted three commercial ships, forcing a complete halt of inbound tanker traffic for 12 hours.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: India deploys three additional destroyers to Gulf of Oman for merchant escort operations due to elevated threat levels
Bunker wait times at Fujairah anchorage extend to 72 hours due to irregular supply arrivals and heightened security protocols

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iranian oil exports dropped 93% in May to 2.01 million barrels due to U.S. blockade enforcement.
Major container lines (CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd) suspended all bookings to major Persian Gulf ports to protect crews and assets.
Commercial transits through Suez Canal drop by 85% year-over-year; major alliances issue total suspension notices for Red Sea
Major carriers formally instruct all vessels to avoid Gulf of Oman until further notice.
UAE maritime authorities order clearing of outer anchorage areas citing imminent threat levels.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: 7 million barrels of oil exited Hormuz.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Kpler reported four Iranian tankers (Hilda I, Amber, Silvia 1, Happiness I) transited the Strait of Hormuz with AIS off.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: $1 per barrel toll demanded for escorted transit.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iran established the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) demanding transit tolls. US warned operators of sanctions.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: 4 drones destroyed; coastal radar sites bombed.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: US CENTCOM shot down 4 Iranian drones targeting shipping and struck radar sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island.
Combined naval task force temporarily suspends all commercial convoy escorts in the eastern Gulf of Oman due to unacceptably high anti-ship ballistic missile threats.
Naval escort convoy delayed 12 hours due to suspected mine-clearing operations ahead of the transit corridor.
Three VLCCs abruptly divert to Red Sea and West Coast India anchorages pending updated risk assessments following drone alerts.
Naval escorts temporarily halted due to active mine threats and aerial alerts.
Trade flows through the Strait of Hormuz are 90% below normal levels.
IRGC targeted and stopped 1 of 4 oil tankers exiting the Strait of Hormuz, claiming violation of newly declared Persian Gulf Strait Authority traffic scheme.
CENTCOM intercepted 2 Iranian attack drones targeting shipping corridors, preventing vessel strikes.
Indian Navy temporarily suspends escort operations in Eastern Arabian Sea due to increased submarine threats.
Joint naval escort operations temporarily suspended after heavy radar jamming, stranding 14 vessels outside the Strait.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Operation Prosperity Guardian pauses eastbound convoys for 48 hours to reassess naval deployments after repeated drone swarms.
Vessels waiting up to 10 days for bunkering outside the immediate high-risk zone, causing cascading delays in global tanker schedules.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Offshore bunkering operations temporarily halted at Fujairah anchorage due to proximity of naval skirmishes and airspace closures.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Cape of Good Hope Rerouting Extended
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Major carriers announce permanent rerouting around Africa through Q3 2026.
Vessels rerouting to Oman report 7-day delays for bunker fuel as Fujairah remains largely avoided due to war risk.
Vessels awaiting naval escorts at Fujairah anchorage face average delays of up to 2 days before entering the Gulf.
Multinational naval coalition temporarily pauses organized merchant convoys to reassess anti-ship missile threats from coastal batteries.
IRGC enforces parallel transit fee system charging ~$2M per vessel in crypto/yuan. Over 2,000 ships remain stranded.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: IRGC Navy declared the Strait of Hormuz completely closed to all commercial and oil tanker traffic.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Followed US airstrikes. Iran claimed to have hit two ships attempting to pass, though US CENTCOM refuted the claim.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Houthi movement declared a complete ban on Israeli-linked navigation in the Red Sea.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Threatened to target any violating vessels, compounding regional shipping disruptions.
Due to halted regional feedstock blending and severed supply, shipowners must now book spot bunker supplies 4 to 6 weeks in advance.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Major delays to crude and product shipments
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Coalition naval forces temporarily halt escorted convoys during daylight hours due to an elevated drone threat.
U.S. military aircraft fired two Hellfire missiles into M/T Jalveer engine room in the Gulf of Oman.
IRGC Navy intercepted and barred an oil tanker from entering the Strait of Hormuz without proper coordination.

IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US military confirmed only 6 deaths as of early March; CENTCOM stated Lincoln was not hit and missiles didn't come close

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: AI-generated satellite imagery showing 'massive crater' at Al Dhafra AFB runway
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Planet Labs commercial imagery from same date shows runway intact and operational. Metadata analysis confirms AI synthesis.

IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: DoD confirmed 16 US KIA total through Mar 27. Congressional briefings consistent with official count.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: UAE Air Force 'completely grounded' — all F-16s destroyed on tarmac at Al Dhafra
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: UAE MoD released same-day footage of F-16 Block 60 sorties. Al Dhafra operations continued per satellite imagery.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Urea shipments through Hormuz 'resumed under IRGCN escort' — Iran controlling global fertilizer supply
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Lloyd's Maritime Intelligence confirmed zero commercial transits through Hormuz since Day 14. IRGCN escort claim fabricated.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Day 31 record salvo (47 BMs) presented as 'all 47 struck targets without interception'
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: UAE MoD confirmed 39 of 47 intercepted. 8 leakers tracked — 3 sea-falls, 5 territory impacts (no casualties).

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Deepfake video of US CENTCOM commander announcing withdrawal from Gulf
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM public affairs immediately flagged as synthetic. Video uses archived press conference audio with lip-sync manipulation.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC claims US only destroyed one-third of Iran's missile production (implying two-thirds intact)
IRAN_DISINFO.source: Iranian state media / The Wire analysis
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: IDF assessment: 60-70% of pre-war targets destroyed. US intelligence confirms only one-third of drone fleet destroyed. Partial truth used for propaganda framing.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: AI-generated Lego depictions of Trump and Netanyahu used in Iranian propaganda videos
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Videos identified as AI-generated content designed to mock Western leaders and boost domestic morale.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US Fifth fleet commander killed and carrier heavily damaged

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US Navy vessel struck by anti-ship missile in the Gulf of Oman

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: 650 U.S. troops killed or wounded since the start of the war
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: U.S. CENTCOM confirmed only six service member deaths

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Massive destruction and 100+ casualties at Tel Aviv defense headquarters
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Commercial satellite imagery and independent journalists on the ground confirm no hits near the headquarters.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: USS Abraham Lincoln sunk in the Gulf of Oman.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US Navy released photos of carrier operating normally 24 hours post-claim.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Mutiny among US regional allies refusing to fly missions.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Joint GCC-US sorties documented daily by CENTCOM.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Successful strike on central command

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: U.S. Navy withdrew Carrier Strike Group from Gulf of Oman due to IRGC missile threats.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Carrier strike group repositioned for routine logistical replenishment per CENTCOM.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Beijing Protocol unfreezing of $500M assets strictly used for humanitarian aid and civilian infrastructure.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Intelligence indicates funds were diverted to restart military-industrial drone production lines.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC claims to have sunk US Arleigh Burke destroyer in Persian Gulf.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US 5th Fleet confirms all vessels accounted for and operational.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Tasnim reports successful destruction of Israeli Iron Dome battery with single hypersonic strike.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Satellite imagery and IDF statements confirm no damage to air defense batteries in the area.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC destroyed US destroyer in the Red Sea
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US Navy confirmed all ships safe; satellite imagery shows no damage

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Destroyed two Patriot batteries near Tel Aviv
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Alma Research Center and FDD confirm no damage to air defense installations

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Sinking of a US Arleigh Burke-class destroyer in the Persian Gulf
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US CENTCOM visual confirmation of all vessels unharmed; FDD analysis points to recycled 2020 footage.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US Navy aircraft carrier struck and retreating from the Gulf of Oman.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US CENTCOM released date-stamped visual evidence of normal flight operations; no damage reported.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US Patriot battery destroyed in Erbil and 15 US soldiers killed in coordinated drone and missile strikes.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM confirmed no casualties and Patriot systems remain fully operational after successful intercepts.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC destroyed two US Arleigh Burke-class destroyers in the Red Sea.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM visual evidence confirms all ships are fully operational; missiles were intercepted by SM-6.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC captured a US Navy SEAL team alive near Bandar Abbas.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM confirmed 100% personnel accountability; videos published were proven to be deepfakes utilizing old 2016 Farsi Island incident footage.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC claims to have sunk a US destroyer.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US CENTCOM confirmed all vessels accounted for; imagery shows no damage.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US forces retreating from Iraq bases.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: No change in force posture reported by coalition.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Direct hit resulting in critical damage to a US Arleigh Burke-class destroyer in the Strait of Hormuz.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM verified all vessels operating normally with zero damage; footage circulated was recycled from a 2021 naval exercise.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Destroyed US air base used for strikes on Sirik Island
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Claims of destruction of US bases are routinely exaggerated by IRGC; awaits independent BDA.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Military and naval combat capabilities remain fully intact despite Western strikes
IRAN_DISINFO.source: IRGC Spokesman Brigadier General Hossein Mohebbi
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Contradicts documented battle damage assessments of degraded Iranian military infrastructure.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Successful strikes on US bases in Kuwait and US Fifth Fleet headquarters
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: No independent confirmation of significant impact; likely intercepted or exaggerated.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC claimed sinking of a US Arleigh Burke-class destroyer in the Persian Gulf.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US 5th Fleet confirmed all vessels are safe and operating normally; no engagements recorded.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC completely destroyed Al Udeid Air Base runways causing full operational halt.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Commercial satellite imagery shows base fully operational with minor peripheral damage.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Fattah-2 hypersonic missile penetrated US Aegis defense network and sank a US Navy logistics ship.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM confirmed the missile was intercepted by SM-6; no US or allied ships were damaged. Satellite imagery confirms normal operations.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC destroyed multiple US Patriot batteries in Iraq
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Satellite imagery and CENTCOM statements show all batteries operational with no impact craters nearby

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC destroyed two US THAAD batteries in the UAE
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US CENTCOM and commercial satellite imagery (Planet Labs) confirm all THAAD sites are intact and fully operational. Video shared was recycled from a 2022 military exercise.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: A failed US Patriot interceptor missile malfunctioned and fell onto Kuwait International Airport Terminal 1, causing the civilian casualties.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM declared the claim totally FALSE, confirming the terminal was directly struck by Iranian drones.
IRAN_DISINFO.type: False blame / Denial of civilian targeting

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Missile and drone barrage destroyed targets at U.S. bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Most intercepted by CENTCOM; strikes in Kuwait hit civilian Terminal 1, killing 1 and injuring 63, rather than military base destruction.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Successfully targeted and struck an American military vessel in the Sea of Oman.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: U.S. military strongly denied the claim, stating no U.S. vessels had been struck or damaged.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Massive structural collapse at US 5th Fleet Headquarters in Bahrain with hundreds dead.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Commercial satellite imagery from Planet Labs and statement from US CENTCOM confirm operations are normal; only a perimeter security checkpoint was lightly damaged by falling debris.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Oman has officially denied US airspace access following IRGC threats.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Omani Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued an official denial, reaffirming existing logistical agreements.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC claims complete withdrawal of US forces from Iraq following missile strikes.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM denies withdrawal; routine troop rotations ongoing.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Destruction of three Patriot missile batteries at Al-Asad.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Satellite imagery shows no damage to air defense infrastructure.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Senior UAE official Anwar Gargash secretly cooperated with Israel's Mossad to shape aggressive regional policies against Iran.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: ISW-CTP assessed this as a coordinated Iranian effort to sow divisions between the US and Gulf partners.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Iranian military fired warning Qader missiles and Shahed-Dana attack drones at US destroyers DDG-103 and DDG-87, forcing them to retreat.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US CENTCOM strongly denied these claims, stating no shots were fired at US warships and calling the claims a fabrication.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US aircraft carrier severely damaged and sinking in the Gulf of Oman following hypersonic strike.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US Navy 5th Fleet confirmed all carriers are safe; no commercial satellite imagery corroborates the claim; open-source shipping tracking remains unaffected.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC claims destruction of a US Aegis destroyer in the Red Sea.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US CENTCOM confirms no vessels were targeted or damaged; standard operations continue.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Tasnim reports successful hack of Israeli Iron Dome command servers preventing intercepts.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Israeli Cyber Directorate denied breach; visual evidence confirms Iron Dome intercepted incoming projectiles.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Sinking of an Arleigh Burke-class destroyer and severe damage to USS Abraham Lincoln in the Gulf of Oman.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM visual evidence and OSINT confirm the Carrier Strike Group is unharmed; all incoming vectors were intercepted by AEGIS and CAP.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC fired warning shots at two US destroyers in the Sea of Oman, forcing their retreat.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM denied the report, stating no engagement occurred and US warships continue to operate freely.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Damage to Kuwait International Airport Terminal 1 was caused by a malfunctioning US Patriot air defense missile, not an Iranian strike.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM and Kuwaiti officials rejected the claim as disinformation, confirming Iranian drones deliberately struck the civilian facility.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Israeli forces are using Azerbaijani soil for military operations against Iran.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Baku officially rejected the claims as entirely baseless and part of a disinformation campaign.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US carrier strike group retreated from Gulf of Oman after drone swarm
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM satellite feeds and maritime trackers show routine stationing

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Mass casualties at Israeli airbase following coordinated strike
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Alma Research Center verified commercial satellite imagery showing strike missed the base entirely

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Tasnim News claimed US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln is sinking after drone swarm attack.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM released photos of the carrier operating normally; no impacts reported.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Direct ballistic missile hit on USS Dwight D. Eisenhower causing massive fires and casualties.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: OSINT analysts and CENTCOM confirmed carrier operations are nominal; broadcasted footage was identified as a mirrored video from a 2020 IRGC naval exercise.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC claims 50 US military personnel were killed in Bahrain strike and USS Carter Hall was severely damaged.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM confirms zero casualties. Missile impacted the water 5km off the coast. USS Carter Hall is currently deployed in the Red Sea, not Bahrain.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Complete destruction of US Patriot battery in Erbil
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Commercial satellite imagery confirms no damage at the battery site; CENTCOM reported all inbound intercepted.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Total destruction of US aircraft carrier in the Gulf of Oman.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: OSINT satellite imagery shows carrier operating normally; US DoD denied the claim.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Successful sinking of a US Arleigh Burke-class destroyer and death of over 100 US sailors.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM visual evidence and official statements confirm all vessels are fully operational with zero casualties.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US aircraft carrier severely damaged in the Gulf by drone swarm.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM confirmed no attacks on naval assets; commercial satellite imagery shows carrier unimpeded.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC completely destroyed US Central Command forward headquarters in Qatar.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM released live footage of operations at Al Udeid; independent commercial satellite imagery shows minor debris damage outside base perimeter.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US Airbase completely destroyed, 50 casualties.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Satellite imagery shows minor damage to a taxiway, 0 casualties reported.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Direct hit on USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group causing mass casualties and sinking of an escort destroyer.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US CENTCOM confirms carrier group is operating normally in the Gulf of Oman with zero hits; satellite imagery verifies intact fleet.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Destruction of 5 Iron Dome batteries in northern Israel.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: ISW and IDF reporting confirm only minor shrapnel damage to one interceptor site; no batteries destroyed.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Direct hit on US CENTCOM forward headquarters in Jordan, allegedly killing 15 US personnel.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM denied any attacks on personnel; satellite imagery shows no damage to bases in Jordan.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Destruction of three Iron Dome batteries in northern Israel.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: IDF confirmed no damage to air defense arrays; interceptions successfully completed.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Successful destruction of three F-35 aircraft on the ground at Nevatim.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Commercial satellite imagery from Jun 11 shows no damage to hangars or runways at Nevatim.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US Navy vessel heavily damaged in the Persian Gulf by drone swarm.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US 5th Fleet confirmed all vessels are safe and conducting normal operations; no distress signals recorded.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC claims to have sunk a US Arleigh Burke-class destroyer in the Strait of Hormuz during overnight operations.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: US CENTCOM confirmed all vessels safe and accounted for; independent satellite imagery shows normal maritime traffic with no wreckage.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US carrier severely damaged by hypersonic strike
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM confirms all assets unharmed; intercepts successful

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.systems: Shahab-3, Emad, Sejjil, Kheibar Shekan, Fattah-1, Khorramshahr-4, Haj Qasem, Qiam, Zolfaghar
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: US/Israel destroyed estimated 70% of mobile TELs. Production can rebuild but 18-24 month timeline for MRBM-class.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: Drone production is Iran's most resilient capability. Underground facilities at Isfahan, Kerman survived strikes. Cost: $20-50K per unit vs $3.7M interceptor.
Cruise missile production slower than drones. Range 700-1,350 km. Used sparingly — 15 total detected in UAE cumulative.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.systems: Fast attack craft (FAC), explosive USVs, midget submarines, mines, anti-ship missiles
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: IRGCN fast-craft fleet is 80-90% intact. USV attacks on tankers ongoing. Mine-laying in Hormuz confirmed. This is Iran's most intact conventional force.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.systems: Moudge-class frigate, Jamaran-class, Ghadir-class submarine, Fateh-class submarine
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: Regular navy bore heaviest losses. 60+ vessels sunk including all 3 Kilo-class submarines. Remaining capability is IRGCN asymmetric.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.systems: S-300PMU2, Bavar-373, Khordad-15, Khordad-3, Sayyad-2/3, Raad-series
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: Coalition SEAD/DEAD campaign destroyed ~85% of air defense network. Remaining systems relocated to protect Tehran, Isfahan.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.systems: F-14A Tomcat (limited), MiG-29, Su-24MK, F-4E Phantom, F-5E
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: Coalition air superiority achieved Day 1. Most IRIAF bases struck. 2 Su-24MK shot down by Qatar F-15QAs on Day 3. F-14As used only for Phoenix missile ISR role.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.systems: Quds Force (external ops), Basij (militia), conventional armor/artillery
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: Ground forces uncommitted. Quds Force coordinating proxy attacks (Hezbollah, Iraqi PMF). 465 Hezbollah waves launched against Israel.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.systems: Hezbollah (Lebanon), Iraqi PMF/Kata'ib Hezbollah, Houthis (Yemen), Palestinian factions
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.prewar_quantity: Hezbollah: ~150,000 rockets; PMF: ~100,000 fighters; Houthis: extensive drone/missile stock
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: 465 Hezbollah rocket/missile waves. Iraqi PMF attacks on US bases daily. Houthi Red Sea campaign continues. This is Iran's strategic depth.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: Ali Abdollahi announced doctrine change on March 22, 2026

Twin-launch Kheybar-Shekan MRBM deployed (first combat use). IRGC Navy: hundreds of small boats/USVs. China supplying weapons via Russia, Turkmenistan, Pakistan. Shahed factory in Russia.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: U.S. DOD claims 90% of weapons factories destroyed, including all Shahed drone plants and 80%+ of missile/nuclear industrial bases.

Gen. Mohammad Karami announced the deployment of a multi-layered security belt using decentralized tactics to remain unpredictable.
