IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.detail: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian publicly rebuked IRGC leadership over unauthorized UAE strikes (May 4-5), calling action 'utter madness' — first open daylight criticism of IRGC by an Iranian President since 2017. IRGC Khatam al-Anbiya joint command issued counter-statement denying UAE attacks, warned 'crushing response' if attacked. Supreme Leader's office silent at filing time. Indicates ARTESH/IRGC dual-track friction — material to negotiation calculus.
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.event: Internal Tehran command schism surfaces publicly

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: Maintaining deconfliction buffer for energy shipping; operational command remains severely degraded.
Alma Research Center estimates severe depletion of MRBM stockpiles due to sustained launch tempo.
Primary assembly plants damaged; reliance on dispersed, lower-yield caches.
Several units expended in recent wave. High interception rate by allied forces continues.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.branch: Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) / IRGC Navy
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: Continues to enforce controversial toll system on commercial vessels to assert sovereignty.
Production sites in Isfahan and Karaj remain under intermittent strike, limiting replenishment to makeshift assembly.
New attack drone claimed on Jun 6; usage heavily disputed by US CENTCOM.
Iranian lawmakers demanding authorization to develop long-range strategic weapons (ICBMs) capable of directly striking US territory.

IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.systems: Coastal ASCM batteries (Noor/Qader) & Fast Attack Craft
IRAN_MILITARY_ORDER.note: Persistent coalition airstrikes have decimated coastal launchers; surviving fast attack craft mostly restricted to hardened shelters.
First observed use since ceasefire ended.
Production sites actively targeted; remaining inventory relies on dispersed subterranean storage.

IRAN_CAPACITY.note: Production estimates 50-300/month. China assisting with solid fuel precursors. Launch rates dropped by 90%.
Production of offensive missiles exceeds 100 per month.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Strikes on Yazd Missile Base and industrial targets
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Combined forces targeted the deeply buried underground complex in Yazd, marking the 6th strike on the site. Additional strikes hit the Marine Industries Organization (MIO), Parchin Military Complex in Tehran, and the Iran Marine Industrial Company (SADRA) in Bushehr.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple strategic and industrial facilities successfully struck.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Strikes on Imam Ali and Dezful Missile Bases
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Heavy strikes on the Imam Ali Missile Base using bunker-buster munitions. A separate strike targeted bulldozers attempting to clear debris at the Dezful Missile Base.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 9 aboveground structures and 2 tunnel entrances destroyed at Imam Ali; heavy equipment destroyed at Dezful to deny tunnel access.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Extensive strikes on Imam Hussein University (IRGC's military academy) and ballistic missile/UAV production facilities in Tehran. A petrochemical plant in Tabriz was also struck.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Facilities degraded; widespread power outages in Tehran and Alborz provinces; IRGC Navy Commander and Tehran security commander eliminated.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: A new wave of U.S.-Israeli strikes hit northern and eastern Tehran.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: CENTCOM confirms 10,000+ targets struck; Tehran court/MoI destroyed
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM Adm. Cooper confirms US has struck 10,000+ targets. IDF hit Tehran Revolutionary Court, Ministry of Intelligence (10 sites), Quds Force HQ. Strikes reported in 26 of 31 Iranian provinces. UK hosting Hormuz diplomatic conference.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 10,000+ total US targets; IDF struck thousands more. Tehran institutions systematically degraded.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Extensive wave of attacks targeted regime sites in Tehran, including areas near the former U.S. Embassy compound. CENTCOM released an operational update confirming over 12,300 targets struck since the campaign began.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple targets successfully engaged; operations continue.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Campaign enters its second month, maintaining unrelenting firepower with B-2 stealth bombers and F-35s targeting IRGC headquarters, missile depots, and air defense systems.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: IRGC headquarters, missile depots, air defense systems
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Ongoing degradation of Iranian defense industrial capacity.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM reported over 12,300 targets hit and 155 Iranian vessels destroyed since start of campaign. Ultimatum issued to reopen Strait of Hormuz by April 6.
Extensive wave of strikes conducted by IAF against regime sites and the former U.S. Embassy compound.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: US-Israel strikes continue. Trump: US to hit Iran 'extremely hard' for 2-3 weeks. IDF planning 3 more weeks of operations to degrade Iran defense industry. B1 bridge in Karaj struck twice (2 civilian KIA). Iran steel plants remain offline (6-12mo recovery). Pasteur Institute extensively damaged.

US-Israel strikes continue. Trump: US to hit Iran extremely hard for 2-3 weeks. IDF planning 3 more weeks of operations to degrade Iran defense industry. B1 bridge in Karaj struck twice (2 civilian KIA). Iran steel plants remain offline (6-12mo recovery). Pasteur Institute extensively damaged.
U.S. strike on Isfahan nuclear facility
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. strikes targeted a major nuclear facility in Isfahan, resulting in a 'massive fireball' visible from the city.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Massive fireball observed, facility heavily damaged

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Ongoing air campaign against hardened targets
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: F-35s, F-22s, and B-2 Spirits continue to target IRGC facilities and hardened nuclear sites such as Fordow and Natanz using GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators.
U.S. forces conducted a major airstrike on the B1 (Bileghan) Bridge, a vital military supply route connecting Tehran to Karaj.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Bridge struck; Iranian state media reported at least 8 deaths and 95 injuries.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Coalition airstrikes on IRGC-N coastal defense cruise missile sites
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US Navy and allied aircraft struck multiple hardened bunkers suspected of housing anti-ship missiles to degrade threat to maritime shipping.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple secondary explosions observed; local coastal defense capabilities significantly degraded.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Stealth bomber strike on subterranean drone assembly plant
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: USAF stealth bombers deployed precision bunker-buster munitions against a newly identified underground UAV production facility.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Complete destruction of the underground assembly lines; significant structural collapse confirmed via satellite imagery.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Destruction of an IRGC Ground Forces base and a mobile command center in Tehran. CENTCOM deployed EA-37B electronic warfare aircraft to dismantle Iranian command-and-control capabilities.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.opName: Operation Roaring Lion / Operation Epic Fury

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: President briefed on a complex plan to insert ground forces into Iran specifically to secure and remove nuclear materials. Conflict enters an 'operational transition phase' for a final push.
CENTCOM forces engaged and destroyed three mobile anti-ship missile launchers in coastal areas.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Three launchers destroyed, averting imminent threat.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition aircraft struck multiple command and control nodes to disrupt air defense coordination.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Significant disruption of localized air defense network.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: U.S. forces strike Isfahan nuclear site
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. forces targeted Isfahan, home to one of Iran's primary nuclear sites, as part of the campaign's final stages.
Extensive waves of strikes across Iran including the destruction of the B1 (Bileghan) Bridge connecting Tehran to Karaj to disrupt missile transport.
Elimination of Makram Atimi, commander of a central ballistic missile unit in Kermanshah, and several battalion commanders.
Airstrike hit near the Bushehr nuclear facility, killing a security guard and damaging a support building.
Israel expanded operations by striking Hezbollah infrastructure in Beirut, including the destruction of a bridge in eastern Lebanon.
B-52 bombers conducted long-range precision strikes focusing on destroying Iran's remaining naval and missile production assets.
Three U.S. aircraft (F-15E, A-10, MQ-9) lost to hostile ground fire over Iran and Iraq. A Combat Search and Rescue operation recovered one F-15E crew member; the WSO remains missing.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Loss of 3 aircraft; 1 crew member missing in action

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iran claimed to have downed an American C-130 transport plane and two Black Hawk helicopters in southern Isfahan during a U.S. rescue mission.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Claimed loss of U.S. C-130 and two Black Hawks.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. intelligence confirmed the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in a targeted strike on his compound during the operation's initial phases.
Pentagon added Operation Epic Fury to its casualty database, reporting 13 U.S. service members killed and 365 wounded.
Multiple U.S. aircraft (F-15E, A-10, MQ-9) lost or damaged due to hostile fire from Iraqi-based ground forces.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Loss of F-15E, A-10, and MQ-9 aircraft.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Precision strike on IRGC UAV assembly facility
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US and coalition forces conducted precision airstrikes against an IRGC drone assembly plant to degrade regional threat capabilities.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Facility rendered inoperable, destroying multiple UAVs in production.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Final strikes on Iranian oil facilities
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Final US/coalition strikes reported on oil facilities on Lavan and Siri islands on the day the ceasefire was implemented.
The Lavan Oil Refinery on Lavan Island, Iran, was struck causing a major fire, occurring concurrently with the announcement of a fragile ceasefire.
A temporary two-week ceasefire, brokered by Pakistan, officially took effect at 2:02 a.m. Tehran time. Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Ceasefire implemented; commercial vessels resume transiting Strait of Hormuz.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: The ceasefire holds despite high tensions. Diplomatic teams prepare for negotiations in Islamabad scheduled for April 10.
The United States, Israel, and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire. Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and enter negotiations regarding its nuclear program.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Hostilities paused; Strait of Hormuz reopened.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper announced the conclusion of the operation, stating that core strategic objectives were achieved and Iran's projection capabilities neutralized.
The ceasefire remains in place. U.S. forces maintain an air defense umbrella alongside regional partners while broader peace negotiations continue.

President Trump and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth declared 'total and complete victory', stating the campaign shattered Iran's ballistic missile capabilities and air defenses. Trump warned of renewed strikes if Iran fails to comply with terms.
Vice President JD Vance travels to Islamabad, Pakistan to begin formal negotiations aimed at a permanent peace accord. Iran's President Pezeshkian warns 'hands remain on the trigger'.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Peace Talks Collapse & Naval Blockade Ordered
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Islamabad peace talks officially collapsed after 21 hours. President Trump ordered a full naval blockade of all Iranian ports.
A full naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Arabian Gulf and Gulf of Oman went into effect at 10:00 AM ET. Major U.S. airstrikes in Iran paused.
The IDF launched fresh strikes against Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon.
A U.S.-led naval blockade of all Iranian ports went into effect at 5:30 p.m. Iranian time to stop oil exports and imports, enforced by CENTCOM from the Gulf of Oman.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Blockade active; large-scale airstrikes paused due to a strained two-week ceasefire.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Operation Epic Fury transitions from active bombardment to a strategic maritime blockade. CENTCOM begins enforcing a full blockade of all Iranian ports and coastal areas to prevent maritime traffic and oil exports.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Blockade active; major airstrikes remain paused under fragile ceasefire.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. officially launched 'Operation Economic Fury', an escalation of financial sanctions accompanied by a naval blockade in the Persian Gulf.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Strict control of maritime traffic in the Persian Gulf established; ceasefire holds.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Potential nuclear agreement and regional truce
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: President announced U.S. is very close to a deal with Iran to hand over its enriched uranium. A 10-day humanitarian truce was also brokered between Israel and Lebanon.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Pending nuclear agreement; 10-day Israel-Lebanon truce initiated.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US and coalition forces conducted targeted strikes against newly identified air defense batteries and drone launch sites.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Precision strike on IRGC missile facilities
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition forces conducted precision airstrikes targeting underground ballistic missile storage and launch sites in western Iran.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple secondary explosions confirmed; facility rendered non-operational.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: ISR tempo holds; no new kinetic strikes Day 53
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM J3 reports no Epic Fury kinetic strikes during ceasefire Day 14. ISR tempo remains elevated: 24h on-station for RC-135, MQ-9, and P-8A packages. Coverage concentration on Kermanshah, Yazd, and Bandar Abbas. Target folders kept warm pending ceasefire expiration (Apr 22).

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Coalition airstrikes on drone launch facilities
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US and coalition forces conducted precision strikes against unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) storage and launch sites.
U.S. announced an indefinite extension of the temporary ceasefire following diplomatic intervention by Pakistan.
IRGC seized container ships MSC Francesca and Epaminondas, and attacked the Euphoria in response to U.S. blockade.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 2 ships seized, 1 attacked; diplomatic talks stalled

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM reported that coalition forces have directed a total of 31 vessels to turn around or return to port to prevent export of Iranian oil and import of military supplies.
U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) reported destroying six Iranian small boats that attempted to interfere with commercial transit.
US and coalition forces resumed strike operations following the conclusion of the D49-D65 ceasefire period.
Targeted strikes against Iranian UAV launch facilities to degrade offensive capabilities.
Near-miss UAV/missile event in Salalah; 2 foreign nationals wounded by debris. Oman MoFA: 'most likely off-axis Iranian munition.' Under investigation.
Sorties focused on air defense degradation.
Combined air and naval strikes targeting logistics networks supporting Iranian coastal operations.
F-15E strike eagles targeted early warning radar facilities to open an air corridor.
Tomahawk cruise missiles launched from naval assets hit IRGC fast attack craft pens.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Heavy damage to piers and docked vessels

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Precision standoff munitions struck unmanned aerial vehicle assembly lines.
CENTCOM announces strikes on coastal air defense sites.
Pentagon briefing details precision strikes against underground facilities.
Precision strike on identified missile assembly facility.
Interception of multiple hostile UAVs over the Persian Gulf.
Suppression of coastal air defense radar systems.
U.S. Central Command forces struck a ground control station and downed several Iranian attack drones after Iranian forces fired on commercial vessels.
IRGC targeted an American airbase in Kuwait with missiles and drones. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted several of these threats.
U.S. struck a ground control station in the southern port of Bandar Abbas following Iranian drone attacks on commercial ships.
Iran launched a ballistic missile and drone attack targeting a U.S. airbase in Kuwait; intercepted by Kuwaiti air defenses.
Coalition aircraft conducted a precision strike on an IRGC UAV storage and assembly facility.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Target destroyed; multiple secondary explosions observed.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US Navy guided-missile destroyer intercepted an anti-ship ballistic missile launched toward the Persian Gulf.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Intercept successful; no damage to coalition assets or commercial traffic.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition forces targeted early warning radar installations and surface-to-air missile batteries.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Radar systems disabled; SAM battery sustained heavy damage.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Airstrikes on coastal defense cruise missile (CDCM) sites
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition forces launched coordinated airstrikes neutralizing IRGC coastal defense cruise missile sites near the Strait of Hormuz.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: UAV intercepts and retaliatory strike on launch facility
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US Navy F/A-18s intercepted three Iranian UAVs targeting coalition vessels. Follow-on strikes hit a drone launch facility in Chabahar.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: Chabahar UAV Launch Facility, 3x Shahed-136 UAVs

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Strategic bomber strikes on ballistic missile storage
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: MoD reports strategic bomber strikes targeting underground ballistic missile storage bunkers near Kermanshah.

U.S. aircraft fired a Hellfire missile into the engine room of the Gambia-flagged cargo vessel M/V Lian Star in the Gulf of Oman after it ignored warnings and attempted to violate the naval blockade of Iranian ports.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Targeted strike on M/V Lian Star
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: A U.S. aircraft fired a single Hellfire missile into the engine room of the Gambia-flagged cargo vessel M/V Lian Star in the Gulf of Oman after the ship ignored more than 20 warnings and attempted to breach the blockade toward an Iranian port.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Vessel disabled and left adrift in international waters

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM forces operating in the Gulf of Oman disabled a Gambia-flagged vessel attempting to reach an Iranian port. A U.S. aircraft fired a Hellfire missile into the ship's engine room after issuing warnings.
An Iranian drone struck a Kuwaiti military base in retaliation. Several Americans suffered minor injuries from falling debris.
Coalition forces targeted underground missile storage facilities.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Facility heavily damaged, secondary explosions confirmed

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Air defense systems intercepted a barrage of hostile UAVs.
Precision strikes on coastal defense cruise missile launch pads.
Suppression of Enemy Air Defenses (SEAD) operation against radar sites.
Iran shoots down an American MQ-1 drone. IRGC claims it violated airspace, while CENTCOM maintains it was in international airspace.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: U.S. retaliatory strikes on Goruk and Qeshm Island
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. fighter aircraft target radar installations and drone command-and-control sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island in retaliation for the downed MQ-1.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Air defenses, ground control station, and two attack drones eliminated

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Fighter jets heard over Tehran, and three large explosions reported in the port city of Bandar Abbas.
IRGC claims to have targeted a U.S. airbase in retaliation for the strikes on Goruk and Qeshm Island.
Kuwait reports its air defense systems actively intercepted hostile missile and drone attacks over its territory.
CENTCOM reported intercepting two Iranian ballistic missiles targeting American forces in Kuwait. No casualties were reported.
CENTCOM reported precision airstrikes on IRGC missile storage facilities in Bandar Abbas.
MoD reports coalition air defense intercepted hostile UAVs over the Gulf.
Iran shot down a U.S. surveillance drone over the Persian Gulf, severely straining the ceasefire.
U.S. forces launched targeted airstrikes against Iranian radar, air defense, and drone command sites along the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran retaliated by firing missiles at the U.S.-hosted Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait.
IRGC launched a coordinated barrage of missiles and drones targeting the U.S. Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain and military installations in Kuwait.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Attacks largely thwarted; intercepted or fell short

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. forces downed several Iranian drones targeting civilian shipping and launched retaliatory strikes on military infrastructure on Qeshm Island.
IRGC claimed its navy targeted the vessel Panaya with missiles in retaliation for a prior U.S. strike on an Iranian tanker.
IRGC launched two ballistic missiles toward Kuwait; both fell short or broke apart en route.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Missiles fell short or broke apart

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: IRGC launched three ballistic missiles targeting Bahrain; successfully intercepted by U.S. and Bahraini air defense forces.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Iranian Drone Attack on Merchant Vessels
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: IRGC launched three one-way attack drones targeting civilian merchant vessels; shot down by CENTCOM forces.
A U.S. military aircraft fired a Hellfire missile into the engine room of the Botswana-flagged oil tanker M/T Lexie after it ignored warnings to halt transit.
CENTCOM conducted self-defense strikes destroying an IRGC ground control station and a communications tower on Qeshm Island.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: IRGC ground control station and communications tower

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Iranian Missile and Drone Attack Claims
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: IRGC claimed retaliatory strikes against U.S. Fifth Fleet HQ in Bahrain, a U.S. airbase, and the merchant vessel Panaya. CENTCOM denied any targets were hit.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: U.S. Fifth Fleet HQ, U.S. airbase, merchant vessel Panaya
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: No intended targets hit; no U.S. casualties

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: U.S. enforces naval blockade against oil tanker
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: A U.S. military aircraft fired an AGM-114 Hellfire missile into the engine room of the unladen, Botswana-flagged oil tanker M/T Lexie after it ignored warnings while attempting to run the blockade to Kharg Island.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Iranian drone attack on civilian shipping
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iran launched three one-way attack drones targeting civilian shipping transiting the Persian Gulf in response to the tanker strike.
targets: Civilian shipping in the Persian Gulf
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: All three drones were shot down by U.S. forces

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. Central Command launched self-defense airstrikes targeting an Iranian military ground control station and an IRGC communications tower on Qeshm Island near the Strait of Hormuz.

The IRGC launched a coordinated wave of ballistic missiles and drones targeting the U.S. Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain and Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait in retaliation for Qeshm Island strikes.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Attacks failed to hit U.S. military targets; intercepted by air defenses or fell short

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Iranian drone and missile strike on Kuwait Airport
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iran launched 35 missiles and drones across the region overnight. Projectiles struck Terminal 1 of Kuwait International Airport, causing severe structural damage and casualties. Kuwait suspended all flight operations.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Severe structural damage, 1 killed, 63 injured; flight operations suspended

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition aircraft executed suppression strikes against coastal air defense networks to secure airspace corridors.
Precision standoff weapons launched from US Navy vessels targeting UAV manufacturing logistics.
Long-range bombers targeted hardened subterranean missile storage facilities following observed launch preparations.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple secondary explosions confirmed facility damage.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Operation End Declaration & Strategic BDA Presentation
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. Secretary of State declared 'Epic Fury is over' before the House Foreign Affairs Committee, outlining the extensive destruction of Iran's defense industrial base and military assets.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Strategic victory declared; U.S. posture shifted to defensive operations

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: IRGC Retaliatory Strikes on Kuwait and Bahrain
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: IRGC launched ballistic missiles and drones targeting U.S. and allied interests. An Iranian drone struck Kuwait International Airport, while U.S./Bahraini defenses intercepted three missiles targeting Bahrain.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Kuwait: 1 KIA, 63 WIA. Bahrain: 3 missiles successfully intercepted.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Widespread explosions and active air defense fire were reported across multiple Iranian cities, including Tehran (Hakimiyeh, District 14), Karaj, Robat Karim, Urmia, and Bojnourd.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Explosions observed; specific targets and battle damage unknown

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Inspectors General for DoD, State, and USAID commenced legally mandated, whole-of-government oversight coordination for Operation Epic Fury as it exceeded 60 days.
Coalition aircraft executed precision strikes targeting reconstituted IRGC missile storage facilities and underground command nodes.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Facilities destroyed; extensive secondary explosions observed.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Allied naval air defense systems intercepted a coordinated barrage of 15 attack drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 15 of 15 drones successfully intercepted. No coalition casualties or damage.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio formally declared the offensive kinetic phase of Operation Epic Fury over, transitioning the operation to a blockade phase.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Sustained U.S. strikes ended; transition to naval blockade and containment

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iranian forces launched a retaliatory drone and missile strike targeting Kuwait International Airport.
Overnight Iranian drone strikes were reported targeting Bahrain.
The Department of War Office of Inspector General announced a whole-of-government review of Operation Epic Fury as it exceeded 60 days.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: CENTCOM forces conducted targeted self-defense strikes on an Iranian military ground control station and an IRGC communications tower on Qeshm Island.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Strikes occurred overnight following Iranian missile and drone attacks.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: CENTCOM air defenses successfully intercepted a second wave of Iranian kamikaze drones targeting U.S. forces in Kuwait.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Intercept occurred in the early hours of June 3. No U.S. personnel or assets were harmed.
Coalition F/A-18s conducted precision strikes against active anti-ship missile batteries posing a threat to maritime traffic.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 3 mobile launchers and associated radar systems destroyed.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US naval assets intercepted 4 one-way attack drones over the Persian Gulf. Follow-on strikes neutralized the origin launch facility.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 4 UAVs downed; launch facility command node severely damaged.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Targeted standoff strikes executed against integrated air defense radars to maintain operational air supremacy.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Primary radar array and power generators destroyed.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. forces conducted a maritime interdiction and right-of-visit boarding of the sanctioned stateless oil tanker MT Davina in the Indian Ocean.
Iran claimed it targeted a U.S. Navy destroyer in the Gulf of Oman, which CENTCOM officially denied as a lie, confirming U.S. assets operate unimpeded.
CENTCOM forces intercepted and shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: U.S. Airstrikes on Coastal Radar Sites
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. forces conducted self-defense airstrikes targeting Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites in Goruk and on Qeshm Island in response to drone launches.
CENTCOM coordinated airstrikes targeting newly deployed surface-to-air missile systems along the southern coast.
Coalition naval assets successfully intercepted multiple one-way attack drones identified as Shahed-136 variants in the Strait of Hormuz.
CENTCOM shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz.
U.S. forces launched targeted airstrikes against Iranian coastal surveillance radar installations along the Strait of Hormuz.
targets: Coastal surveillance radar on Qeshm Island

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: IRGC launched seven ballistic missiles targeting U.S. allies Kuwait and Bahrain in retaliation for radar strikes.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 6 missiles intercepted, 1 fell short; minor debris damage

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. forces conducted defensive precision airstrikes targeting Iranian coastal surveillance radar installations on the mainland at Goruk and on Qeshm Island.
U.S. Indo-Pacific Command seized the MT Dvina, a sanctioned supertanker belonging to Iran's Ghost Fleet illegally smuggling oil.
IRGC launched seven ballistic missiles targeting U.S. military bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. CENTCOM intercepted six; the seventh failed to reach its target. No U.S. casualties.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 6 missiles intercepted, 1 failed to reach target

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: The United States is considering making frozen Iranian assets available to Gulf allies to repair infrastructure damage caused by recent attacks.
CENTCOM forces shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz.
U.S. forces launched self-defense strikes against Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites in response to drone threats.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: Radar sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: IRGC fired seven ballistic missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain. Six were intercepted by allied air defenses; one struck Kuwait International Airport.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: Kuwait and Bahrain (U.S. 5th Fleet HQ targeted)
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 6 intercepted; 1 impacted Kuwait causing casualties and damage

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. forces shot down two additional Iranian one-way attack drones operating in the Strait of Hormuz.
CENTCOM forces intercepted and shot down an Iranian one-way attack drone threatening international shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz.
CENTCOM forces intercepted and shot down an additional Iranian one-way attack drone threatening international shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz.
Targeted ballistic missile launch sites following resumed hostilities.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Sites heavily damaged, launch capabilities degraded.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition forces intercepted a swarm of one-way attack UAVs.
Precision strikes on coastal defense command nodes.
IDF conducted airstrikes targeting suspected Hezbollah command centers in Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahiyeh).
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Target struck; acted as the catalyst for an Iranian retaliatory missile barrage.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iran launched a barrage of ballistic missiles toward Israel in direct retaliation for the Beirut strikes.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Missiles intercepted by IDF and regional air defense systems; no immediate casualties reported.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Defying diplomatic pressure, the Israeli Air Force launched retaliatory airstrikes targeting military installations in western and central Iran.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: Military installations in Tehran, Tabriz, Isfahan, and Karaj
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Explosions reported in major Iranian cities; Tehran airspace temporarily closed.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Yemen's Houthis launched a missile toward Israel in alignment with Iran.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Israeli forces worked to intercept the incoming missile.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: U.S. Retaliatory Strike on Radar Installations
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. forces retaliated against Iranian coastal radar installations following an Iranian drone strike on Kuwait International Airport.
U.S. Navy destroyers (USS Gonzalez, USS Nitze, and USS Mason) intercepted and cleared a swarm of 100 Iranian fast attack craft launched from six ports.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: All 100 contacts cleared; zero U.S. casualties or hull damage

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM announces resumption of precision strikes targeting air defense and drone manufacturing capabilities following the end of the ceasefire.
Targeted strike on ballistic missile storage facility.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Secondary explosions observed; significant degradation of site

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition forces intercepted a barrage of incoming one-way attack drones.
CENTCOM confirmed the resumption of coalition airstrikes targeting integrated air defense systems and early warning radar nodes following the end of the temporary ceasefire.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Successful suppression of targeted air defense nodes

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Coastal Missile Intercepts and Retaliatory Strikes
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition naval forces intercepted anti-ship cruise missiles and conducted immediate retaliatory strikes against the mobile launch sites along the coastline.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Threats neutralized; multiple mobile launchers destroyed

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM forces conducted coordinated strikes against IRGC coastal defense systems and missile launch sites.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple anti-ship missile launchers and radar arrays destroyed

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: UAV Interception over Strait of Hormuz
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition destroyers intercepted and destroyed three one-way attack drones launched toward international shipping lanes.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 3 drones destroyed; no damage to coalition vessels

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Ministry of Defence intelligence report published showing post-strike satellite imagery of facilities targeted on Day 101.

CENTCOM executed retaliatory airstrikes against nearly 20 military targets in Iran, aiming to degrade maritime monitoring and air defense capabilities following the downing of a U.S. Apache helicopter.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Successful precision strikes on air defense systems and radar sites. Zero U.S. casualties.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: The IRGC launched Operation True Promise IV, firing missiles and drones at U.S. military installations in Bahrain, Jordan, and Kuwait. Additionally, the IRGC shot down a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone.
targets: U.S. Navy Fifth Fleet HQ (Manama, Bahrain)
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Incoming Iranian missiles and drones successfully intercepted by U.S. air defenses; no casualties or significant damage. MQ-9 Reaper drone shot down.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. Air Force and Navy fighter jets targeted nearly 20 Iranian sites following the downing of a U.S. Apache helicopter.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: Iranian air defenses, ground control stations, and radar installations

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iran launched retaliatory drone and missile attacks putting the regional ceasefire on hold.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: U.S. and allied military installations in Jordan, Bahrain, and Kuwait

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: U.S. Apache downed over Strait of Hormuz
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: A U.S. Army AH-64 Apache was shot down by Iranian forces during a patrol. Both pilots survived and were rescued. Triggered immediate retaliatory U.S. strikes.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Aircraft destroyed; pilots rescued. Fragile ceasefire officially collapsed.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM launched three waves of self-defense strikes using Air Force and Navy fighters in response to the helicopter downing.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple Iranian radar and air defense sites struck near the Strait of Hormuz.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. F-15s, Navy assets, and Marines fired precision munitions at Iranian surveillance and communication networks. Widespread explosions reported.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Heavy damage inflicted on targets in Bandar Abbas, Minab, Jask, Sirik, Qeshm Island, and western Tehran.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iran retaliated by launching ballistic missiles and drones at 18 U.S. military sites across the Middle East, including a barrage of 12 missiles at Muwaffaq Salti Air Base.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple U.S. and coalition bases targeted; 18 sites engaged in total.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: U.S. announces decisive phase and ultimatum
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Defense officials announced a decisive 'negotiating with bombs' phase. Iranian officials requested a halt to bombing; U.S. issued an ultimatum demanding immediate surrender of the nuclear program.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Temporary pause in strikes while ultimatum is evaluated; heavy bombing threatened for Day 105 if peace deal isn't signed.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: IRGC threatened to fire on any transiting vessel. CENTCOM denied the strait was closed, affirming commercial transit continues.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Severe maritime tension; global oil prices surge past $115 per barrel.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iran launched a massive wave of retaliatory missiles and drones targeting U.S. bases across the region. Jordanian air defenses successfully intercepted 20 Iranian missiles aimed at the U.S. command center in the Azraq area.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple missiles intercepted by Jordanian air defenses; Kuwait temporarily closed its airspace.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: U.S. Strikes Cancelled and Ceasefire Framework
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: President Trump threatened severe strikes on Iran's Kharg Island and energy infrastructure, but abruptly cancelled the planned operations. Qatar-mediated talks produced an agreement in principle for a 60-day ceasefire.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Planned U.S. strikes cancelled; 60-day ceasefire agreement in principle reached.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Strait of Hormuz Blockade and Merchant Ship Attacks
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Despite diplomatic progress, Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya military command declared the Strait of Hormuz completely closed to all vessels. Merchant ships MV Jalveer and Settebello were attacked in the Gulf of Oman, resulting in civilian casualties.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Strait of Hormuz blockaded; civilian seafarers killed, prompting diplomatic protests.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US forces fired at least 49 Tomahawk cruise missiles and precision-guided munitions at Iranian targets in response to the downing of a US Apache helicopter, expanding the target list deeper into Iranian territory.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Significant strikes on Iranian military infrastructure; Iran responded by declaring the Strait of Hormuz closed.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM forces disabled the Guinea-Bissau-flagged oil tanker M/T Jalveer in the Gulf of Oman after it repeatedly ignored US directives.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Tanker disabled; CENTCOM reaffirmed control over the Strait of Hormuz, successfully escorting over 200 commercial ships.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: IRGC launched a coordinated barrage of 52 ballistic missiles and drones targeting US military installations across three neighboring countries.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 24 drones neutralized in Kuwait; 20 ballistic missiles intercepted in Jordan. No casualties or major damages reported.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. Marine Corps, Air Force, and Navy assets fired precision munitions, including Tomahawk missiles, against Iranian military targets in southern Iran following the shootdown of a U.S. AH-64 Apache.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Successful degradation of surveillance, communication, and air defense sites.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: IRGC launched retaliatory drone and missile strikes overnight targeting 18 U.S. military sites across the Gulf region.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: Ali Al-Salem Air Base, Ahmad Al-Jaber Air Base, Sheikh Isa Air Base, U.S. Fifth Fleet HQ
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Base damage unconfirmed; triggered high-alert posture.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iran launched 52 missiles and drones over a 48-hour period. Coalition partners engaged in widespread interceptions across the region.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: Azraq (Jordan), Kuwait airspace, Strait of Hormuz
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Jordan intercepted 20 ballistic missiles. Kuwait neutralized 24 drones. U.S. downed 2 OWA drones.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: A diplomatic breakthrough successfully halted the escalation, averting U.S. strikes on Iranian oil infrastructure.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Hostilities paused, Strait of Hormuz blockade ended, and initial $12 billion in Iranian funds unfrozen.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iran launched missile and drone attacks against U.S. and coalition bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan overnight.
targets: U.S. and coalition bases in Bahrain
U.S. and coalition bases in Kuwait
U.S. and coalition bases in Jordan
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Attacks executed; followed by U.S. strike preparations that were subsequently canceled.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. President abruptly canceled scheduled, imminent strikes against Iranian air defenses and communications, citing a pending 12-nation diplomatic settlement.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: U.S. strikes aborted; military remains on standby with naval blockade in place.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iranian officials publicly denied that a final agreement had been reached, contradicting U.S. announcements of a finalized diplomatic breakthrough.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Diplomatic uncertainty; active conflict phase status remains ambiguous despite halted strikes.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM forces successfully intercepted and downed multiple Iranian one-way attack drones attempting to strike commercial ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
President Trump called off further scheduled military strikes against Iran, announcing that a settlement to end the conflict was near.

EPICFURY_BDA.postStrikeAssessment: Struck for the sixth time since the operation began.
9 aboveground structures and 2 tunnel entrances destroyed.

EPICFURY_BDA.preStrikeStatus: Bulldozers clearing debris from previous strikes
EPICFURY_BDA.postStrikeAssessment: Equipment successfully destroyed, preventing access to collapsed tunnel launchers.
Struck, contributing to widespread regional power outages.
Struck by new wave of U.S.-Israeli airstrikes
Bombed and destroyed per IDF statement.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Diplomatic off-ramps are currently inaccessible, increasing the likelihood of protracted military engagements.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Muscat has paused mediation efforts, citing a lack of credible de-escalation commitments from Tehran after the Day 68 ceasefire violation.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Moderation in northern proxy deployment
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran seeks to preserve Lebanese Hezbollah's strategic deterrence capabilities rather than commit them to the current active operations phase.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Observed reduction in offensive posturing and cross-border shelling by Hezbollah units, despite the resumption of broad hostilities.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Nuclear enrichment facility lockdown
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Tehran is fortifying critical infrastructure against anticipated deep-penetration pre-emptive strikes by adversarial forces.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Movement of advanced centrifuge cascades deeper into the Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant and deployment of additional point-defense systems around Natanz.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Drone strike on Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant generator
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Demonstrates precision targeting of nuclear-adjacent infrastructure, elevating risk perception for regional actors.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: IISS reports a drone strike targeting the generator supplying electricity to the Bushehr plant.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: NPT Review Conference collapse
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Diplomatic off-ramps are closing as international consensus fails specifically over the Iran nuclear file.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Carnegie reports the NPT Review Conference ended without consensus due to irreconcilable differences over Iran.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Intensification of Israeli strikes in Lebanon
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Horizontal escalation creates a strategic dilemma for Tehran regarding the survivability of Hezbollah.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Crisis Group documents intensified strikes in Nabatieh from May 22-24.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: US political endorsement of anti-Hezbollah operations
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: US rhetoric supports expanding the theater of conflict to Lebanon, reducing restraint on allied operations.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: US Secretary of State affirms Israel's right to intensify operations against Hezbollah.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: US retaliatory strikes in southern Iran
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Continuation of active kinetic operations and maritime insurgency dynamics in the Strait of Hormuz.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: US conducts fresh strikes in southern Iran following drone attacks on commercial shipping.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: IRGC Aerospace Force decentralizes command structure for mobile missile units
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Indicates preparation for sustained, dispersed active operations to mitigate the impact of command-and-control degradation.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ISW reports shifts in Iranian command architecture allowing local commanders to initiate launches without direct central approval.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Resumption of Omani backchannel mediation focusing on target limitation
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Despite active phase operations, both sides are signaling a desire to contain the conflict by keeping strikes restricted to military targets.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Crisis Group notes diplomatic flurry via Muscat aimed at establishing red lines against civilian infrastructure strikes.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Resumption of Deep-Penetration Strikes
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran's decision to authorize deep-penetration ballistic strikes signifies an explicit departure from the D49-D65 ceasefire norms, aiming to reset deterrence at a higher escalation rung.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: MoD reports indicate successful intercepts of MRBMs targeting strategic bases, a significant shift from localized skirmishes.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.source: Institute for the Study of War (ISW)

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Diplomatic Backchannel Reactivation via Oman
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Quiet Omani mediation efforts suggest ongoing attempts to establish new red lines despite active military engagements.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Flight tracking and reporting note unscheduled Omani diplomatic flights to Tehran shortly after the resumption of strikes.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Naval Posturing in the Strait of Hormuz
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: IRGC-N exercises involving fast-attack craft swarms indicate preparation for asymmetric escalation if conventional strike exchanges intensify.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Satellite imagery confirms abnormal massing of IRGC-N assets near key maritime chokepoints.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Resumption of Deep Strikes
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran has shifted back to a high-intensity strike posture, attempting to overwhelm regional air defenses.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: MoD reports confirm 45+ ballistic missile intercepts over coalition airspace following the ceasefire collapse.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Hardened Negotiating Stance
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Despite the active conflict, backchannel signals indicate Iran is attempting to establish a new escalation baseline rather than entering total regional war.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Crisis Group analysis of intercepted diplomatic cables and ongoing but calibrated tactical strikes.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Attrition Warfare Transition
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iranian forces are prioritizing sustained, lower-tier drone swarms to systematically deplete coalition interceptor stockpiles.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: MoD situational updates record continuous waves of UAVs requiring Patriot and THAAD engagements.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: MoU Negotiation
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Tentative deal to extend ceasefire and lift blockades in the Strait of Hormuz.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Crisis Group reported a one-page MoU for a 60-day ceasefire has been largely negotiated.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Naval Operations in Strait
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Ongoing US-Iran military friction over control of the Strait of Hormuz.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: US Navy issued notice to challenge IRGC protection racket and force requirements for vessels.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: US Political Pressure
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Economic and political costs pushing US administration toward finalizing a deal.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Brookings analysis noted war is a liability ahead of midterms due to high gas prices.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Military Threat Posture
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: US threatening resumption of full attacks if negotiations fail.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Defense Secretary Hegseth stated at Shangri-La Dialogue that US is ready to restart attacks on Iran.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran asserts territorial control over Strait of Hormuz; US CENTCOM announces military operations north of Musandam Peninsula.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Direct military confrontation in the Strait of Hormuz indicates continued active hostilities and escalation of maritime domain risks.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ISW reports Iran demanding tolls and using force; CENTCOM responding with military operations.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran reconstitutes missile capacity at bases like Yazd.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran is actively rebuilding strike capabilities, signaling intent to sustain military operations despite severe industrial base damage.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ISW notes missile capacity reconstitution.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran's ballistic missile arsenal degraded by one-third to one-half; launchers reduced by half.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Significant material losses constrain Iran's ability to maintain high-volume missile strikes.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: FDD assesses severe depletion of missile inventory and launchers since the conflict began.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: 60-day MoU pending, deferring technical nuclear details.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Diplomatic efforts are advancing via a deal, but failure to resolve nuclear issues leaves long-term strategic risks unaddressed.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: International Crisis Group reports the deal initially favors Iran and delays HEU stockpile disposal.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Regime transitions to Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader, focusing on asymmetric extortion.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: New leadership is prioritizing aggressive regional tactics to project strength.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Carnegie Endowment notes succession dynamics and continued reliance on asymmetric strategies in the Gulf.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Deconfliction Channel Silence
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Absence of back-channel communications indicates a formalized shift to sustained active conflict with diminished diplomatic off-ramps following the ceasefire collapse.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Diplomatic sources report standard intermediary channels in Oman remain unutilized despite resumed kinetic exchanges.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Multi-Axis Strike Attrition Strategy
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Resumed kinetic operations emphasize high-volume drone and missile swarms intended to test and exhaust coalition interceptor stockpiles.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: MoD reports confirm renewed, coordinated strike packages targeting distributed regional military infrastructure.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Resumption of mutual kinetic strikes
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Return to active operations signifies the collapse of recent ceasefire frameworks and a renewed commitment to military pressure to achieve strategic objectives.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: MoD reports confirm widespread active intercepts and airstrikes resuming across the theater.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Suspension of backchannel mediation
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Strategic communication channels are closing, raising the threshold for future diplomatic off-ramps and increasing the risk of miscalculation.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Diplomatic sources note that the Oman-brokered backchannel dialogue has been paused indefinitely following the shift back to active conflict.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Missile launcher dispersal and hardening
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran's resumption of hostilities is accompanied by dispersed launcher formations to prevent pre-emptive strikes, signaling preparation for a sustained campaign rather than a single retaliatory volley.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Commercial satellite imagery showing widespread dispersal of solid-fuel SRBMs from central depots to hardened underground shelters in the Zagros mountains.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Suspension of backchannel proximity talks
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: The collapse of Omani-mediated backchannels indicates both sides have abandoned immediate de-escalation off-ramps, preferring to establish new deterrence baselines through direct kinetic action.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Omani foreign ministry officials confirmed the indefinite suspension of proximity talks in Muscat following the resumption of active strikes.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Strategic withholding of proxy network assets
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Despite the resumption of direct state-on-state strikes, Iran is deliberately withholding its highest-tier regional proxies from full-scale escalation, attempting to contain the conflict geographically and avoid triggering overwhelming secondary responses.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Absence of coordinated, multi-front swarm attacks in the first 48 hours of resumed operations; observed proxy actions remain strictly localized to existing operational tempos.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: IRGC-Quds Force shifts command structure, deploying senior advisors to frontline proxy units
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Indicates preparation for sustained, coordinated multi-front attacks to test coalition defenses.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ISW strategic assessment citing intercepted communications and structural command shifts in Iraq and Syria.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Resumption of complex drone swarm and ballistic missile operations
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Confirms strategic decision by the Iranian military to re-escalate kinetic pressure following the ceasefire collapse.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: MoD press release confirming interception of 20+ coordinated UAVs and 3 medium-range ballistic missiles.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Divergent diplomatic signaling regarding nuclear facility oversight
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Suggests internal strategic divisions; the civilian government is attempting to preserve backchannel negotiation tracks despite active military escalation.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Chatham House briefing noting a softening of rhetoric from the Iranian Foreign Ministry relayed through Omani intermediaries.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Redeployment of advanced mobile air defenses to coastal regions
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran is rapidly hardening its southern flank to protect critical infrastructure against anticipated preemptive or retaliatory strikes following the resumption of active hostilities.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Commercial satellite imagery and regional defense reports detailing the movement of Bavar-373 and 3rd Khordad systems toward the Persian Gulf.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Shift toward attrition-focused strategic messaging
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iranian strategic communication is pivoting to normalize prolonged conflict, aiming to prepare the domestic populace for sustained economic and military pressure rather than promising decisive victories.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Broadcast statements by IRGC commanders emphasizing operational endurance and the exhaustion of adversary air-defense stockpiles.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Enhanced multi-front drone swarm coordination
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Tehran is attempting to overwhelm allied defensive systems by synchronizing launches with regional proxies, demonstrating an intent to deliberately broaden the conflict's geographic scope during the renewed active phase.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Intercepted command-and-control communications and synchronized UAV launch patterns reported by allied MoDs.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Lebanon Front Expansion
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Geographical expansion of the conflict into Lebanon.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Israeli forces captured Beaufort Castle north of the Litani River.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Negotiation Deadlock
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: US demands complicate and stall draft MoU peace negotiations.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Demand for immediate handover of Iran's highly enriched uranium (HEU) and permanent international management of the Strait of Hormuz.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Suspension of Peace Talks
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: The ceasefire is collapsing, with both sides preparing for a second wave of hostilities.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Iran officially suspended all peace talks in Islamabad protesting the Israeli offensive in Lebanon.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Missile Reconstitution
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran is leveraging the ceasefire to rebuild its degraded capabilities.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Reconstitution of missile arrays in hardened underground sites such as Yazd.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Nuclear Breakout Danger
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: A hardened, surviving Iranian state may accelerate its nuclear timeline.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Assessments suggest clandestine nuclear breakout could be pursued as a survival mechanism.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Regime Survival Prioritization
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran's leadership appears to be recalibrating, signaling through backchannels a desire to prevent full-scale conventional ground conflict to preserve internal stability.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Omani intermediaries report Iranian willingness to pause ballistic missile strikes on critical energy infrastructure if reciprocal guarantees are met.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Asymmetric Proxy Escalation
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: IRGC Quds Force is shifting strategy towards unacknowledged proxy sabotage to maintain deterrence without triggering direct coalition airstrikes.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Observed distribution of advanced drone components to Iraqi militia groups and localized GPS spoofing near the Strait of Hormuz.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Nuclear Threshold Rhetoric

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Supreme National Security Council statements threaten NPT withdrawal, yet parallel diplomatic notes to the IAEA offer continued camera access.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: IRGC rhetoric regarding regional infrastructure
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Statements from senior IRGC commanders suggest an expanded target set, potentially threatening regional energy infrastructure if direct strikes on Iranian territory continue.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Broadcast statements on Iranian state media emphasizing readiness to strike economic targets across the Gulf.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Relocation of mobile SAM batteries to western border regions
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Movement of mobile air defense systems indicates preparation for anticipated intercepts and an intention to contest airspace more aggressively in the active operational phase.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Satellite imagery analysis showing redeployment of SAM units from central garrisons to western frontier provinces.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Adaptation of Strike Packages
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran has altered its missile deployment strategy, launching smaller but more frequent salvos to exhaust air defense intercepts, signaling an intent for prolonged attrition rather than a single decisive blow.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Observed shift in operational tempo and smaller volley sizes coordinated with drone swarms over the past 48 hours.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Diplomatic Back-channel Assurances
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Tehran is attempting to compartmentalize the conflict by sending assurances to Gulf states through Omani backchannels that their energy infrastructure will not be targeted if they deny airspace to adversary forces.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Omani diplomatic flights to Tehran and subsequent statements from Muscat regarding regional neutrality.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: IRGC Rhetorical Shift on Conflict Duration
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: IRGC commanders have publicly framed the current phase of operations as a 'war of existential necessity,' indicating a high threshold for domestic economic pain and reluctance to accept near-term off-ramps.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Statements broadcasted on IRINN and affiliated Telegram channels by IRGC Quds Force leadership.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Suspension of U.S. Negotiations

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ISW-CTP reported that Major General Ahmad Vahidi's faction officially suspended direct and indirect negotiations with the U.S., using Israeli operations in Lebanon as a pretext.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Linkage of U.S. Ceasefire to Lebanon

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Monetization of Strait of Hormuz
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran is exploiting its control over the Strait of Hormuz to extract economic rents, reducing its incentive to compromise on its nuclear file and prolonging the conflict.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Geopolitical strategists report Iran is extracting $1M to $2M daily in cryptocurrency from commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Deterrence Threat Against Israeli Civilians
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran is conducting calculated information operations and issuing deterrence signals aimed at pressuring the U.S. into restraining Israeli military action in Lebanon.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Major General Ali Abdollahi Ali Abadi issued an implicit threat to target civilians in northern Israel if the IDF strikes Beirut.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.source: Institute for the Study of War (ISW) & Critical Threats Project (CTP)

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran halts indirect negotiations and fires ballistic missiles at US forces
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: The suspension of diplomatic channels combined with direct military targeting of US forces in Kuwait indicates a sharp resumption of active conflict dynamics and retaliatory operations.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Iran halted indirect negotiations in response to Israeli strikes in Lebanon; CENTCOM intercepted two Iranian ballistic missiles fired at American forces in Kuwait.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Deadlock in MOU negotiations and threats of resumed combat
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Hardline stances in negotiations, including rejected demands and public threats to resume combat operations, complicate de-escalation efforts and preserve high tensions.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Trump administration demanded strict timelines and Strait of Hormuz withdrawal on draft MOU; Iranian negotiator Ghalibaf warned of readiness to return to combat.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran opposes HEU transfer abroad
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Refusal to transfer highly enriched uranium signals an intent to maintain nuclear leverage, undermining potential war-ending agreements.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly opposes transferring HEU abroad due to vulnerability to military pressure.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Relocation of UAV Launch Sites
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Relocation of strategic assets away from vulnerable coastal regions indicates an intent to sustain active military operations while mitigating the impact of expected retaliatory strikes.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Satellite imagery tracking movement of mobile launchers and construction of hardened inland shelters.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Collapse of Oman Diplomatic Channel
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: The cessation of discrete diplomatic engagement via Muscat suggests both parties are deprioritizing political off-ramps in favor of establishing military deterrence.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Regional diplomatic sources confirm the Omani mediation team has indefinitely paused shuttle diplomacy flights.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Prolonged Carrier Strike Group Deployment
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Extended deployment of US naval assets signals preparation for a protracted operational phase rather than anticipating near-term de-escalation.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: CENTCOM public announcement extending the deployment of Carrier Strike Group assets in the CENTCOM Area of Responsibility.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: U.S. enforces blockade by disabling Iranian tanker and striking IRGC station on Qeshm Island
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: U.S. strict enforcement of the maritime blockade in the Strait of Hormuz is triggering direct kinetic retaliation and rendering a sustainable ceasefire highly unlikely.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: U.S. forces disabled an Iranian oil tanker's engine room and CENTCOM destroyed an IRGC ground control station on Qeshm Island.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: IRGC ballistic missile strike targets U.S. Navy Fifth Fleet HQ in Bahrain
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran is shifting from proxy warfare to direct state-level attacks on U.S. command nodes, demonstrating extreme brinkmanship to break the blockade.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: U.S. and Bahraini air defenses intercepted three ballistic missiles targeting the Fifth Fleet headquarters.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Collapse of U.S.-Iran indirect negotiations in Oman over HEU and maritime demands
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran's refusal to surrender its highly enriched uranium and its linkage of bilateral peace talks to the Lebanon conflict deepens the diplomatic impasse.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Iranian negotiators rejected U.S. demands to transfer HEU and suspended talks, conditioning future diplomacy on a halt to Israeli operations against Hezbollah.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: IRGC massive missile and drone strike on U.S. bases in Kuwait
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Direct kinetic action by Tehran's hardline leadership underscores the failure of the fragile ceasefire and risks rapid escalation into a full-scale regional war.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: IRGC launched 13 ballistic missiles and 17 drones at U.S. bases; intercepted debris damaged Kuwait International Airport Terminal 1, killing one civilian.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran attempts to institutionalize 'Smart Control' transit regime in the Strait of Hormuz
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran is exploiting the de facto ceasefire periods to coerce non-Iranian shipping into compliance, aiming to permanently normalize its sovereignty over the waterway.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: IRGC Navy and Persian Gulf Strait Authority are forcing commercial vessels to submit transit information, creating an illegal traffic separation scheme.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran suspends direct negotiations with the U.S.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Tehran is deflecting negotiations from nuclear enrichment to Lebanon, hardening its diplomatic stance.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ISW reports Iran suspended direct talks on June 1 in response to U.S. demands for HEU surrender and Hormuz control.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran institutionalizing control over Strait of Hormuz.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran is cementing its strategic maritime leverage, shifting focus to sovereignty claims.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ISW assesses Iran is using the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) to normalize control over the Strait.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Proxy missile and drone strikes on Gulf states.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran-backed Iraqi militias are targeting non-belligerent GCC states to pressure Washington.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ICG reports strikes on Fujairah Petroleum Zone (UAE), Kuwait International Airport, and U.S. Navy 5th Fleet in Bahrain.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: U.S. precision munitions depletion.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: U.S. strike options are severely constrained, reducing the likelihood of a major offensive campaign.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Brookings assesses the U.S. has depleted 25-50% of critical precision munitions, creating a strategic limitation.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iranian military reconstitution via 'mosaic model'.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran maintains a survivable second-strike capability despite previous airstrikes.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: IISS reports Iran's decentralized command and underground facilities allow for continued drone/missile production.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Strategic stalling and military reconstitution
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran is leveraging the diplomatic pause to stall negotiations, consolidate maritime control via the Persian Gulf Strait Authority, and rebuild drone/missile production.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ISW-CTP reports Iran refuses HEU concessions and seeks to force commercial vessels to request IRGC permission for Strait of Hormuz transit.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Drone and missile strike on Kuwait International Airport
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: A direct retaliatory strike against regional infrastructure indicates a willingness to escalate violently in response to US enforcement of the naval blockade.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: CENTCOM confirmed the deliberate strike, which killed 1 civilian and wounded 63, was retaliation for disabling the tanker M/T Lexie.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire framework
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Maintaining the northern front against Israel aligns with Iran's broader strategy to deflect US negotiation pressure away from the Strait of Hormuz.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Hezbollah rejected Litani River withdrawal terms; Iranian FM threatened full-scale war resumption if Beirut is struck.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Management of escalation thresholds in 'battle of wills'
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Both Washington and Tehran are balancing severe economic and military pressure while avoiding actions that would trigger an unmanageable full-scale regional war.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: MEI and RANE analysts assess the most likely path is an extended unstable ceasefire with functional closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran launched drone and missile attacks on US facilities in Kuwait and Bahrain following a US strike on an Iranian telecom tower on Qeshm Island.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: ICG assesses this escalation is a calculated show of force by Tehran to establish red lines and reinforce its bargaining position in ongoing negotiations.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Military exchange on June 3; ICG analysis.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iranian strategy focuses on preserving the status quo and solidifying control over the Strait of Hormuz via the Persian Gulf Strait Authority.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: ISW assesses Vahidi's faction views the current status quo as favorable, using the period to normalize sovereignty and maritime legitimacy.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ISW report on PGSA and strategic intent.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran is attempting to pivot negotiations away from its nuclear program and toward halting Israeli operations in Lebanon.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: ISW and ICG note Tehran calculates that protecting Hezbollah is vital to prevent degradation of its primary proxy and maintain regional deterrence.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Think tank analysis on negotiation linkage.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Pakistan is facilitating the exchange of drafts for an MOU to extend the ceasefire by 60 days.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: A diplomatic off-ramp remains possible through mediation, though structural gaps persist between the US and Iranian positions.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: FDD cyber intelligence report detailing anomalous scanning activities originating from known APT33 infrastructure targeting Saudi and Emirati grid nodes.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Movement of mobile TELs from hardened shelters to prepared launch sites
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Return to active phase indicates collapse of previous back-channel mediation; IRGC leadership signaling willingness to absorb potential retaliatory strikes to maintain deterrent posture.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Commercial satellite imagery showing dispersal of missile transport-erector-launchers.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Russian diplomatic delegation arrives in Tehran
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Moscow attempting to manage the scope of the escalation to prevent a complete closure of the Strait of Hormuz while maintaining its strategic partnership.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Public flight tracking data of state aircraft and statements from the Russian Foreign Ministry.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Redeployment of Bavar-373 and S-300 air defense batteries to coastal regions
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Preparation for anticipated retaliatory airstrikes; signals intent to engage in a prolonged kinetic exchange rather than backing down after initial salvos.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Open-source intelligence analysis of localized road closures and ground-level footage of military convoys moving toward the southern coast.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: House Foreign Affairs closed session on 60-day clock and Gulf force posture.
$200B request messaging; carrier sustainment and munitions restock.
$20bn Gulf marine war-risk backstop announced (public summary).

Congress considering Iran war supplemental funding package to replenish military stockpiles. Cost estimates range from $6.2B-$13.4B for Operation Epic Fury through March 19. Pentagon reportedly requesting $200B supplemental.

War Powers Resolution votes failed in House (212-219) and Senate. Democrats criticizing lack of clarity on war justification and objectives. Senator Blumenthal expressed dissatisfaction with classified briefings.

Pentagon $200B supplemental request faces stiff opposition from Democrats and some Republicans. Rep. Lauren Boebert declared 'I am a no on any war supplemental.' Sen. Chip Roy demanding game plan details.
Trump posts on Truth Social: 'we are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great military efforts in the Middle East.'
President Trump indicated war approaching completion: 'We are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts in the Middle East'
Treasury Sec. Bessent says US has 'plenty of money' to fund Iran war but rules out tax increases. Defends recent Iran/Russian oil sanctions relief.
Trump ultimatum: Iran must open Strait of Hormuz 'completely, without threat' or US will target power plants. Warned of massive oil field strikes if Iran threatens Qatar.

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt stated that the U.S. was 'ahead of schedule' in Operation Epic Fury, originally estimated to last four to six weeks, and was 'very close' to meeting its core objectives.
President Trump echoed Leavitt's statements, describing operations as 'extremely' ahead of schedule.

White House confirmed a 15-point action list had been sent to Tehran (reportedly via Pakistani mediators) for ending the conflict. Leavitt insisted talks were 'productive' and 'continuing' despite initial Iranian rejections.
White House Press Secretary Leavitt warned President Trump was prepared to 'unleash hell' if Iran refused a deal, stating the Iranian regime had been 'defeated militarily.'
President Trump announced a delay in threatened strikes on Iran’s energy infrastructure, extending the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to April 6, citing 'very well' negotiations.
During a cabinet meeting, President Trump mentioned taking control of Iran’s oil as an 'option,' comparing it to past U.S. strategy in Venezuela.
Pentagon weighed deploying up to 10,000 additional troops to the region to provide the President with more military options, adding to existing paratroopers and Marines.
President Trump announced a 10-day extension of the pause on striking Iranian energy infrastructure, moving the deadline to April 6, 2026.

Special Envoy Steve Witkoff confirmed that the U.S. presented Iran with a 15-point peace proposal via intermediaries, including demands for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and cease nuclear enrichment.
President Trump stated that taking control of Iran’s oil remains 'an option,' though he declined to elaborate, warning Iranian leaders to agree to terms 'before it is too late.'

Military and intelligence officials held a closed-door briefing for lawmakers, where Rep. Nancy Mace (R-SC) expressed 'grave concerns' about the lack of an exit strategy and opposed the deployment of ground forces.
President Trump claimed the U.S. military had 'decimated' 99% of Iranian capabilities and that the U.S. has 'already won the war.'
President Trump confirmed that negotiations are ongoing but insisted that Iran must 'open up' the Strait of Hormuz, jokingly suggesting renaming it the 'Strait of Trump.'

The administration continues to maintain that 'talks are going very well' on social media, while simultaneously preparing for potential military escalation if the April 6 deadline passes without a breakthrough.
