Defense Minister Katz publicly affirms support for US Project Freedom and reiterates Israel will respond 'decisively' to any Iranian or proxy strike.
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.source: IDF Spokesperson, Times of Israel, Haaretz

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF holds posture; FM Sa'ar welcomes Project Freedom pause with caveats
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IDF maintains elevated readiness but does not raise alert tier on May 6; no kinetic IDF activity in Iranian/Iraqi/Syrian airspace last 24h. FM Sa'ar statement: 'Israel supports any pause that translates into verifiable Iranian missile/UAV cessation. We assess Iranian intent based on action, not declarations.' PM Netanyahu absent from public statements pending coalition consultations. Hezbollah-IDF Lebanon line continues to trade limited fires per Day 67 pattern.
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.source: IDF Spokesperson, Times of Israel, Reuters

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Israeli Air Force conducts targeted strikes against suspected IRGC underground command bunkers in retaliation for the Oman ceasefire violation.
Arrow 3 defense systems successfully intercept a barrage of medium-range ballistic missiles targeting the Tel Aviv metropolitan area.

Israeli forces have crossed the Litani River and are operating deep within Lebanese territory to dismantle Hezbollah infrastructure. PM Netanyahu emphasized operations will persist until security is restored to the north.
The Institute for National Security Studies (INSS) published a summary noting tactical deterrence was restored, but the Iranian regime remained resilient under new leader Mojtaba Khamenei.
Israeli officials expressed concern that U.S.-led peace talks in Islamabad might exclude Israeli priorities, such as permanent restrictions on Iran's ballistic missile program.
Israel held a ceremony for the arrival of its first KC-46 Gideon refueling aircraft, a critical asset for long-range strike operations.
The IDF reported that approximately 2,500 Hezbollah fighters had been killed since the start of Operation Roaring Lion.
IAF executes precision strikes against alleged IRGC logistics nodes and forward command posts in eastern Syria under Operation Roaring Lion.
Israeli Shayetet 13 units reportedly conduct covert sabotage operations against arms smuggling vessels in the Red Sea corridor.
David's Sling batteries successfully intercept a coordinated salvo of medium-range ballistic missiles targeting the Haifa port industrial zone.
IDF spokesperson confirms the destruction of a subterranean precision-guided munition (PGM) factory linked to proxy forces.
IAF F-35I Adir aircraft execute deep penetration strikes against IRGC underground ballistic missile facilities near Isfahan.
Israeli integrated air defense systems successfully intercept a swarm of 12 incoming Shahed-type munitions launched from Syrian territory.
Coordinated precision strikes neutralize a Quds Force forward operating command node in western Iraq.
IAF executes precision strikes on hardened ballistic missile launch sites in western Iran, significantly degrading immediate launch capabilities.
Israeli multi-layered defense system neutralizes a coordinated barrage of 20+ loitering munitions targeting northern strategic infrastructure.
Under Operation Roaring Lion, Israeli F-35I Adir aircraft strike IRGC underground ballistic missile storage facilities in central Iran.
IAF conducts deep-penetration strikes targeting underground drone assembly facilities in western Iran and proxy supply lines in Syria.
IDF confirms the Arrow 3 air defense array successfully intercepted an incoming medium-range ballistic missile over the Red Sea.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IAF Precision Strikes under Operation Epic Fury
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Israeli Air Force executes synchronized strikes targeting subterranean drone assembly facilities and key ballistic missile launch infrastructure.
Deployment of additional armored and artillery units to the northern front to secure borders against localized incursions.

The IDF issued evacuation warnings for seven villages in southern Lebanon as Israeli forces pushed deeper to target Hezbollah infrastructure. Hezbollah reported carrying out over 20 operations against Israeli forces.
Iran's IRGC claimed to have shot down a U.S. MQ-1 drone that allegedly entered Iranian territorial waters, following similar claims regarding an MQ-9 earlier in the week.
Israeli F-35I Adir aircraft successfully struck subterranean command nodes and drone assembly facilities in western Iran.
Intense exchanges of fire reported along the Blue Line as IDF ground units advanced to dismantle Hezbollah anti-tank positions.
IAF executes a series of coordinated strikes targeting underground IRGC drone manufacturing facilities and logistics hubs in western Iran.
IDF engineering units continue to dismantle and clear residual Hezbollah infrastructure along the northern border, transitioning to long-term post-war security protocols.
Israeli military intelligence presents an updated assessment to the security cabinet regarding Iran's internal leadership transition and the status of its remaining degraded military capabilities.
Israeli Air Force maintains heightened readiness, conducting routine surveillance patrols over the Mediterranean and northern airspace to ensure operational deterrence.
Israeli forces conduct precision strikes targeting suspected IRGC drone and missile reconstitution efforts, maintaining active combat operations under Operation Roaring Lion.
Israel transitions toward political stabilization efforts amid ongoing high regional tensions; active large-scale airstrikes remain ceased.
IDF Southern Command announces tactical repositioning of artillery units to counter anticipated ballistic missile barrages under Operation Roaring Lion.
Israeli Air Force F-35I Adir squadrons execute deep-penetration strikes targeting subterranean IRGC drone assembly facilities and command nodes.
Israel intensified military operations in Lebanon against Hezbollah, bringing the reported number of Hezbollah fighters killed to over 2,500 since the war began.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: Operation Roaring Lion: Syrian Border Strikes
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IAF fighter jets conduct precision strikes targeting IRGC Quds Force logistics hubs and weapons depots near the Syria-Lebanon border.
Israeli Navy missile boats intercept an unflagged vessel in the southern Red Sea suspected of smuggling precision-guided munitions to proxy forces.
An Israeli drone strike in the Nabatieh area of southern Lebanon injured two Lebanese soldiers. Israeli Home Front Command updated defensive guidelines through Jun 7.
A scheduled Home Front Command siren test was conducted in Israel's Northern District.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: Operation Roaring Lion - Northern Sector Strikes
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IAF conducts targeted strikes on subterranean missile production facilities near the Lebanon border.
David's Sling battery successfully intercepts three mid-range ballistic projectiles fired from Syrian territory toward the Golan Heights.
IAF completes a series of precision deep strikes targeting subterranean UAV assembly facilities and mobile launcher depots near Isfahan.
Israeli Arrow 3 defense system successfully intercepts a medium-range ballistic missile exo-atmospherically over the Dead Sea region.
Operation Roaring Lion: IAF F-35I Adir aircraft execute targeted bunker-buster strikes on remaining IRGC ballistic missile facilities near Kermanshah.
IDF air defenses intercept a synchronized suicide drone swarm launched by Axis of Resistance militias from Syria; IAF retaliates against command nodes.
IAF strikes multiple suspected ballistic missile assembly sites in western Syria near Damascus; significant secondary explosions reported by regional observers.
David's Sling intercepts multiple medium-range projectiles launched from western Iraq targeting the Eilat port region. No casualties reported.
IAF executes deep penetration strikes against IRGC underground missile assembly sites near Shiraz.
David's Sling and Iron Dome systems successfully intercept multiple UAVs launched from Iraqi territory towards northern Israel.
Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon continued overnight following the collapse of the US-backed ceasefire proposal.
Multiple squadrons of F-35I Adir aircraft successfully strike subterranean UAV assembly plants.
IDF deploys additional Iron Dome batteries to the northern border in anticipation of proxy retaliation.
Israeli Air Force aircraft operating in northern Israel were targeted by surface-to-air missiles overnight, triggering rocket sirens across northern communities with no damage reported.
IAF strikes suspected IRGC UAV assembly facilities near Isfahan using precision-guided munitions launched from standoff range.
Israeli F-35I Adir aircraft conduct suppression of enemy air defenses (SEAD) missions in western Iran to clear operational corridors.
IAF F-35I stealth fighters execute deep-penetration strikes targeting underground ballistic missile storage and launch erectors near Kermanshah.
Shayetet 13 naval commandos intercept an unmarked cargo vessel in the southern Red Sea carrying advanced guidance kits destined for Iranian proxy forces.
Reports disclosed Israel deployed Mossad, elite helicopter rescue, and commando units to southern Azerbaijan, along with covert sites in Iraq, UAE, and Somaliland during the conflict.
IDF struck approximately 150 Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon and targeted a suspicious vehicle in Tebnit, later reviewed for potentially carrying Lebanese Armed Forces personnel.
A precise IDF airstrike in southern Gaza killed Hamas terror cell commander Muhanad Othman Yassin Farwana.
The IDF announced the deaths of Captain Shahar Gamla (Egoz Unit) and Sergeant Ohad Yaari during clashes against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.
The Israeli Home Front Command's defensive policy guidelines, active since June 2, expired at 20:00.
IDF executes continued reciprocal operations targeting Hezbollah militant assets in Lebanon, maintaining pressure on the Axis of Resistance.
IAF executes deep precision strikes on an IRGC Quds Force underground logistics and command hub in western Syria as part of Operation Roaring Lion.
Israeli multi-tiered air defense intercepts a coordinated swarm of 12 one-way attack drones launched simultaneously from Iraq and southern Lebanon.
IAF executes deep-penetration strikes targeting subterranean IRGC missile assembly facilities near Isfahan; secondary explosions observed.
Arrow 3 missile defense system intercepts a volley of two medium-range ballistic missiles outside the Earth's atmosphere, launched toward the Tel Aviv metropolitan area.
Multiple squadrons execute precision strikes on IRGC missile production facilities under Operation Epic Fury.
IDF confirms Arrow 3 intercepted two medium-range ballistic missiles targeting the coastal plain.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: Airstrike on Hezbollah Headquarters in Beirut
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Israel conducted an airstrike on Hezbollah's administrative and political headquarters in the southern Beirut suburb of Dahiyeh, killing at least two people.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: Iranian Ballistic Missile Barrage on Israel
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Iran fired 22-24 ballistic missiles targeting Israel's Nevatim and Tel Nof airbases. The IDF claimed all missiles were intercepted or landed in open areas.

Dozens of Israeli fighter jets struck nine air defense systems in western and central Iran, and three petrochemical factories in the southwest. Explosions were reported in Tehran, Tabriz, and Isfahan.
IAF F-35I Adir squadrons execute deep-penetration strikes targeting IRGC subterranean command and control bunkers under Operation Roaring Lion.
Precision standoff munitions hit identified Shahed-136 drone assembly and launch sites clustered near the Iranian-Iraqi border.
Israeli Navy, coordinating with allied maritime elements, intercepts two vessels suspected of transferring advanced guidance components to regional proxies.
Multiple IAF squadrons execute precision strikes on key command centers.
IDF deploys additional Iron Dome batteries along the northern sector.
Israel continues to conduct kinetic strikes against Hezbollah positions in Lebanon amidst ongoing regional volatility.
IAF F-35I Adir jets execute precise strikes against suspected IRGC underground missile assembly sites in the Isfahan province.
As part of Operation Roaring Lion, widespread cyber intrusions disrupt Iranian early warning radar networks, suspected to be led by Unit 8200.
David's Sling and Iron Dome systems successfully intercept a coordinated barrage of 35+ loitering munitions targeting northern Israeli airbases.
IDF airstrikes intensified in southern Lebanon against Hezbollah infrastructure, resulting in dozens of casualties.
Israeli Air Force neutralizes key IRGC forward operating bases in continuous waves under Operation Roaring Lion.
Under Operation Roaring Lion, IAF F-35I Adir squadrons execute deep strikes against suspected underground ballistic missile assembly facilities near Isfahan.
IDF air defense network intercepts a coordinated salvo of medium-range ballistic missiles over the atmosphere using Arrow 3 and newly activated Arrow 4 interceptors.
As part of the broader diplomatic resolution, military operations scale down leading to a cessation of active hostilities in Lebanon.
IDF Air Force strikes identified ballistic missile launch sites deep inside central Iran.
Arrow 3 system intercepts exoatmospheric threat suspected to be targeting Haifa port facilities.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78)
US_NAVAL_FORCES.status: ~9 months at sea — nearing breaking point
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Flagship CSG. Air wing: VFA-11/31/81/136 (F/A-18E/F), VAQ-129 (EA-18G). Positioned for deep-strike sorties into Iran.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72)
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Surged from San Diego. Air wing: VFA-22/94/113/25 (F/A-18E/F). Primary Gulf CAP and tanker escort.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Bataan (LHD-5)
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: 26th MEU embarked. AV-8B/F-35B + AH-1Z. Hormuz transit escort duty.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Mason (DDG-87)
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Ballistic Missile Defense picket. SM-3 Block IIA inventory. Shot down 3 Iranian BMs on Day 1.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Carney (DDG-64)
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Screen for Lincoln CSG. Tomahawk strike platform. 30+ TLAMs fired in first 72 hours.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Ohio (SSGN-726)
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: 154 Tomahawk tubes. Pentagon confirmed SSGN involvement without naming vessel. Assessed as Ohio.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: MCM Squadron
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Clearing reported mine-laying in Hormuz shipping lanes. Operating with UK RFTG (HMS Diamond, RFA).

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: HMS Prince of Wales (R09)
US_NAVAL_FORCES.status: UK RFTG — carrier strike + Hormuz escort
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Response Force Task Group. 8× F-35B (809 NAS). Providing southern Arabian Sea air cover.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US Central Command Gulf Assets
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Conducting precision strikes with long-range missiles and hunting Iranian drone/missile launchers

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Abraham Lincoln
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: EA-18G Growlers launching from flight deck in support of Operation Epic Fury as of March 7

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS George H.W. Bush
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Conducting continuous combat air patrols in support of Operation Epic Fury.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Gerald R. Ford
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Supporting Strait of Hormuz reopening and providing regional air superiority.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Theodore Roosevelt
US_NAVAL_FORCES.status: Active Operations in Gulf of Oman
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Conducting continuous combat air patrols to secure maritime routes.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Ronald Reagan Carrier Strike Group
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Positioned near the Strait of Hormuz to secure shipping lanes during the ceasefire.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS George Washington
US_NAVAL_FORCES.status: Active deployment in Fifth Fleet AOR
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Providing sustained air cover and deterrence capabilities.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: Strait of Hormuz Patrols
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Despite the destruction of Iranian naval assets, ship-tracking data indicates the Strait of Hormuz remains heavily populated by Iran-linked vessels.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Arleigh Burke
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Providing regional ballistic missile defense.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US 5th Fleet
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Operations focused on reopening the Strait of Hormuz after Iranian closure, amid global energy market disruptions.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US Naval Blockade Task Force
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Tasked with enforcing the full naval blockade of Iran announced on April 13.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US Naval Blockade — Day 1 Enforcement
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: CENTCOM begins enforcing blockade of all maritime traffic entering/exiting Iranian ports at 10 AM ET Apr 14. Non-Iranian port Hormuz transits unimpeded. Trump warns Iranian vessels approaching blockade will be 'eliminated'. CTF-152 and USS Arleigh Burke (DDG-51) lead interdiction patrols.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: CENTCOM Fleet
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Monitoring Strait of Hormuz reopening.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Abraham Lincoln
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Maintained regional presence in the Gulf following participation in Operation Epic Fury.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US Central Command Naval Forces
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Deployed to ensure compliance with ceasefire terms and oversee the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Harry S. Truman
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Patrolling the Gulf of Oman.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: CENTCOM Blockade Fleet
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: A dozen ships and dozens of aircraft enforcing the blockade on all vessels entering or leaving Iranian ports. Over 10,000 sailors and Marines involved.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: U.S. Naval Blockade Force
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Enforcing naval blockade of Iranian ports during the ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Spruance
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Disabled and seized Iranian tanker M/V Touska on April 19 in the north Arabian Sea.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Harry S. Truman
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Operating in the Fifth Fleet AOR enforcing maritime security and providing early warning radar.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Leading Carrier Strike Group 2 in the Red Sea / Gulf of Aden operational area.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Bataan
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Positioned in Eastern Mediterranean for contingency operations and strategic deterrence.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Ralph Johnson
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Engaged and destroyed three anti-ship ballistic missiles in the Red Sea over the past 48 hours.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Thomas Hudner
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Operating in the Red Sea corridor providing regional BMD.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: U.S. Fifth Fleet / Blockade Force
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Maintaining a blockade on Iranian ports; an agreement to lift the blockade and resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains unfinalized.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US 5th Fleet Strike Group
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Enforcing the naval blockade of Iran and monitoring the Strait of Hormuz.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Theodore Roosevelt
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Operating in the Gulf of Oman conducting CAPs and strike support for Operation Epic Fury.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: Saronic Corsair
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Operated by CENTCOM Task Force 59; conducted a first-of-its-kind autonomous rescue of two downed U.S. Army pilots in the Gulf of Oman.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: U.S. Navy Blockade Enforcement
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Enforced ongoing blockade of Iranian ports; disabled the Guinea-Bissau-flagged tanker M/T Jalveer using Hellfire missiles.

ISRAEL_DEFENSE_SYSTEMS.note: Overwhelmed by saturation attacks since Day 5; prioritizing population centers
Stunner missile supply critical; US emergency resupply Mar 8
Highest-value asset; used against Khorramshahr-4 MRBM
~800 missiles expended in first 3 days; critical shortage
Next delivery April 2027; battery relocation from Guam/UAE
10kW prototype; effective vs slow drones only; range <7km
Demonstrated successful exoatmospheric intercepts during the reporting period.
Expedited combat deployment of initial two batteries following heavy ballistic threat assessments.

US_CASUALTIES.name: 2 US Navy servicemen
US_CASUALTIES.cause: Drone strike on airfield

US_CASUALTIES.name: 6 US Army soldiers
US_CASUALTIES.cause: Drone struck unprotected Tactical Operations Center

US_CASUALTIES.name: CIA station personnel
US_CASUALTIES.cause: 2 drones struck embassy compound + CIA station

US_CASUALTIES.name: LCpl Kevin Melendez
US_CASUALTIES.cause: Non-hostile incident (details undisclosed)

US_CASUALTIES.name: Sgt. Benjamin Pennington
US_CASUALTIES.cause: Seriously wounded by drone Mar 1, died Mar 8

US_CASUALTIES.name: 67 US Army personnel
US_CASUALTIES.cause: Cumulative wounded over first 48h

US_CASUALTIES.name: 2 DoD employees
US_CASUALTIES.cause: Wounded when tanker MT Stena Imperative ablaze

US_CASUALTIES.name: 6 US KC-135 crew
