GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.def: Ballistic missile, Cruise missile, Drone/UAV — daily intercept counts where MoD reports.
MoD often reports intercepted / detected; gap may include sea falls or leakers.
High = official MoD; Medium = derived; Low = fragmentary or incident-only.
Foreign direct investment — long-term stakes in real assets abroad.
Sovereign wealth fund — state-owned pools (often oil-linked) investing globally.
Force majeure — contractual suspension due to events beyond control.

US codename for joint US-Israeli military operations against Iran launched February 28, 2026. Objectives include destroying Iran's missile capabilities, annihilating its navy, severing proxy support, and preventing nuclear weapons acquisition.
Israeli codename for coordinated strikes with the US under Operation Epic Fury, with Israel focusing on leadership decapitation strikes while US handles large-scale capability degradation.

Iran's strategic oil export hub in Persian Gulf handling 90% of crude exports. Contains deep-water ports for supertankers and major storage facilities. Key economic pressure point targeted by US forces in March 2026.
US tactic of sequential strikes on same targets to maximize casualties, first reported in Iran conflict. Involves initial strike followed by second attack on rescue/response personnel.
Targeted elimination of leadership command structure. Opening phase of Epic Fury involved unprecedented Israeli decapitation strikes killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and senior officials.
Newly developed Israeli air-launched ballistic missile used in Iran strikes. Fired from F-15s during largest combat sortie in Israeli history (200+ fighter jets).
Near-total maritime closure by Iran beginning March 4, 2026. Affects 20% of global oil/LNG transit with over 3,000 vessels stranded. Central strategic issue of the conflict.
Kuwait's largest oil refinery (730,000 bpd capacity) repeatedly targeted by Iranian drone strikes. One of Middle East's largest facilities affected by conflict expansion.
Previous US operation against Iran in June 2025 involving limited nuclear target strikes. Predecessor to more comprehensive Operation Epic Fury.
Socio-economic demographic of over 220,000 Indian nationals repatriated from GCC/Iran due to 2026 conflict. Driving reverse migration and secondary city real estate growth in India.
Temporary governing body established March 1, 2026 to exercise functions of Iran's head of state after Supreme Leader's death
Iranian drone attack on Kuwait's largest oil facility (730,000 bpd capacity) on March 21, 2026
Congressional Authorization for Use of Military Force being discussed by US lawmakers to formally authorize Iran war operations
Iranian intercontinental ballistic missile attack on US-UK base 4,000km from Iran, demonstrating extended range capability
Proposed US operation to occupy or blockade Iran's critical oil export hub to cripple Iranian economy
Remote-controlled explosive boats used by Iran to attack vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, representing a growing asymmetric threat in the narrow waterway.
US tactic reportedly used to maximize casualties by striking the same target twice in succession.
Iranian military strategy that decentralizes capabilities and allows for swift replacement of leaders, enabling continued war of attrition despite leadership losses.
Legal concept invoked by Gulf energy producers when forced to halt exports due to war conditions beyond their control.
Process by which E3 (UK, France, Germany) can trigger reinstatement of UN sanctions on Iran if nuclear negotiations fail.
Underground nuclear weapons facility in Iran reportedly destroyed by US-Israeli strikes on March 3, 2026.
Iranian warship class, all four vessels reportedly destroyed by US-Israeli forces during the war.

Iran's military response operation against US-Israeli strikes. Fourth in series of operations using this codename, involving ballistic missile and drone attacks on targets across Middle East including Gulf states.

Newly developed Israeli air-launched ballistic missile system fired from F-15 fighter aircraft. Made combat debut during opening strikes of Iran war, representing significant advancement in Israeli standoff strike capability.
US Special Operations Command Central unit established December 2025 to deploy low-cost one-way attack drones in Middle East. First-of-its-kind squadron operating LUCAS drones under SOCOM leadership.

GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.term: LUCAS Drone (Low-cost Unmanned Combat Attack System)
GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.def: US one-way attack drone reverse-engineered from captured Iranian Shahed-136. Costs ~$35,000 per unit, operated by Task Force Scorpion Strike. Features enhanced satellite communications and AI-enabled targeting vs original Shahed design.

US Army next-generation ground-launched ballistic missile replacing ATACMS. Range over 500km, can carry two missiles per HIMARS pod vs one ATACMS. Made combat debut against Iran in Operation Epic Fury.

Legal declaration by QatarEnergy suspending contractual LNG delivery obligations due to Iranian strikes on Ras Laffan facilities. Affects 17% of Qatar's LNG export capacity for potentially 3-5 years, impacting global energy markets.
Iranian blockade of critical Persian Gulf waterway through which ~20% of global oil/LNG normally transits. Over 3,000 vessels stranded in region, creating major global energy supply disruption.

Sinking of Iranian Navy frigate by US submarine USS Charlotte near Sri Lanka on February 28, 2026. First submarine combat sinking since Falklands War and first by US submarine since WWII. 104 Iranian personnel killed.
Iranian hypersonic ballistic missile system operationally deployed during the 2026 war, featuring high maneuverability and speed capabilities
Israeli air-launched ballistic missile fired from F-15s, newly developed system used in combat operations against Iran
Proposed US military operation to blockade or occupy Iran's strategic Kharg Island oil hub to cripple Iran's economy
Additional insurance costs for ships transiting high-risk areas like Strait of Hormuz during the Iran conflict
Tactical vulnerability where ballistic missiles equipped with cluster bomblets become difficult to intercept after payload opens and releases submunitions
Disruptions to Iran's covert oil tanker network used to evade sanctions, affecting global oil supply chains
Use of SpaceX's military satellite constellation providing high-bandwidth, unjammable communications for autonomous drone swarms in contested environments

Presidential directive signed by Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on March 24, 2026, declaring a national energy emergency due to oil supply threats from the Iran War, authorizing government procurement of fuel and emergency measures
Brigade-sized rapid deployment unit within the 82nd Airborne Division, approximately 3,000 soldiers ready to deploy anywhere in the world within 18-24 hours
Underground Iranian nuclear facility (Kūh-e Kolang Gaz Lā) near Natanz, buried up to 100 meters deep, assessed by Western intelligence as potential new enrichment plant construction site
Covert Iranian nuclear weapons development site northeast of Tehran, targeted by Israeli strikes for secretly developing critical nuclear weapons components
A significant Iranian nuclear facility located in Yazd Province, which was a target of strikes during the conflict.
An Iranian uranium processing facility located in Yazd Province, which was a target of strikes during the conflict.

A critical geopolitical chokepoint and narrow sea passage connecting the Persian Gulf with the Arabian Sea, essential for global oil shipments. Its potential closure has significant economic implications.

A peace proposal presented by the Trump administration to Tehran via Pakistan, demanding the dismantling of Iran's nuclear program, ending uranium enrichment, and ensuring freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.
Integrated coastal defense network deployed by UAE naval forces to intercept low-altitude suicide drones.
Extended-range variant of the Karrar UCAV observed in recent swarm attacks.
A multi-national defensive protocol activated in response to escalating hostilities on war day 33.
An Iranian high-altitude, long-endurance unmanned combat aerial vehicle, observed operating in Gulf airspace during the late March and early April 2026 escalations.
A joint GCC maritime patrol operation initiated on Apr 2 to secure the Strait of Hormuz.
A joint GCC air defense protocol activated during Phase 3 to synchronize radar data across the Arabian Peninsula.
UAE-led coalition defensive posture enacted on Apr 1 to intercept medium-range ballistic missiles and drone swarms over the Persian Gulf.
A revised maritime defense protocol activated on Apr 2 across Gulf ports to counter asymmetric naval threats.
A United Nations Security Council resolution drafted to condemn Iranian strikes on GCC infrastructure and demand an immediate ceasefire.
The destruction of an incoming ballistic missile outside the Earth's atmosphere, significantly minimizing debris risk to civilian populations below.
A joint defensive maneuver organized by GCC forces observed in the northern Gulf region.
Joint GCC aerial patrol operation initiated on Apr 1 to secure critical infrastructure.
A joint GCC naval and air defense operation initiated in late March 2026 to secure the Strait of Hormuz and the Arabian Gulf from asymmetric maritime threats.
Upgraded Iranian surface-to-air missile system deployed along the Gulf coast, noted in intelligence reports for posing increased risks to regional aviation.
An Iranian long-range surface-to-air missile system recently deployed near coastal battery sites, as observed on Apr 2.
A newly deployed air-to-surface variant of the Zulfiqar ballistic missile, observed in recent strikes.
A joint GCC naval escort mission initiated on Apr 03 to protect commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz from asymmetric maritime threats.
An upgraded variant of the Iranian heavy artillery rocket, first identified in debris from an intercepted strike on Apr 02.
A medium-range ballistic missile introduced into the conflict on Apr 03, featuring an advanced guidance system to bypass electronic countermeasures.
A multi-layered naval defense established near the strait.
A declared maritime no-go area severely restricting commercial shipping, formally enacted on Apr 04.
New variant of Iranian loitering munition observed in Apr 6 strikes on GCC oil infrastructure.
Multinational naval and air defense coordination framework activated on Apr 5 to protect energy transit in the Strait of Hormuz.
A 14-day ceasefire agreement announced on Apr 8, mediated by Pakistan, to pause hostilities between Iran and Gulf forces, with negotiations scheduled for Apr 10.
Iranian policy established on Apr 8 allowing limited commercial transit through the Strait of Hormuz under Iranian military management and proposed transit fees.
A joint GCC air defense framework activated to counter coordinated drone swarms targeting coastal infrastructure.
A joint GCC naval patrol operation established on Apr 9 to secure maritime routes in the Persian Gulf amid heightened conflict.
Sporadic breaches of the April 8 ceasefire occurring in the pre-dawn hours, primarily involving rogue drones.
The emergency ruling body of the Iranian government formed after the reported death of the Supreme Leader.
Upgraded Iranian mobile surface-to-air missile system, analogous to the S-400, used to defend launch sites from counter-strikes.
A fragile two-week ceasefire agreement taking effect around Day 40, aiming to halt large-scale strikes and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
Mediated negotiations commencing on Apr 10 in Pakistan between U.S. and Iranian representatives to stabilize the ceasefire.
A defensive naval and air deployment initiated on Apr 09 to intercept low-altitude threats over coastal waters.
A term for the major military conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran that began on Feb 28 and ended with a fragile ceasefire on Apr 8.
The framework establishing a fragile two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran, brokered by Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Apr 8.
Israel's massive air campaign in Lebanon striking Hezbollah targets, which Iran claims violates the US-Iran ceasefire agreement.
A conflict tracking dashboard (also referred to as the OSS Report) providing live military, economic, and casualty metrics.
A multi-national task force established to coordinate missile defense and early warning systems across the GCC, heavily active during Phase 3 of the conflict.
Iraqi Shia paramilitary group claiming responsibility for secondary drone launches against Kuwaiti islands.
A coordinated air defense operation on Apr 12 resulting in the interception of multiple drones originating from Kish Island before reaching the UAE airspace.
A newly deployed anti-ship cruise missile variant observed on Apr 13 targeting commercial shipping lanes near the Strait of Hormuz.
A joint GCC air defense initiative announced to coordinate regional interception capabilities against incoming drones and missiles.
Upgraded solid-fueled short-range ballistic missile deployed by Iran in the Apr 14 strikes against GCC infrastructure.
IRGC operational name for the second wave of strikes launched on Apr 13 and Apr 14.

A series of peace negotiations brokered by Pakistan following the Apr 7 ceasefire announcement, aimed at converting the fragile truce between the U.S.-Israeli coalition and Iran into a lasting agreement.

A formal diplomatic demand submitted by Iran to the UN Security Council on Apr 14, seeking financial compensation from Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, and Jordan for alleged complicity in the Feb 28 strikes.
An economic warfare measure implemented by the United States on Apr 14, targeting all vessels entering or departing Iranian ports in response to Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
A U.S.-led naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman, fully implemented on Apr 15 to halt Iranian maritime trade.
An alternative designation used by some analysts and media outlets to refer to the 2026 Iran War.
A regional radar and sensor network enhanced during the conflict to detect low-altitude incoming threats.
High-altitude radar balloons deployed by GCC forces in Phase 3 to detect low-flying drones.
A new variant of Iranian solid-fueled short-range ballistic missile identified by CENTCOM on Apr 16 during the attacks on GCC infrastructure.
A modified medium-range ballistic missile first identified in strikes on Apr 15, featuring enhanced terminal evasion capabilities against regional air defenses.
A modified jet-powered variant of the Shahed series loitering munition, observed with increased terminal velocity, first identified on Apr 17.
A decentralized naval mining and fast-attack boat doctrine employed by the IRGC Navy to disrupt commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, intensified since Apr 15.
A newly deployed Iranian hypersonic ballistic missile variant, first identified during the Apr 16 barrage against GCC critical infrastructure.
Joint GCC naval patrol operation initiated on Apr 17 to secure the Strait of Hormuz from asymmetric fast-attack craft.
Extended-range variant of the Shahed-136 loitering munition, featuring enhanced evasion capabilities and confirmed in theater starting Apr 17.
Emergency Security Control of Air Traffic, implemented by Gulf nations including Kuwait and the UAE in mid-April 2026, leading to widespread commercial flight cancellations.
A joint GCC air defense initiative launched on Apr 18 to coordinate counter-UAS strategies.
A new variant of Iranian ballistic missile first observed on Apr 18 targeting coalition naval assets.
An enhanced extended-range variant of the Zolfaghar ballistic missile identified in strikes on Apr 17.
A joint GCC maritime patrol initiative launched on Apr 18 to secure the Strait of Hormuz.
A tactical doctrine employing coordinated fast-attack craft and loitering munitions to restrict commercial maritime transit through the Strait of Hormuz.
An advanced Iranian mobile air defense system, noted for upgraded radar capabilities observed targeting Gulf drones during the April offensive.
A tactical ballistic missile variant newly deployed by IRGC forces against coastal infrastructure, featuring a specialized terminal guidance system.
A petrochemical plant in Ruwais, UAE, which became a target of Iranian strikes during the conflict.
A major refinery in Bahrain targeted by persistent missile attacks.
A maritime area declared by GCC-aligned forces on Apr 21 restricting civilian and commercial vessels near the Iranian coast, enforced by naval patrols.
A modified variant of the Shahed-136 loitering munition, observed with supplementary fuel tanks since Apr 21, allowing deeper penetration into the Arabian Peninsula.
The U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports aimed at strangling Iran's economy during the 2026 conflict.
A container ship seized by Iran's IRGC in the Strait of Hormuz on Apr 22.
A container ship seized by Iran's IRGC in the Strait of Hormuz on Apr 22.
A joint naval patrol framework established by GCC states in early May 2026 to secure commercial transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.definition: Trump-administration suspension (announced ~1900Z May 5, effective May 6 ops cycle) of US Navy escort missions through the Strait of Hormuz. The complementary US-led blockade against Iranian commercial maritime traffic remains in force. Cited rationale: 'Great Progress' toward a final agreement with 'Representatives of Iran,' tabled by Pakistan/Saudi/UAE/Bahrain joint request. Duration: 'short period' — undefined.

IRGC-led joint operational command for Iranian air defense and strategic strike forces. On May 6, 2026, issued formal statement denying any Iranian operations against the UAE in 'recent days' while warning of 'crushing response' if attacked — contradicting Iranian President Pezeshkian's same-day public rebuke of IRGC over those strikes.

GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.def: The return to active hostilities across multiple fronts starting on May 4 (War Day 66), ending the 17-day ceasefire.
An isolated airspace violation over Oman on May 6 (War Day 68), marking an exception to Oman's typical non-engagement status.
A confirmed ceasefire violation involving an interception event over Omani territory on May 6.
Joint Air Defense Command. A formalized PGCC integrated air and missile defense initiative operationalized after early war communication gaps. Began directing allied intercepts around Day 75.
Smaller, harder-to-detect loitering munition increasingly utilized by Iranian-aligned forces for localized strikes on coalition critical infrastructure in late May.
A joint naval and aerial escort operation implemented by GCC states to protect commercial shipping assets transiting the Strait of Hormuz against fast-attack craft and UAV threats.
An advanced Iranian ballistic missile equipped with a hypersonic glide vehicle payload, reported to have been deployed against high-value GCC air defense nodes starting in mid-May.
A coordinated offensive tactic observed in Phase 3 where low-RCS drones are clustered closely with decoy electronic warfare emitters to confuse coastal Early Warning radars.
An advanced Iranian surface-to-air missile system variant identified actively tracking coalition aircraft during the late May offensives.

Extended range and enhanced payload variants of Iranian loitering munitions observed with increased frequency in Phase 3. They feature upgraded terminal guidance systems resistant to standard GCC electronic warfare countermeasures.

The unified regional missile and air defense coordination network activated by the GCC Secretariat. Used heavily since the ceasefire violation to coordinate interceptor trajectories across Saudi, Bahraini, and UAE airspace.

A joint maritime security operation initiated by the US 5th Fleet and GCC naval forces to secure critical energy shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman against fast-attack craft and USVs.
An IRGC designation for the contested maritime area near the Strait of Hormuz where civilian shipping and oil tankers are actively targeted or seized following the May 04 ceasefire collapse.
The official Iranian domestic designation for the renewed regional military campaign that commenced on May 04 after the failure of the Muscat Protocol negotiations.

Gulf Integrated Air Defense Network; a unified command structure sharing real-time tracking data across GCC states, fully operationalized during Phase 3 of the conflict to counter synchronized drone and missile salvos.
An unofficial risk-management designation adopted by maritime insurance syndicates after May 18 to categorize the high-threat navigational corridor between Oman and Iran.
Joint Air Defense Shield - Gulf. A coordinated multi-national air and missile defense architecture activated by GCC states during Phase 3 of the conflict to counter saturation strikes.
An advanced Iranian solid-fueled short-range ballistic missile featuring terminal maneuverability, heavily utilized against fortified military nodes in late May 2026.
Extended-range coastal defense cruise missile utilized by IRGC Navy in recent Phase 3 anti-shipping strikes.
UAE's multi-layered electronic warfare protocol designed to blind drone swarms before they cross the coastal threshold, newly implemented in Phase 3.
The renewed period of active hostilities and kinetic exchanges that commenced on War Day 66 (May 4), following the collapse of the 16-day regional ceasefire.
An electronic warfare tactic observed during Phase 3 where coordinated loitering munition swarms employ localized GNSS spoofing to obscure their true attack vectors from GCC defense radars.
The primary IRGC coastal missile and drone launch complexes utilized extensively during the May Resurgence to target Gulf maritime traffic and northern GCC states.
An advanced Iranian solid-fuel medium-range ballistic missile featuring enhanced terminal maneuverability, observed in strikes targeting deep GCC infrastructure during Phase 3 of the conflict.
A joint GCC-US naval sensor network rapidly deployed along the western coast of the Persian Gulf to detect low-flying UAS and fast-attack surface craft.
The renewed allied naval escort and air defense coordination mission covering commercial transit through the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman following the ceasefire collapse.
A coordinated GCC and allied counter-battery artillery and airstrike campaign targeting forward launcher sites along the Iranian coastline.
An advanced solid-fueled short-range ballistic missile (SRBM) utilized by Iranian forces in the later phases of the 2026 conflict, featuring enhanced terminal maneuverability.
The joint GCC-US naval operation initiated to secure the Strait of Hormuz and escort commercial shipping amidst heightened Iranian maritime threats.
The strategic stalemate in the Strait of Hormuz where allied forces blockade Iranian ports while Iranian forces maintain a 'mine-and-missile' threat against commercial shipping.
The asymmetrical area-denial strategy employed by Iranian naval and aerospace forces in the Persian Gulf to deter maritime transit.

Strait of Hormuz Exclusion Zone, an operational maritime boundary temporarily declared by GCC and US NAVCENT forces in mid-May 2026 to protect commercial shipping from asymmetric naval swarm threats following the ceasefire collapse.
Designated maritime and aerial corridors within the Persian Gulf recognized by commercial shipping and aviation as high-risk due to persistent Iranian anti-access/area denial operations.
The employment of low-cost loitering munitions to deplete GCC interceptor stockpiles immediately preceding a ballistic missile salvo.

A jet-powered variant of the Shahed-136 loitering munition, observed in strikes against GCC infrastructure beginning in mid-May 2026. It features higher terminal velocity and reduced radar cross-section, complicating interception windows.
The integrated air and missile defense (IAMD) coordination protocol activated by GCC and CENTCOM forces during the second active phase of the conflict to counter mixed-mode swarms.
Cumulative risk to civilian areas and infrastructure from falling debris caused by persistent high-altitude intercepts, heavily documented over Gulf metropolises during the renewed May offensives.
Slang for Iranian low-radar-signature loitering munitions deployed heavily after the Day 65 ceasefire collapse, often observed exploiting commercial flight corridors to mask their final approach.
Iranian agency established to collect transit tolls (up to $2 million per vessel) and require permits for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz. Sanctioned by the U.S. Treasury.
The renewed period of active hostilities and modified tactical engagements following the collapse of the Day 49-65 ceasefire period.
A long-range ballistic missile variant introduced by Iranian forces in late May 2026, featuring a maneuverable reentry vehicle and reduced radar signature.
A coordinated GCC-wide air and missile defense protocol activated on May 25 to counter multi-axis drone and missile swarms.
A new Iranian air defense system reported to have shot down a US MQ-9 Reaper or Orbiter drone near Qeshm Island on May 30.
An authority established by Iran to collect tolls of approximately $2 million per vessel from ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
Asymmetric naval strategies involving fast attack craft and naval mines deployed by the IRGC in late May to disrupt commercial shipping.
A U.S. military initiative announced on May 3 to secure the Strait of Hormuz, involving the deployment of 15,000 service members to escort commercial shipping.
A seven-dimensional metric used by analysts to measure the sovereign destruction of the Iranian state following decapitation strikes.
An anonymous protest group that placed functional arcade cabinets at the D.C. War Memorial on May 11 to satirize the war's escalation.
An Iranian solid-fuel Medium-Range Ballistic Missile (MRBM) with enhanced guidance, frequently cited as a primary threat to regional U.S. bases.
An extended-range anti-ship ballistic missile deployed by IRGC forces beginning in late May to target maritime escorts.
Joint GCC naval and coastal defense operation initiated during the Phase 3 resumption of hostilities to secure maritime transit.
Strait of Hormuz Safe Transit Zone, a multilateral naval corridor established after the ceasefire collapsed.
Upgraded, extended-loiter variant of the Gaza drone, widely observed in the Phase 3 Gulf strikes.
An upgraded variant of the Iranian UAV observed heavily in Phase 3, featuring enhanced anti-jamming capabilities, optical camouflage, and extended loitering time.
A declared high-risk maritime area established around Day 80 due to increased naval mine deployment and fast-attack craft activity in the Strait of Hormuz.
Gulf Cooperation Council Networked Engagement and Tracking System, a unified early warning datalink activated in mid-May 2026 to coordinate air defense tracking across member states.
A US-linked airbase in Kuwait targeted by IRGC drones and missiles on Jun 1 during the 2026 Iran War.
A location in southern Iran where US CENTCOM targeted Iranian drone and radar sites on May 31.
An Iranian island in the Strait of Hormuz, targeted by US CENTCOM strikes on May 31 to eliminate drone command centers.
An alternative name for the 2026 Iran War, referring to the broader regional conflict involving the US, Israel, Iran, and Gulf states.
Coordinated naval and aerial operational framework utilizing multiple low-cost loitering munitions to overwhelm GCC coastal air defenses, frequently referenced in post-Day 90 defense briefings.
A joint air-defense coordination framework activated by GCC states during Phase 3 of the conflict to counter low-altitude drone swarms.
A multi-national naval convoy framework established in late May to protect commercial shipping following the collapse of the April ceasefire.
IRGC Navy asymmetric warfare strategy involving coordinated swarming and targeted mine-laying operations in the Strait of Hormuz, escalated after Day 75 of the conflict.
A temporary maritime safe zone established in the central Gulf post-Day 85 to allow neutral commercial shipping transit amidst sporadic Phase 3 hostilities.
An upgraded Iranian short-range ballistic missile with evasive maneuvering capabilities, first definitively identified in strikes against GCC infrastructure during late May 2026.
A coordinated attack pattern utilizing autonomous explosive unmanned surface vessels (USVs) launched from the Iranian coast targeting naval defense perimeters.
Mobile anti-ship missile systems deployed by the IRGC along the Strait of Hormuz, heavily utilized in interdiction attempts since late May.
A joint GCC-US naval and aerial patrol initiative established in late May to protect commercial shipping routes in the Gulf of Oman.
An upgraded solid-fueled tactical ballistic missile with enhanced terminal maneuverability, identified in recent strikes on Eastern Province facilities.
An extended-range variant of the Iranian Qader anti-ship cruise missile, first definitively identified by US and GCC intelligence sources in late May 2026 during Phase 3 of the conflict.

A standardized tracking designation utilized by GCC integrated air defense networks to monitor, classify, and mitigate threats from large falling debris following exo-atmospheric ballistic missile intercepts.
Iranian naval and anti-ship missile posture designed to dynamically throttle, rather than fully blockade, commercial transit through the Strait of Hormuz to apply economic leverage.
Colloquial designation used by regional observers for the enhanced US/GCC joint air defense interoperability protocols enacted following the May ceasefire collapse.
An electronic warfare variant of the Iranian Karrar drone, newly observed during Phase 3 escalations in May 2026, utilized specifically to spoof or suppress GCC early warning coastal radars.

A localized damage metric adopted in Phase 3 to assess civilian and infrastructure risk from mid-air interception fragments, which became critical as intercepts occurred closer to coastal energy facilities.
The period of renewed active hostilities following the breakdown of the late-April ceasefire, characterized by swarm drone tactics and maritime harassment beginning around War Day 66.
Extended-range variant of the Shahab ballistic missile family, observed in strikes post-Day 75.
Advanced high-altitude long-endurance (HALE) Iranian UAV utilized extensively in Phase 3 ISR operations over the Gulf.
A joint US-GCC integrated air and missile defense architecture activated in late May 2026 to counter Phase 3 asymmetric threats.
A medium-range ballistic missile deployed by IRGC forces in Phase 3 of the 2026 conflict, characterized by improved terminal evasion capabilities.
Gulf Coalition Joint Operations Command, established late May 2026 to coordinate combined GCC integrated air and missile defense operations.
A joint GCC naval patrol operation initiated to counter Uncrewed Surface Vessel (USV) threats in the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman following the Phase 3 resumption of hostilities.
Autonomous Uncrewed Surface Vessels equipped with target-recognition AI, increasingly deployed by adversary naval forces in maritime corridors post-ceasefire.
A newly formalized naval task force initiated in late May 2026 by GCC states and Western allies to escort commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz following the Phase 3 escalation.
An Iranian long-range anti-ship cruise missile increasingly utilized in anti-ship roles during the May 18 to May 24 naval skirmishes.
An upgraded variant of the Iranian Karrar combat drone featuring anti-jamming navigation, first observed in late May strikes targeting GCC energy infrastructure and shipping lanes.
A joint GCC-Western naval operation launched in late May to escort critical LNG and crude oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz amid intensified asymmetric maritime attacks.
A sovereign toll system implemented by Iran, forcing transiting vessels to pay passage fees via cryptocurrency during the Strait of Hormuz blockade.
Iranian asymmetric tactic emerging in late May to target Gulf subsea cables and regional data centers, demanding protection fees from foreign telecom operators.
Enhanced GCC integrated air defense posture adopted after the breakdown of the May ceasefire.
A coordinated GCC air defense posture activated in late May 2026 to enhance interoperability during the renewed active hostilities phase (Phase 3).
Simultaneous deployment of loitering munitions and short-range ballistic missiles designed to overwhelm layered coastal defense systems.
Gulf Cooperation Council Integrated Air Defense System; a unified radar and interception network activated fully to counter coordinated drone and missile swarms during Phase 3 of the conflict.
A joint GCC-US air defense coordination mechanism formally activated on May 20 to counter high-density saturation drone strikes originating from southern Iran.
IRGC forward operating base identified by regional intelligence as the primary launch site for Shahed-136 swarms targeting the UAE and Bahrain during Phase 3 of the conflict.
Low-Altitude Loitering Munition. A new class of Iranian suicide drones deployed extensively post-Day 85, designed to evade standard Gulf radar horizons by flying tightly to the sea surface.
A declared maritime no-go area announced by the IRGC on May 25, extending 15 nautical miles from the Iranian coastline, heavily monitored by shore-to-ship missile batteries.
Gulf Cooperation Council Joint Defense Intelligence Cell, established to coordinate real-time air defense tracking and intelligence sharing across member states during Phase 3 of the conflict.
Advanced Iranian medium-range ballistic missile increasingly deployed in Phase 3 strikes, known for its heavy payload and evasive maneuvering capabilities.
A multi-national naval escort initiative established in late May 2026 to protect commercial shipping transiting the Strait of Hormuz from asymmetric and drone threats.
Tactics observed in Phase 3 where sanctioned or 'ghost' tankers spoof AIS data to obscure their locations near Iranian territorial waters amid heightened regional intercepts.
A tactical terminology used by CENTCOM and GCC Defense Intel to describe the post-ceasefire swarm tactics combining low-altitude Shahed-136B decoys with high-altitude ballistic assets.
Anti-Ship Ballistic Missile, increasingly utilized by IRGC naval forces in Phase 3 to target commercial shipping in the Gulf of Oman.
Maritime security tactic involving spoofed AIS signals and private armed escorts to bypass IRGC interdiction zones.
A joint GCC air and missile defense sensor-sharing protocol, formally activated on May 23 (Day 85) to counter high-altitude loitering munitions and synchronize early warning data across the Gulf.
An upgraded Iranian solid-fuel medium-range ballistic missile (MRBM) observed in strikes after Day 80, featuring a highly maneuverable re-entry vehicle designed to bypass terminal-phase interceptors.
An electronic warfare tactic documented in late May where inbound UAVs broadcast overlapping decoy AIS or ADS-B signatures to saturate local civilian air traffic control screens.
An advanced Iranian high-altitude, long-endurance combat UAV, increasingly observed in the Phase 3 strikes targeting Gulf energy infrastructure.
An extended-range solid-fueled short-range ballistic missile (SRBM) utilized by Iranian forces, first identified in the Day 90+ volley targeting GCC logistics hubs.
A joint GCC-US naval surveillance initiative launched in late May to monitor mine-laying activities and fast-attack boat swarm threats in the Strait of Hormuz.
A joint GCC airspace early-warning integration protocol activated in Phase 3 of the conflict to share radar telemetry across member states in real-time.
Iranian long-range mobile surface-to-air missile system, reportedly upgraded with new radar and interceptor capabilities deployed in late May 2026.
Joint GCC naval and air defense operation initiated in mid-May 2026 to secure critical maritime choke points in the Strait of Hormuz against asymmetrical threats.
A new generation of Iranian hypersonic glide vehicles observed being positioned near coastal launch sites starting Day 72.
An advanced, longer-range iteration of the Iranian Shahed series loitering munitions observed deployed against GCC energy infrastructure starting in late Phase 3.
A Phase 3 Iranian tactic involving the launch of loitering munitions from civilian merchant vessels or disguised dhows, minimizing early warning times.
A coordinated launch pattern observed post-ceasefire involving mixed deployment of loitering munitions and ballistic missiles to overwhelm regional air defense networks.
A joint integrated air and missile defense (IAMD) coordination protocol activated by GCC states on May 25 to share early warning radar telemetry in real-time.
An upgraded Iranian medium-range ballistic missile with evasive maneuvering capabilities, first identified in the war's third phase to bypass standard GCC interceptor altitudes.
A networked radar and optical tracking system rapidly deployed by GCC states to counter low-altitude drone infiltration and spoofing tactics.
Naval and aerial early warning cordons established by GCC navies after Day 66 to detect low-altitude loitering munitions before they cross the coastline.
A strategic operation initiated in late May by GCC and allied navies to monitor and restrict Iranian fast-attack craft deployments originating from the Bandar Abbas naval base.
A modified short-range ballistic missile introduced in late May strikes, featuring a maneuverable reentry vehicle (MaRV) designed to bypass terminal phase interception.
A coordinated GCC and allied naval protocol implemented in late May 2026 to ensure safe passage of commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz during Phase 3 of the conflict.
A joint air and missile defense initiative formally activated by the GCC Secretariat in late May 2026, integrating early warning systems across the Arabian Peninsula.
A newly operationalized radar and sensor network sharing real-time telemetry across UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Bahrain to intercept incoming threats.

A tactical electronic warfare deception method documented in early June where commercial shipping Automatic Identification Systems (AIS) are manipulated to create ghost flotillas in the Persian Gulf, aiming to saturate allied targeting systems.
An upgraded solid-fuel medium-range ballistic missile observed in Jun 03 strikes, featuring an advanced evasive maneuver reentry vehicle.
Maritime evasion strategies adopted by Iranian state shipping post-blockade, utilizing spoofed AIS signals and ship-to-ship transfers to obscure cargo origins as documented starting Jun 02.
A Phase 3 Iranian loitering munition variant with enhanced radar evasion capabilities, first observed deployed in the Gulf after the Day 66 ceasefire collapse.
IRGC asymmetric naval and aerial tactics deployed in Phase 3 to harass maritime traffic near the Strait of Hormuz, forcing reroutes without initiating a formal blockade.
Network of illicit tankers used by Iran to bypass sanctions and maintain revenue during Phase 3, increasingly targeted by GCC interdictions.
Air Defense Identification Zone - Gulf; an expanded airspace monitoring protocol activated by GCC forces during the Phase 3 renewed hostilities.
Iranian medium-range ballistic missile utilized extensively against hardened GCC military infrastructure during the second wave of hostilities.

GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.term: Strait of Hormuz Blockade Phase III
GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.def: The renewed attempt by Iranian naval and IRGC forces to choke shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz starting in late May 2026, characterized by widespread deployment of smart naval mines and drone swarms.
Coordinated barrage tactic utilizing multiple Fath-360 short-range ballistic missiles to overwhelm GCC air defense systems, prominently observed in late May and early June 2026.
A widespread cyber warfare campaign targeting regional GCC desalination and power infrastructure, initially detected in mid-May 2026.

Iranian hypersonic glide vehicle whose localized deployment was first claimed by Iranian state media during Phase 3 strikes in late May 2026, posing a novel challenge to regional upper-tier air and missile defense systems.

Iranian military reconnaissance satellite providing overhead intelligence and targeting telemetry for precision strikes, increasingly referenced in adversary BDA (Battle Damage Assessment) reports since late May.
A coordinated GCC and US CENTCOM integrated air and missile defense network strategy implemented to intercept multi-axis drone and missile swarms targeting critical energy infrastructure.
A long-range loitering munition (suicide drone) utilized by Iranian forces, known for its extended range and ability to suppress air defenses.
Iranian surface-to-air missile system, capable of engaging multiple targets simultaneously, frequently deployed in coastal defense configurations.
A newly identified Iranian naval modernization program observed during the late May 2026 naval skirmishes.

GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.term: Strait of Hormuz Safe Transit Corridor (SHSTC)
GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.def: A multinational naval corridor established in late May 2026 by combined GCC and US naval forces to ensure the passage of commercial vessels amid intensified Iranian anti-ship missile threats.

An advanced Iranian high-altitude, long-endurance combat drone that saw escalated deployment by the IRGC starting in Phase 3 of the conflict, particularly targeting deep inland GCC energy infrastructure.
A heavily contested maritime area around Iran's primary oil export terminal, declared a no-go zone by coalition forces in late May 2026, leading to significant spikes in global energy markets.
The critical window of time between a kinetic incident and a verified intelligence consensus on responsibility.
A term used by media analysts to describe when a specific explanation or blame for a military incident becomes widely accepted before complete evidence is available.
A colloquial term for the systematic disruption of oil shipping lanes near Kharg Island by coalition electronic warfare and naval interdictions starting around May 30.
A layered directed-energy defense initiative hastily integrated by US and GCC forces to counter drone swarms, achieving initial operating capability in late May 2026.

A maritime tactic where a vessel broadcasts a falsified Automatic Identification System signal to mask its true location, identity, or destination, frequently utilized by Iranian IRGC naval units during the Phase 3 conflict.
A designated operational area established by GCC and allied naval forces to monitor and intercept Iranian ghost fleet operations and asymmetric naval threats.
A GCC integrated air-defense protocol activated on May 20 to share early-warning radar tracks and sensor data in real-time across member states.
A decentralized network of mobile launcher staging areas on Kish Island used extensively for coastal bombardment and drone swarms in May 2026.
A fresh exchange of missile and drone attacks testing the fragile ceasefire, involving Iranian ballistic missiles targeting Bahrain.
Retaliatory airstrikes reportedly conducted by the UAE Air Force targeting an Iranian oil refinery on Lavan Island.
A coordinated GCC naval operation initiated on May 25 to secure the Strait of Hormuz from asymmetric fast-attack craft.
An updated variant of the Iranian solid-fueled SRBM observed for the first time during Phase 3 renewed hostilities on May 28.
Upgraded Iranian surface-to-air and surface-to-surface missile system deployed in late May 2026 coastal batteries to threaten Gulf shipping.
Emergency hydrocarbon routing protocol enacted by GCC states on May 29 to bypass the Strait of Hormuz via the ADCOP pipeline to Fujairah and overland trucking routes.
A public-interest tracker mapping shipping incidents, vessel seizures, and traffic collapse in the Strait of Hormuz.

GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.term: Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity (C2PA)
GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.def: A standard for secure provenance signals used to verify authentic war footage and counter AI disinformation in the conflict.
Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy, primarily responsible for asymmetric warfare and coastal defense in the Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz.
A specialized Iranian naval unit tasked with patrolling, minelaying, and potentially blockading the Strait of Hormuz during conflict phases.
Iranian designation for the renewed offensive operations following the breakdown of the Day 49-65 ceasefire.
Coordinated deployment of Shahed-136 loitering munitions alongside ballistic missiles to saturate GCC air defense systems during the Day 66+ active phase.
Gulf Airspace Coordination Center, an integrated air and missile defense command established during Phase 3 to synchronize GCC air defense.
Upgraded Iranian loitering munition identified in Phase 3 strikes, featuring extended range and evasive flight path programming.

A declared maritime and aerial exclusion area extending 50 nautical miles from Bandar Abbas, established by allied GCC naval coalitions in late May 2026 to contain IRGC fast-attack craft and coastal missile batteries.
A revised IRGC offensive doctrine observed since Day 80, utilizing highly synchronized, low-altitude multi-vector drone swarms intended to overwhelm C-RAM and Patriot defense layers.
A tactical maneuver observed since late May involving mass deployment of loitering munitions coupled with localized GPS spoofing to overwhelm point-defense systems.
Suspected Iranian subterranean launch complex located in the Zagros mountains, newly designated by US CENTCOM in late May as the origin point for recent ballistic barrages.
Joint GCC/US Fifth Fleet maritime and airspace coordination protocol activated on Jun 01 to synchronize interceptor allocation across the Persian Gulf.
A coordinated allied naval and air patrol initiative launched around War Day 96 to secure critical energy infrastructure in the northern Gulf.
A tactical maneuver observed post-ceasefire (Day 66+) where loitering munitions are launched in tightly clustered formations followed by low-altitude glide phases to evade radar horizons.

Refers to the D68 (May 06) incident where Iranian proxy forces launched projectiles through Omani airspace during the negotiated ceasefire, marking the first major geographic expansion of the conflict's second active phase.

An Iranian medium-range ballistic missile (MRBM) with an estimated 2,000 km range, characterized by mid-course evasion capabilities. Deployed in strikes against regional infrastructure following the breakdown of the Day 65 ceasefire.
A defensive engagement utilizing the THAAD system or SM-3 interceptors occurring at exo-atmospheric altitudes, primarily targeting MRBM class threats.
Air Defense Identification Zone incursion; heavily monitored metric post-ceasefire (Day 66+).
Solid-fuel medium-range ballistic missile (MRBM) frequently cited in Day 80+ interception reports over GCC airspace.
A modified variant of the Iranian solid-fueled ballistic missile introduced in Phase 3, observed in late May strikes against GCC critical infrastructure.
Coordinated cyber-physical attacks targeting regional desalination plants on May 28.
A de facto high-risk maritime area declared by global insurers following the May 25 tanker incidents.
Advanced sensor systems employed by Gulf defense forces to detect and track low-flying loitering munitions that evade conventional radar.
Maneuverable Reentry Vehicle, utilized increasingly since mid-May to bypass GCC layered air defenses.
A Jun 3 drone and missile attack on Kuwait International Airport's Terminal 1, resulting in 1 death and 63 injuries, disrupting the regional ceasefire.
A proposed diplomatic agreement negotiated around Jun 3, intended to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for Iran surrendering its enriched uranium.
An advanced modification of the Iranian medium-range ballistic missile featuring terminal maneuverability, observed in heavy utilization against GCC infrastructure starting late May 2026.
A term used by CENTCOM and GCC defense forces to describe the decentralized Iranian fast-attack craft swarms operating in the Strait of Hormuz since the resumption of hostilities.
The strategic repositioning of GCC and allied forces along the eastern seaboard following the breakdown of the Day 49-65 ceasefire.

A 'shadow fleet' crude oil tanker sailing under a false Botswana flag (IMO: 9203277) that was disabled by a US CENTCOM Hellfire missile on Jun 2, 2026, for violating the maritime blockade while heading to Kharg Island.
The central US command-and-control hub at Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar, revealed on Jun 5, 2026, to have been severely damaged and rendered inoperable by Iranian missiles early in the 2026 conflict.
An Iranian drone attack on Terminal 1 of Kuwait International Airport on Jun 3, 2026, resulting in one fatality (an Indian national), 63 injuries, and severe structural damage.
A joint GCC and allied naval operation launched in early June to secure energy shipping lanes through the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman against asymmetric maritime threats and drone swarms.
A coordinated strike involving a high volume of loitering munitions and SRBMs designed to overwhelm integrated air and missile defense (IAMD) systems, frequently observed in Phase 3 of the conflict.
Maritime emergency procedures forcing commercial shipping to bypass the Strait of Hormuz via the Habshan-Fujairah pipeline and alternative land-bridge routes.
A joint US-GCC maritime and airspace integrated defense framework, formally activated in late May to counter complex, multi-axis drone and missile swarms in the Gulf.
A temporarily secured naval route established by US 5th Fleet in late May to escort commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz amid elevated Phase 3 threats.

Gulf Secure Transit Corridor. A newly established multilateral maritime convoy framework initiated on Jun 02 to escort commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman following renewed hostilities.

GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.term: Goruk, Sirik, and Qeshm Island Radars
GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.def: Iranian coastal surveillance radar installations destroyed by U.S. airstrikes on June 5, 2026, in retaliation for Iranian drone launches.
An upgraded Iranian anti-ship ballistic missile featuring improved terminal guidance and maneuverability, newly deployed against maritime traffic in early June.
A joint GCC-US naval protocol initiated in early June to provide overlapping air defense coverage for commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
A maritime tactic where sanctioned 'dark' vessels are utilized to covertly refuel Iranian fast attack craft outside of standard port facilities.
Integrated regional air defense network coordinated by GCC states and US CENTCOM, fully activated during the Phase 3 renewed offensives.
Advanced Iranian medium-range ballistic missile (MRBM) first observed in Phase 3 strikes on GCC energy infrastructure. Features maneuvering reentry vehicles (MaRV) to complicate interception.
Regional deployment of Aegis Ashore systems integrated with GCC air defense networks, widely activated after War Day 75 to counter ballistic threats.
A shift in Iranian targeting observed since Day 75, utilizing smaller, decentralized drone swarms launched from maritime platforms rather than mainland bases to reduce GCC early warning time.
US-led maritime blockade on Iranian ports initiated on Apr 13 to pressure Tehran into peace negotiations, leading to multiple disabled vessels and retaliatory strikes.
Alternative name for the 2026 Iran War, highlighting the conflict's intersection with the Islamic holy month.
Alternative geopolitical designation for the 2026 Iran War.

A newly established maritime protection zone initiated in early June 2026 by combined GCC and US naval forces to ensure the safe passage of commercial vessels following intense Phase 3 shipping attacks.
A coordinated attack methodology observed post-Day 65, employing mixed arrays of loitering munitions launched from multiple decentralized points to overwhelm regional air defenses.
An unofficial designation adopted by maritime insurers after May 15 to describe the expanded high-risk navigational area encompassing the Gulf of Oman and extending deeper into the Arabian Sea.
An advanced Iranian solid-fueled medium-range ballistic missile (MRBM) frequently utilized in Phase 3 strikes, featuring mid-course evasive maneuvering.
A joint GCC air and missile defense framework activated during Phase 3 to integrate early warning radar systems and share interceptor data across the Arabian Peninsula.
The concerted effort by allied naval forces to seize or disable unmarked and illicit tankers illegally exporting Iranian oil to fund the war effort during Phase 3.
Upgraded variant of the Shahab-3 MRBM equipped with multiple independently targetable reentry vehicles (MIRVs), first observed during the resumed bombardments of Phase 3.

An advanced Iranian unmanned combat aerial vehicle (UCAV), also known as 'Gaza', increasingly deployed in Phase 3 of the conflict to evade regional air defense networks due to its higher operational altitude.
Iran's renewed effort since mid-May 2026 to intermittently block commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz using asymmetrical naval swarm tactics and sea mines.
The multinational maritime security framework initiated on May 15 (Day 77) by GCC and allied navies to escort energy shipments through the Persian Gulf following Phase 3 escalations.
Phase 3 Renewed Offensives: Operational designation for the post-ceasefire resumption of hostilities that commenced on May 04 (Day 66).
Swarm Munitions Intercept Concept: A layered air defense protocol deployed by GCC and NAVCENT forces to counter high-density, low-altitude drone barrages observed in late Phase 3.
Hormuz Littoral Shield: A coordinated multinational naval and coastal patrol configuration implemented following the D68 ceasefire violations in Omani airspace.
Phase 3 maritime operations by coalition navies aimed at intercepting unmarked or spoofed vessels suspected of smuggling Iranian munitions and dual-use components across the Gulf of Oman.
A tactic observed where multiple waves of low-cost drones are launched simultaneously with ballistic missiles to exhaust local air defense interceptor reserves.
An unverified maritime denial zone declared by Iranian naval forces, severely restricting commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz during late Phase 3.
A tactical terminology adopted by GCC defense ministries during Phase 3 to describe the expanded urban radius threatened by high-altitude hypersonic and ballistic intercepts.
An extended-range variant of the Iranian UCAV observed initiating swarms against deep GCC infrastructure and economic hubs from Day 95 onward.
Coordinated UAV and loitering munition flight patterns designed to overwhelm regional air defense systems by executing simultaneous multi-vector approaches and mid-flight altitude alterations.
A coordinated Iranian missile and drone offensive launched in mid-May 2026 targeting GCC infrastructure and air defense arrays.
Very Short Range Air Defense intercepts, increasingly utilizing directed energy or gun-based CIWS systems against low-flying UAS deployed during Phase 3.
Converted commercial or disguised naval vessels used to launch short-range UAS beyond the immediate coastal radar horizon, a tactic observed increasing after war day 75.
Additional shipping and insurance costs applied to vessels using alternative routing to avoid the Strait of Hormuz due to active hostilities.
Newly identified Iranian medium-range ballistic missile variant utilized in late-phase strikes against fortified targets.
Multi-layered Integrated Air and Missile Defense, representing the coordinated use of Patriot, THAAD, and indigenous systems by GCC nations to intercept complex salvos.
Task Force 55, a newly constituted joint US-GCC naval convoy protection element operating in the Strait of Hormuz since late May to secure critical energy shipping lanes.
A joint coastal and airspace defense initiative launched by GCC states in early June to coordinate sensor fusion and interceptor allocation.

A coordinated GCC-US defensive posture framework initiated in early June 2026 to protect desalination plants and power grids from low-altitude suicide drone swarms during the high-demand summer months.
Drone-Swarm Evasion Protocol; a standardized set of evasive maritime maneuvers and electronic countermeasure guidelines issued to commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz starting Jun 4.
Hormuz Maritime Exclusion Zone: A localized operational area declared near the Strait of Hormuz where maritime traffic is restricted due to high risk of surface-to-sea missile engagement.

OSINT term for the deployment of multiple low-RCS (radar cross-section) loitering munitions launched simultaneously from dispersed maritime platforms to overwhelm Gulf air defense networks, first heavily utilized around May 15.
Advanced Iranian high-altitude, long-endurance combat drone with extended range and payload, observed frequently in post-ceasefire swarm attacks after Day 70.
The Israeli military code-name for joint and independent operations during the 2026 Iran War.
A classification of the Apr 08 ceasefire status, denoting a general pause in strategic hostilities punctuated by isolated tactical strikes, such as the Jun 03 violation.
An advanced Iranian short-range ballistic missile (SRBM) introduced in Phase 3 of the conflict, characterized by evasive terminal maneuvers intended to bypass Patriot and THAAD interceptors.
A multi-national naval escort corridor formalized in early June 2026 to ensure the safe passage of commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz amid persistent anti-ship missile threats.
An advanced variant of the Iranian Karrar UCAV, featuring improved radar cross-section reduction and electronic counter-countermeasures, first observed on May 15.
A UAE-developed localized electronic warfare jamming system deployed on May 20 to protect critical desalination infrastructure from loitering munitions.

A declared maritime area in the central Persian Gulf where civilian navigation is heavily restricted due to ongoing naval and anti-ship missile threats, established by combined GCC naval forces around Day 75 of the conflict.
The renewed period of active hostilities beginning on Day 66, following the collapse of the Day 49-65 regional ceasefire.
Strategic terminology used by GCC and Western analysts to describe the Day 68 Iranian drone strike impacting Omani territory, effectively terminating the expectation of unbreached Omani neutrality.
A temporary maritime exclusion area declared by joint GCC naval commands to prevent IRGC fast-attack craft incursions and asymmetric surface threats during Phase 3 escalations.
A coordinated US-GCC counter-battery suppression campaign focused on degrading mobile missile launch sites along the Makran coast.
A joint GCC integrated air and missile defense protocol formally activated in late May 2026 (Phase 3) to share real-time telemetry across UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Bahrain airspace.
A medium-range ballistic missile variant utilized by Iranian forces in the June 2026 strikes, characterized by a detachable maneuverable reentry vehicle.
A proposed Memorandum of Understanding actively drafted in early June 2026 by the U.S. and Iran, mediated by Pakistan, intended to establish a permanent framework to end the conflict.
A strategic phase characterized by the resurgence of direct military exchanges and strikes in June 2026, utilized by both sides to gain leverage during ongoing ceasefire drafting.

Electro-Magnetic Disruption Area. A designated airspace or maritime zone where active electronic warfare operations have severely degraded GPS and communications, commonly reported over the northern Persian Gulf during Phase 3 of the conflict.

Suspected Iranian cyber warfare unit identified by regional cybersecurity analysts, believed responsible for coordinated reconnaissance and probing attacks on GCC energy grid SCADA systems observed in early June 2026.
The unified GCC and CENTCOM defensive posture initiated after the breakdown of the ceasefire on May 04 (Day 66).
A joint GCC maritime and air defense initiative launched in early June to secure the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf coastlines following renewed Phase 3 drone swarm attacks.
Extended-range variant of the Shahab-4 ballistic missile recently identified by OSINT analysts as deployed by Iranian forces during the Phase 3 resurgence.
A GCC unified air and missile defense protocol officially activated during Phase 3 to share real-time telemetry and target acquisition data across national borders.
A joint US-GCC maritime and airspace domain awareness initiative launched in late May to counter advanced low-altitude UAV threats in the Persian Gulf and enhance early warning coordination.
An upgraded variant of the Iranian loitering munition featuring advanced anti-jamming capabilities, first documented in the Day 90+ strikes on Saudi and Qatari offshore facilities.
An integrated early-warning and air defense framework established by GCC states in late May 2026 to counter low-altitude drone swarms.
An advanced Iranian short-range anti-ship cruise missile utilized extensively in Gulf of Oman maritime skirmishes since early June.
A newly formalized joint GCC-US air defense intelligence sharing and interception coordination mechanism activated on Jun 2.
A recently identified low-altitude loitering munition deployed by Iranian forces during Phase 3 of the conflict, designed to evade conventional high-altitude radar systems.
A joint GCC naval and air defense initiative launched in early June 2026 to secure critical energy infrastructure along the Persian Gulf coastlines.
Iranian missile strikes that prompted a significant Israeli retaliation on June 8, 2026, breaking a fragile ceasefire.
GCC and allied maritime interdiction operations targeting unregistered Iranian crude oil tankers attempting to fund Phase 3 operations.
IRGC naval doctrine focused on harassing commercial shipping and mining the Strait of Hormuz to exert severe economic pressure during the prolonged Phase 3 of the conflict.
The logistical and financial penalties associated with diverting commercial shipping away from the Persian Gulf.
A real-time tracking metric utilized in Phase 3 to measure the volume of crude oil successfully navigating the Strait of Hormuz amidst maritime disruptions.
An upgraded medium-range ballistic missile introduced by Iran late in the conflict, featuring evasive maneuver capabilities during its terminal phase.
A naval tactic observed post-Day 80 involving coordinated fast-attack craft and loitering munitions to temporarily block chokepoints without capital ships.
An upgraded Iranian solid-fuel medium-range ballistic missile observed in recent salvos since May 25, featuring a maneuverable reentry vehicle capable of terminal phase evasion.
An extended-range variant of the Paveh land-attack cruise missile, increasingly observed in Phase 3 strikes against GCC energy infrastructure after the ceasefire collapse.
Upgraded variant of the Iranian Kheibar Shekan solid-fueled medium-range ballistic missile, observed in deep-strike attempts against GCC facilities in Phase 3.
A tactical doctrine employing massed Shahed-136 loitering munitions in synchronized waves to overwhelm multi-layered C-UAS Grids.
A major Iranian petrochemical facility in Mahshahr targeted by Israeli airstrikes during the June 2026 escalation.
A tactical doctrine employing coastal missile batteries and fast attack craft to temporarily block maritime transit through the Strait of Hormuz, predominantly seen during Phase 3.
The systematic targeting of unregistered or dual-use maritime vessels suspected of transporting military logistics or illicit crude oil in the Gulf.
A joint GCC-US naval and aerial operation launched in late May to secure the Strait of Hormuz and intercept low-flying Iranian loitering munitions.
An upgraded Iranian air defense and surface-to-surface missile platform identified by OSINT analysts as responsible for the high-altitude interceptions over the Persian Gulf.
A tactic observed in Phase 3 where low-flying Shahed UAV swarms are launched simultaneously with MRBMs to overwhelm multi-layered air defense systems.
Air defense engagement occurring in the final seconds of a ballistic missile's trajectory, increasingly common in Phase 3 as Iranian forces employ depressed trajectories.
Tactical approach combining simultaneous ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and UAV swarms to overwhelm layered air and missile defense systems.
The joint GCC air campaign launched in late May 2026 to target mobile missile launchers along the Iranian coast.

Anti-Access/Area Denial zone; in the context of Phase 3, refers to the highly contested airspace and maritime territory near the Strait of Hormuz where long-range coastal defense cruise missiles are actively deployed.
An upgraded Iranian loitering munition characterized by enhanced anti-jamming capabilities, first visually confirmed in Phase 3 strikes targeting maritime infrastructure.
The strategic posture adopted post-Day 65, characterized by sustained active hostilities and complex drone swarms following the breakdown of the Phase 2 ceasefire.
A coordinated strike tactic observed since Jun 1 involving synchronized cyber disruption and physical loitering munitions to blind regional defense radars.
Anti-Ship Ballistic Missile (Khaibar-buster variant), increasingly utilized by Iranian coastal batteries during Phase 3 hostilities targeting commercial shipping.
Gulf Maritime Security Construct, an ad-hoc regional and international naval coalition formalized post-Day 66 to protect commercial shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz.
A decentralized drone swarm tactic observed after the Day 65 ceasefire collapse, characterized by autonomous target selection and multi-vector saturation attacks.
A localized kinetic incident that occurred on May 06 (Day 68), resulting in a temporary ceasefire violation within Omani airspace before the broader Phase 3 active hostilities resumed.
A coordinated offensive maneuver utilizing tightly grouped loitering munitions, heavily documented by GCC defense forces in the post-Day 66 Phase 3 active period to oversaturate point defenses.
A naval and coastal defense strategy employed by the IRGC to restrict commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz using swarming drones, smart mines, and fast attack craft.
A tactical maneuver observed since May 18, utilizing low-flying Shahed variants simultaneously with high-altitude ballistic trajectories to strain GCC air defense radar tracking limits.
Iranian surface-to-air missile system actively deployed along the Persian Gulf coast in June to target GCC and allied surveillance assets.
Term referring to the reconstituted Iranian clandestine tanker fleet heavily interdicted by US 5th Fleet and allied navies starting in early June.
Joint GCC electronic warfare initiative initiated in early June to jam incoming drone navigation systems.
Arabian Gulf Zone: A coordinated maritime exclusion and air defense operational area established by GCC unified command to intercept aerial threats.
Iranian hypersonic medium-range ballistic missile, reported to have been deployed against high-value strategic targets during phase 3 of the conflict.
Iranian strategic doctrine attempting to block the Strait of Hormuz using asymmetric naval assets and shore-based anti-ship cruise missiles.
A joint GCC naval and air defense initiative launched in late May to protect critical energy infrastructure and secure maritime shipping lanes in the Gulf.

Maritime Exclusion Zone - Gulf of Oman. A contested area informally declared highly risky by maritime insurance syndicates following a surge in anti-ship missile activity and naval mining attempts in early June 2026.
A proposed diplomatic framework drafted by the Pakistan-Qatar mediation track intended to negotiate the lifting of the U.S. naval blockade in exchange for halting proxy strikes.
The ongoing maritime interdiction campaign that remained in effect past Day 85, significantly impacting global energy shipping and regional supply chains.
A tactic observed where loitering munitions are launched from dispersed, mobile platforms rather than fixed bases, increasing interception difficulty across the Gulf.
Areas designated by civil defense authorities where intercepted missile and drone debris is most likely to land, prompting localized shelter-in-place orders without full-scale air raid sirens.
A tactical maneuver involving simultaneous deployment of multiple low-cost loitering munitions to overwhelm local air defense radars ahead of heavier ballistic missile strikes.
Counter-battery operations directed at anti-ship missile launchers and fast-attack craft facilities along the coastline to protect shipping lanes.

Loitering Munition Swarm. A tactic increasingly employed by IRGC-aligned forces during Phase 3 (after D66), utilizing dozens of low-cost Shahed-series drones deployed simultaneously to overwhelm integrated air defense radars.
Phase 3 Interdiction Network. The semi-official designation for the integrated GCC-US radar and kinetic interceptor web optimized and deployed following the D49-D65 ceasefire.
Extended Range Precision Guided Munition used by UAE Mirage 2000-9 aircraft for stand-off strikes against Iranian air defense networks starting late May.

A multi-national naval escort and airspace integration initiative launched in late May 2026 to protect commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman during Phase 3 of the conflict.
A GCC integrated air defense strategy utilizing networked civilian ATC and military radars to track low-altitude drone threats.
A phenomenon causing localized structural damage (e.g., shattered glass) resulting from the low-altitude interception of ballistic missiles over urban areas.
Hormuz Restricted Navigation Zone, a multilateral maritime corridor established around Day 95 to escort commercial vessels through high-risk sectors.
A modified variant of an Iranian medium-range ballistic missile introduced late in the conflict, featuring localized terminal maneuverability to evade established air defense nets.

A newly deployed solid-fueled medium-range ballistic missile utilized by Iranian forces since late May, featuring terminal maneuverability designed to evade Patriot PAC-3 and THAAD interception layers.

A secured shipping lane established by US NAVCENT and GCC naval forces on Jun 02 to guarantee the safe passage of critical energy exports amidst rising anti-ship missile threats in the Strait of Hormuz.
Civilian or military sectors temporarily quarantined after successful mid-air intercepts, due to unspent toxic hypergolic propellants falling from intercepted ballistic missiles.
Designated maritime and airspace corridors where electronic warfare is actively jamming communications, preventing civilian and non-military access.
An upgraded, longer-range intelligence and attack drone deployed by Iran in the latter phases of the 2026 conflict to evade high-altitude radar.
An upgraded Iranian medium-range ballistic missile (MRBM) featuring solid-propellant and terminal-phase evasion maneuvers. First definitively cataloged by CENTCOM early in Phase 3 of the conflict.
Market terminology for the elevated maritime insurance and shipping cost multiplier applied to commercial vessels traversing the Strait of Hormuz following the collapse of the Day 49-65 ceasefire.
A unilateral maritime exclusion zone declared by Iranian naval forces in early June 2026, restricting commercial transit through the eastern approaches of the Strait of Hormuz to pressure the GCC.
A shared radar and sensor data architecture rapidly scaled among GCC states during Phase 3 to counter low-altitude UAS threats bypassing localized air defenses.
Advanced AIS (Automatic Identification System) manipulation tactics used by illicit oil carriers in the Gulf to mimic legitimate UAE or Omani vessels, complicating target identification.
Network of commercial tankers operating with disabled AIS tracking, attempting to bypass Strait of Hormuz exclusion zones following the breakdown of the ceasefire.
Combined maritime task force operation initiated on May 15 by GCC naval forces to secure merchant shipping corridors against coastal anti-ship missile threats.
The strategic repositioning of naval and air defense assets following the breakdown of the Phase 2 ceasefire on Day 66, initiating a new wave of active operations.
A new tactical designation for coordinated low-altitude loitering munition swarms deployed extensively since May 08 to bypass coastal radar networks.
A joint GCC defense doctrine activated to integrate early warning radar data directly with commercial shipping operators navigating the Strait of Hormuz.
A tactical approach involving asymmetric naval swarms, naval mines, and coastal anti-ship missiles aimed at restricting access to the Strait of Hormuz, notably observed escalating in Phase 3.
The use of multiple small, fast, and often unmanned surface vessels (USVs) to overwhelm traditional naval defenses, frequently employed in Gulf waters since late May.
An Iranian anti-ship ballistic missile (ASBM) equipped with an optical seeker, increasingly utilized to target shipping lanes in the Gulf of Oman during Phase 3 of the conflict.

A tactical maneuver involving the simultaneous deployment of dozens of low-cost loitering munitions designed to overwhelm theater air and missile defense systems, widely observed in Phase 3 of the conflict.

A military and economic strategy involving asymmetrical naval mining and anti-ship missile deployments to block maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, causing severe energy market disruptions.
A network of ships operating without standard international tracking or insurance, utilized by belligerents to export oil covertly during naval blockades in the Gulf.
A flight path algorithm observed in late-phase Iranian Shahed-136 variants designed to spread out dynamically upon detecting active radar locks.
High-altitude missile defense engagement occurring just below the Karman line, frequently utilizing THAAD or Arrow 3 systems to counter exoatmospheric threats.
A coordinated attack utilizing multiple loitering munitions and cruise missiles simultaneously to overwhelm integrated air and missile defense (IAMD) systems.
A newly deployed class of Iranian medium-range ballistic missiles observed in the June 2026 strikes.

items.desc: Port Shuaiba, friendly fire incident, airport strikes, civilian death.

items.name: 2026 Iranian strikes on Saudi Arabia
items.desc: PSAB, Ras Tanura, embassy strikes, Al-Kharj residential attack.

items.name: 2026 Iran war (main article)
items.desc: Overview article with timeline and cross-references.

items.name: Timeline of the 2026 Iran war
items.desc: Chronological event listing.

items.name: Bahrain AP vs BDF methodology
items.desc: Mar 5 AP: 75M intercepted (65 destroyed), 124D (88 downed, 36 LANDED). BDF official uses 'intercepted and destroyed' combining methods. True penetration higher than BDF implies. RECOMMEND: Use BDF cumulative but flag AP methodology.

items.name: Saudi aggregate gap
items.desc: Saudi MoD issues fragmented regional bulletins (3-6/day). No single cumulative. Overlap risk when summing. Aviation A2Z '25 BMs + 151 drones by Day 12' is estimated aggregate, not official.

items.name: Qatar post-Mar 10 gap
items.desc: Most comprehensive Qatar total (151M, 69D, 2 aircraft) dates to Mar 10. No updated cumulative through Mar 16. True total substantially higher.

items.name: Kuwait window inconsistency
items.desc: Some bulletins cover 24h, others 48h. Army GHQ and National Guard issue separate reports — double-count risk. Breaking Defense '97 BM / 283 drones' at ~36h consistent with later FDD tallies.

items.name: Wikipedia UAE injury date error
items.desc: Wikipedia states '141 injured as of 10 March' but 141 is from the March 14 MoD statement. Mar 10 actual: 122 injuries.

items.name: Qatar Feb 28 vs Mar 1 timing
items.desc: Big Qatar salvo (65 BM) reported in Feb 28 headlines but MoFA presser dated Mar 1. Likely: strikes began late Feb 28, presser issued Mar 1 covering first 24h.

items.name: Unofficial overlay confidence
items.desc: Rows marked U-High/U-Med/U-Low are sourced from third-party, local, or Arabic-language reporting that has not been formally confirmed by official MoD statements. U-High = 2+ strong non-official sources or 1 strong source + imagery. U-Med = 1 strong source. Treat as supplementary layer only.

items.name: BNA (Bahrain News Agency)
items.desc: Official Bahraini state news. Arabic-first. Used for daily BDF cumulative checkpoints including Mar 3 (73M/91D), Mar 6 (78M/143D), Mar 9 (102M/171D), and foreign minister damage statements.

items.name: QNA (Qatar News Agency)
items.desc: Official Qatar state news. Arabic-first. Daily defense bulletins and cross-posted Gulf intercept reporting.

items.name: KUNA Arabic
items.desc: Official Kuwait state news agency Arabic feed. Separate from English KUNA. Used for airport and civil aviation damage reports.

items.name: ONA (Oman News Agency)
items.desc: Official Omani state news. Used for Salalah Port fuel tank damage and drone interception reports.

items.name: SPA Arabic (Saudi Press Agency)
items.desc: Official Saudi Arabic state news. Used for embassy strike detail, GCC condolences, and drone interception bulletins.

items.name: Muscat Daily
items.desc: Oman-based English/Arabic daily. Used for Duqm worker housing injury (Mar 1), MKD VYOM tanker fatality (Mar 2), and Duqm fuel tank strikes (Mar 3).

items.name: Times of Oman
items.desc: Major Omani English-language daily. Used for Salalah Port fuel tank damage (Mar 11) and Dhofar drone interceptions (Mar 3). Relays ONA official reporting.

items.name: Kuwait Times
items.desc: Major Kuwaiti English-language daily. Used for Mar 7 drone intercepts, Mar 12 airport damage, and Saudi Zulfi residential strike. Relays KUNA and Civil Aviation statements.

items.name: Arab Times (Kuwait)
items.desc: Kuwait English daily. Used for KAFCO airport fuel tank fire (Mar 8) and Public Institution for Social Security building damage (Mar 9). Cites DGCA/KUNA.

items.name: Asharq Al-Awsat
items.desc: Pan-Arab daily with strong Arabic and English editions. Used for Bahrain Bapco refinery fire (Mar 5), Sitra 32 casualties including 4 critical (Mar 9). Cross-region coverage.

items.name: Gulf Times (Qatar)
items.desc: Qatar-based English daily with extensive regional conflict coverage. Published daily PDF editions with running updates on UAE/Dubai/Qatar attacks. Key articles: Mar 22 (regional escalation update), Mar 25 (Qatar denies US-Iran mediation), Mar 27 (multi-country missile tracking). Cross-references UAE MoD data with Qatari perspective.

COUNTRY_SUMMARY.name: UAE
COUNTRY_SUMMARY.keyTargets: Al Dhafra, Al Minhad, DXB, Jebel Ali, Ruwais, Fujairah

cadence.note: UAE MoD publishes same-day cumulative. Near-certain daily update.

COUNTRY_SUMMARY.name: Qatar
COUNTRY_SUMMARY.keyTargets: Al Udeid AFB, Ras Laffan, Mesaieed, Hamad Intl

cadence.note: Qatar publishes ~60% of days. Post-Mar 10 gap is 23 days — severely overdue. Prolonged silence may indicate infrastructure damage or reporting collapse.
