Oil-Price-Jun1.execSummary: Brent crude oil prices rose to $93.28 per barrel on Jun 1 amid the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz and renewed US-Iran strikes.
Oil-Price-Jun1.appendDetailedSection: The extended closure of the Strait of Hormuz by Iran has maintained a global fuel crisis, further exacerbated by military escalations on May 31 and Jun 1.

market_volatility_may.execSummary: Regional exchanges experienced a 4% dip following the May 18 escalation, marking the steepest single-day decline of Phase 3.
market_volatility_may.appendDetailedSection: Market sentiment remains fragile as the Phase 3 active hostilities persist, with energy futures heavily impacted by transit corridor threats.

energy_volatility_may.execSummary: Energy markets experienced renewed volatility in late May due to rising USV maritime threats near the Strait of Hormuz, pushing Brent crude past $92/bbl.

MARKET_PHASE3_MAY.execSummary: In late May, Brent crude surged past $115/bbl due to the formalization of the Gulf Maritime Coalition and increased harassment of shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
MARKET_PHASE3_MAY.appendDetailedSection: Shipping insurance premiums have reached 3.5% of hull value for vessels traversing the Persian Gulf. Commercial rerouting has delayed average transit times by 14 days.

energy_commodities.execSummary: Brent crude futures spiked past $115/bbl on Jun 3 following intensified Phase 3 strikes on GCC energy infrastructure and the introduction of heavier missile variants.
energy_commodities.appendDetailedSection: Day 96 saw a sharp 4% jump in global energy benchmarks as the new Khorramshahr-4 deployments increased risk premiums, raising concerns about prolonged disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

markets_may_review.execSummary: Gulf financial markets experienced elevated volatility throughout late May due to critical infrastructure cyber threats, prompting localized trading halts.
markets_may_review.appendDetailedSection: Sector analysis reveals energy and utilities suffered the highest drawdowns, whereas cybersecurity and local defense contractors saw significant inflows.

markets_may.execSummary: Late May saw a sharp spike in Brent crude approaching $105/bbl following new threats to the Strait of Hormuz on May 24.
markets_may.appendDetailedSection: Shipping insurance premiums have increased by 2.5% for vessels traversing the Gulf of Oman.

volatility_index.execSummary: Regional market volatility spiked sharply in early June due to the introduction of new Iranian SRBMs (Khaibar-5), reversing the tentative stabilization seen during mid-May.
volatility_index.appendDetailedSection: Insurance premiums for shipping in the Persian Gulf increased by an additional 15% following the formation of TF-55 on May 28, reflecting heightened risk perceptions despite enhanced naval escorts.

market_resilience.execSummary: Tadawul and DFM showed localized recoveries in early June as combined GCC defense protocols restored investor confidence.
market_resilience.appendDetailedSection: Bourse & Bazaar analysis on Jun 05 indicates that logistics and defense stocks in the region have rallied significantly despite the Phase 3 resumption of hostilities.

energy_sector_impact.execSummary: Brent crude volatility has stabilized as Phase 3 shifts away from direct energy infrastructure targeting towards military installations.
energy_sector_impact.appendDetailedSection: Recent data indicates a 15% drop in maritime insurance premiums for vessels traversing the Strait of Hormuz since early June, reflecting adjusted risk models for the Ghost Fleet Interdiction strategy.

ENERGY_EXPORT.execSummary: Risk premiums on Brent crude escalated following the Jun 04 introduction of advanced swarm tactics targeting periphery export facilities.

hormuz_shipping.execSummary: Launch of Operation Sentinel Shield has partially stabilized regional shipping premiums, though Lloyd's war risk rates remain elevated.
hormuz_shipping.appendDetailedSection: The deployment of additional multinational assets under Operation Sentinel Shield starting Jun 02 has restored some confidence in Gulf shipping lanes, preventing further spikes in Brent crude prices despite ongoing hostilities.

OIL_VOLATILITY.execSummary: Brent crude futures experienced sharp intra-day volatility as Day 66-101 (Phase 3) strikes increased frequency, countering the stabilizing effects seen during the Day 49-65 ceasefire.
OIL_VOLATILITY.appendDetailedSection: Market pricing now factoring in a sustained conflict premium, discounting the likelihood of a near-term diplomatic offramp after the Oman Protocol Breach.

strait_transit_costs.appendDetailedSection: Insurance premiums for Strait of Hormuz transits spiked in early June following the declaration of the Bandar Abbas Exclusion Zone, with VLCC rates increasing by 14%.

markets_energy_phase3.execSummary: Brent crude prices experienced significant volatility following the Phase 3 resurgence, spiking above $110/bbl due to renewed targeting of energy infrastructure.
markets_energy_phase3.appendDetailedSection: Despite initial market stabilization during the early ceasefire, the renewed attacks on regional logistics hubs have forced commodities traders to readjust supply disruption probabilities.

Day100_Market_Update.execSummary: Regional exchanges stabilized on Jun 9 after volatility from late Phase 3 escalations.
Day100_Market_Update.appendDetailedSection: Market responses have dulled as the conflict enters its fourth month, with investors pricing in sustained active hostilities.

SHIPPING_INSURANCE.execSummary: War risk premiums in the Gulf hit 3.5% of hull value as of Jun 10.
SHIPPING_INSURANCE.appendDetailedSection: Following the resumption of hostilities and breakdown of the ceasefire, maritime insurance premiums have climbed rapidly. On Jun 10, Lloyd's syndicates quoted 3.5% for Strait of Hormuz transits, significantly impacting global energy logistics.

hormuz-shipping-jun.execSummary: Prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz continues to inflate regional insurance premiums and limit crude export volumes.

a.title: Iran's Remaining Offensive Capacity
a.execSummary: Iran entered the war with ~2,500 ballistic missiles and ~400–480 launchers. By Day 34, CENTCOM has struck 10,000+ targets; IDF 60-70% of pre-war targets destroyed. Iran sustains 5-20 BMs daily plus drone swarms. Hormuz remains effectively closed. IRGCN asymmetric capability (170 fast craft, ~6,000 mines) is 80-90% intact. Drone production (~10,000/month) enabled by Chinese weapon transfers via Russia/Turkmenistan/Pakistan. Beidou nav receivers confirmed for Shahed drones. IRGC shifted to economic targeting (tech companies).
keyFindings: Iran's conventional navy is 'combat ineffective' with 60+ ships sunk, but IRGCN asymmetric capability (170 fast craft, ~6,000 mines, drone boats) is 80–90% intact — making Hormuz disruption sustainable for months.
The Day 17–19 uptick in missile fire with cluster munitions contradicts simple depletion narratives — Iran is managing inventory deliberately, not running out.
Khamenei's assassination triggered IRGC seizure of state control via Mojtaba Khamenei; Iran explicitly rejected ceasefire talks, signaling intent for prolonged attrition.

detailedSections.heading: Ballistic Missile Inventory
detailedSections.content: Pre-war: ~2,500 missiles, ~400-480 launchers. By Day 15: ~150 active launchers, 70%+ destroyed. ~1,412 BMs fired through Day 19. Iran reluctant to bring launchers out of tunnels due to airstrike risk.

detailedSections.heading: Drone Capacity Underestimated
detailedSections.content: ~3,277 drones launched by Day 19. Production capacity ~10,000/month. War on the Rocks: 83% decline measures tempo, not capacity. Dispersed manufacturing generates few signatures. Iran may be stockpiling for coordinated attacks.

detailedSections.heading: Naval Asymmetric Threat
detailedSections.content: 60+ ships sunk; conventional navy 'combat ineffective.' But IRGCN retains 80-90% of fast attack craft (~170 vessels), ~6,000 mines, drone boats. Iran began mining Strait in Week 2. Could disrupt Hormuz with drones alone 'for months.'

detailedSections.heading: Depletion Curve Adaptation
detailedSections.content: Missile fire declined ~90% from Day 1 peak of 428. But Day 17-21 uptick with cluster munitions achieved highest hit rate. Iran managing inventory deliberately — fewer missiles, higher yield. Consistent with attrition strategy.

detailedSections.heading: Command Decapitation and IRGC Control
detailedSections.content: Khamenei killed Feb 28. Mojtaba elected March 9 under IRGC pressure. 500+ C2 sites struck. Personnel operating from tents. But Russia/China ISR partially compensating for degraded communications.

b.title: US Offensive Sustainability — Operation Epic Fury
b.execSummary: By Day 34, CENTCOM confirms 10,000+ US targets struck (IDF thousands more). 850+ Tomahawks fired (surpassing Iraq 2003). 57,000+ troops in theater. IDF hit Tehran Revolutionary Court, 10 MoI sites, Quds Force HQ. Strikes in 26 of 31 provinces. Trump signals exit in 2-3 weeks but IDF plans 3+ more weeks. UK hosts Hormuz diplomatic conference. April 6 deadline 4 days away. Cost: $500M/day with $200B supplemental requested.
keyFindings: The Navy fired ~400 Tomahawks in 72 hours (~10% of ready inventory), replacement 'could take years' — but rapid transition to JDAMs by Day 5 mitigated the standoff munitions constraint.
The USS Gerald R. Ford is at ~9 months at sea (nearing breaking point) with no replacement carrier in theater — the structural carrier rotation gap is the Navy's most acute sustainability risk.
Iran's strategy of targeting tankers is deliberate and effective: one KC-135 shot down, 5 more damaged on the ground, removing 6 from service in the campaign's most critical logistics bottleneck.

detailedSections.heading: Force Disposition
detailedSections.content: 2 carrier strike groups (Lincoln, Ford) plus 30+ warships. ~200 fighters across 8 bases. Ford at ~9 months at sea — nearing breaking point. Bush completing workups but not in theater. F-22s deployed to Israel for first time.

detailedSections.heading: Munitions Transition
detailedSections.content: ~400 Tomahawks in first 72 hours (~10% of inventory). By Day 14, >90% were low-cost JDAMs. Hegseth claimed 'nearly unlimited' stockpile — assessed credible given 30,000+ annual kit purchases. Boeing holds $7.5B contract.

detailedSections.heading: Cost Trajectory
detailedSections.content: $11.3B in first 6 days. ~$500M/day ongoing. Pentagon requesting $200B supplemental. Penn Wharton projects $40-95B for 2-month conflict. Israel spending ~$410M/day; IAF dropped 12,000 bombs in 18 days.

detailedSections.heading: Critical Constraints
detailedSections.content: KC-135 shot down over Iraq (6 crew KIA), 5 damaged at Prince Sultan. THAAD gap through April 2027. Ford needs replacement. 60-day War Powers deadline ~April 29. Only 25% public support for strikes.

detailedSections.heading: Campaign Phase Progression
detailedSections.content: Phase 1 (D1-4): Air defense, C2, nuclear sites. Phase 2 (D5-12): Industrial base, drone factories, naval. Phase 3 (D12+): Kharg Island, Hormuz sites, energy. 7,000+ targets struck; 6,000+ sorties.

c.title: Defender Interceptor Depletion
c.execSummary: The interceptor depletion problem is arguably the single most consequential variable in the war. Iran's offensive missiles cost $100K–$11.5M; interceptors cost $3.7M–$28M. Gulf states burned ~800 PAC-3 interceptors in three days — more than Ukraine used in four years. By March 14, Israel informed the US it was 'critically low' on ballistic interceptors, with no new THAAD deliveries until April 2027.
keyFindings: Core asymmetry is existential: Iran expends $20K–$500K weapons to force $3.7M–$28M interceptor expenditure — cost-exchange ratios of 10:1 to 370:1 in Iran's favor, making exhaustion the deliberate strategy.
The THAAD delivery gap through April 2027 is immovable — 100 interceptors in procurement backlog, production line diverted to foreign orders, factory expansion won't produce until 2028–2030.
Crisis deferred (not resolved) by destroying 70% of launchers; if mobile launchers are reconstituted, defender stocks would be critically insufficient.

detailedSections.heading: US THAAD Inventory Crisis
detailedSections.content: Pre-war: 534 interceptors; 100-150+ used in June 2025. No new deliveries until April 2027. THAAD radar destroyed in Jordan ($484M loss). South Korea battery being redeployed to Middle East.

detailedSections.heading: Gulf States Burning Through Interceptors
detailedSections.content: ~800 PAC-3 interceptors fired in first 72 hours — more than Ukraine used in 4 years. Gulf states now 'selective about which projectiles to target.' Former US official: 'We have shot several years of production.'

detailedSections.heading: Cost Asymmetry
detailedSections.content: Iran Shahed drone costs $20-50K; US Patriot interceptor $3.7M (ratio up to 370:1). SM-3 Block IIA costs $28M vs Iranian Emad at ~$250K. Iran's strategy is exhaustion, not destruction.

detailedSections.heading: Depletion Curve Scenarios
detailedSections.content: Grieco (Stimson): at Twelve-Day War rate, half of entire US interceptor stockpile depleted in 4-5 weeks. Crisis averted because Iranian launch rates collapsed 90% due to TEL attrition. But structural gap measured in years.

detailedSections.heading: Production Lag
detailedSections.content: THAAD: 96/year, expanding to 400/year over 7-year ramp (first impact ~2028-2030). Patriot PAC-3: 620/year, targeting 2,000 by 2030-32. Lead time 1.5-2.5 years per order. Lockheed investing $2B+ across 20+ facilities.

d.title: Iran Strategic Calculus & Endgame Scenarios
d.execSummary: By Day 34, Trump signals US exit in 2-3 weeks even if Hormuz remains closed. UK hosts international Hormuz conference — Iran FM: 'trust at zero.' April 6 deadline 4 days away. Brent ~$116/bbl (slight ease on ceasefire rumors). IEA warns April supply losses will double March. China confirmed supplying weapons to Iran via Russia/Turkmenistan/Pakistan. CENTCOM: 10,000+ targets struck. IDF plans 3 more weeks — potential US-Israel divergence. War Powers deadline April 28.
keyFindings: US objectives have shifted from nuclear to missiles to regime change and back, with 'unconditional surrender' serving as rhetorical maximum — Hegseth's March 19 removal of any timeline suggests the administration is no longer managing expectations around a near-term exit.

Iran's nuclear calculus has fundamentally changed: with ~75% of known nuclear infrastructure struck, the question shifts from 'can Iran build a bomb' to 'can a successor regime reconstruct under occupation-level surveillance.'

The War Powers Resolution 60-day clock (April 28) is the hardest political constraint — if Congress does not authorize force, Trump faces a constitutional confrontation that could limit escalation options regardless of military success.

detailedSections.heading: Ceasefire Dynamics
detailedSections.content: Trump demanded 'unconditional surrender' March 6. Rejected Egypt-Oman mediation. Iran's Araghchi: 'We never asked for a ceasefire.' Iran demands end on all fronts including Lebanon. Hegseth removed all timelines March 19.

detailedSections.heading: Iran Strategic Calculus
detailedSections.content: CSIS: Iran's strategy is 'Don't Calibrate — Escalate.' Core mechanisms: interceptor exhaustion, Gulf economic coercion, Hormuz leverage (~20% of global oil), US domestic pressure (56% opposition). Mojtaba Khamenei: 'Hormuz leverage must continue.'

detailedSections.heading: Nuclear Status Post-Strikes
detailedSections.content: Trump announced strikes on Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan. IAEA confirmed Natanz damage. ~200kg of 60% uranium remains intact underground — enough for ~5 warheads if enriched to 90%. Breakout timeline moved from zero-day to 8-15 year reconstitution.

detailedSections.heading: Five Endgame Scenarios
detailedSections.content: A: Quick ceasefire (15-25%). B: Grinding degradation (40-50%, most likely). C: Exponential escalation with Houthi activation (20-25%). D: Regime change (38% by year-end per Polymarket). E: Frozen stalemate.

detailedSections.heading: Gulf States Strategic Dilemma
detailedSections.content: GCC condemned Iran but refused to enter war directly. Carnegie: attacks pushing Gulf toward 'total dependence on US security guarantees.' US bases now seen as 'liability rather than asset.' Gulf states urging US not to stop short.

Maritime Security.execSummary: Increased drone activity intercepted off the eastern coast on Apr 8, leading to temporary rerouting of commercial vessels.

tactical_shift_phase3.execSummary: Since Day 80, adversary forces have introduced Zelzal-3B variants, shifting focus to critical infrastructure.
tactical_shift_phase3.appendDetailedSection: The introduction of Zelzal-3B represents an escalation in payload capacity, testing GCC defense tiers.

diplomacy_update.execSummary: Islamabad Peace Talks began on Apr 10, mediated by Pakistan. U.S. delegation led by VP JD Vance. A fragile two-week ceasefire, known as the 'Two Generals Framework', is in effect.
diplomacy_update.appendDetailedSection: Despite the truce, violations occurred. Israel conducted over 100 airstrikes in Lebanon on Apr 08 and Apr 09. Kuwait reported drone attacks on Apr 09. Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement on the 40th day of the conflict, asserting Iran's rights.

naval_posture.execSummary: Naval and air forces shifted to forward screen on Apr 09.

DIPLOMACY.execSummary: Face-to-face peace negotiations commenced in Islamabad on Apr 11 between U.S. and Iranian delegations, mediated by Pakistan.

tactical_shift_apr.execSummary: Adversary forces have shifted to employing jet-powered loitering munitions in Phase 3 operations.
tactical_shift_apr.appendDetailedSection: The introduction of the Shahed-238B on Apr 17 indicates a tactical adjustment aimed at exploiting gaps in point-defense systems.

defense_update.execSummary: As of Apr 14, GCC nations are actively enhancing joint defense protocols under Operation Swift Shield to counter persistent aerial threats.

adv_day46.execSummary: Increased ballistic missile activity observed on Apr 14 across the Gulf, with new missile variants introduced.

islamabad_peace_talks.execSummary: A fragile ceasefire brokered by Pakistan on Apr 7 holds, with active large-scale bombings subsiding. A second round of peace talks is scheduled in Islamabad between U.S. and Iranian teams on Apr 15.

ADV-APR15.execSummary: Adversary forces introduced upgraded rocket variants on Apr 14.

defense_network_apr16.execSummary: On Apr 16, an integrated early warning framework became fully operational to counter low-altitude incursions.

defense_posture.execSummary: Regional defense posture enhanced on Apr 16 with stratospheric early warning integrations.
defense_posture.appendDetailedSection: As of Apr 16, GCC joint command has established a new datalink protocol integrating SAR assets.

STRIKE_SUMMARY.execSummary: Phase 3 missile exchanges have intensified, with Iran deploying the Fattah-3 variant on Apr 16. GCC integrated air defenses maintained a high interception rate.

adv_munitions_apr18.execSummary: New loitering munition variants deployed as of Apr 18.
adv_munitions_apr18.appendDetailedSection: Debris analysis from the Apr 17 engagements confirms the introduction of the Shahed-136B variant.

naval_blockade_apr18.execSummary: U.S. naval blockade implemented against Iranian ports on Apr 18. Iran retaliated by re-closing the Strait of Hormuz.
naval_blockade_apr18.appendDetailedSection: Following the failure of peace negotiations in Islamabad on Apr 13, a naval blockade came into effect on Apr 18. Iran responded immediately by closing the Strait of Hormuz, escalating regional maritime risks.

Day51_Strategic_Update.execSummary: Introduction of the Khaibar-X missile system on Apr 18 signals a shift in deep-strike capabilities.
Day51_Strategic_Update.appendDetailedSection: The Khaibar-X variant was first tracked entering UAE airspace on Apr 18 before being intercepted.

mil_intel.execSummary: As of Apr 19, allied forces note the introduction of extended-range munitions by Iranian forces, prompting shifts in GCC air defense posture.
mil_intel.appendDetailedSection: New telemetry data from the Apr 17 attacks indicates the use of the Zolfaghar-E variant.

TACTICS_UPDATE.execSummary: Adversary forces have increased the use of synchronized low-altitude UAV swarms intended to saturate coastal radar networks.
TACTICS_UPDATE.appendDetailedSection: Recent data from Apr 19 indicates a shift toward nocturnal, multi-vector drone approaches designed to minimize early warning windows.

ceasefire_status.execSummary: As of Apr 21, high-level negotiations are ongoing in Islamabad to prevent the collapse of the two-week ceasefire set to expire on Apr 22. Iran has demanded $270 billion in reparations.
ceasefire_status.appendDetailedSection: Negotiations face significant hurdles due to Iran's $270 billion reparation demand from Gulf nations (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, Jordan).

naval_operations.execSummary: Increased naval coordination among GCC states observed on Apr 21.
naval_operations.appendDetailedSection: New joint patrols in the Strait of Hormuz have commenced to secure shipping lanes.

tactical_shift_late_april.execSummary: Shift observed toward combined drone and hypersonic attacks on coastal infrastructure.

maritime_domain.execSummary: Naval operations intensified around the Strait of Hormuz on Apr 21, with GCC forces establishing exclusion zones.
maritime_domain.appendDetailedSection: New exclusion zones declared on Apr 21 have severely restricted shipping lanes, prompting alternative routing via overland pipelines where feasible.

ceasefire_stalemate.execSummary: On Apr 21, the U.S. announced an extension of the two-week truce for talks in Islamabad, but Iran refuses to negotiate while the U.S. naval blockade ('Operation Economic Fury') continues.

phase3_overview.execSummary: Hostilities resumed on May 4 (Day 66), ending the temporary ceasefire. Oman reported a unique ceasefire violation on May 6.
phase3_overview.appendDetailedSection: Day 68 saw a localized incident in Oman, which was previously a non-participant. This expands the theater of operations slightly, though the main focus remains on UAE, Qatar, and Bahrain.

Oman_D68.execSummary: A ceasefire violation on May 6 shifted the conflict phase back to ACTIVE.
Oman_D68.appendDetailedSection: Oman recorded an anomalous interception event on Day 68, formally breaking the regional pause.

jadc_integration_may26.execSummary: The operationalization of the Joint Air Defense Command (JADC) has reduced airspace deconfliction errors across GCC states by an estimated 40%.
jadc_integration_may26.appendDetailedSection: Saudi and Emirati radar network synchronization has effectively closed the low-altitude gaps previously exploited by Shahed-136 variants during Phase 1.

Phase3_Air_Defense.execSummary: The introduction of hypersonic glide vehicles and shadow swarm tactics by Iran has stressed existing regional interceptors, slightly lowering overall interception rates in May.
Phase3_Air_Defense.appendDetailedSection: Since May 15, the GCC integrated air defense network has adjusted tracking algorithms to better detect Fattah-2 HGV profiles and EW-shielded drone swarms.

adversary_strategy.execSummary: Shift towards targeting strategic maritime chokepoints and enhanced air defense postures observed since mid-May.
adversary_strategy.appendDetailedSection: Adversary forces have increased SAM network density along the coast, indicating an anti-access/area denial (A2/AD) strategy prioritization.

phase3_maritime_shift.execSummary: Since May 23, kinetic activity has shifted noticeably toward asymmetric maritime threats. USVs and low-altitude drone swarms are increasingly testing UAE and Omani territorial waters in the Gulf of Oman.
phase3_maritime_shift.appendDetailedSection: The introduction of the Shahed-136B variant has stretched the engagement envelopes of coastal defense batteries. Coalition naval assets have established a permanent patrol screen under Operation Nimble Guard to mitigate threats to commercial shipping.

air_defense_shift.execSummary: Transition to integrated tracking architectures improved regional intercept rates since D70.
air_defense_shift.appendDetailedSection: Recent analysis during Phase 3 indicates the operationalization of the Gulf Integrated Air Defense Network (GIADN) has led to a noticeable increase in successful early-warning interceptions between D70 and D90, allowing cross-border cueing of Patriot and THAAD batteries.

TACTICAL_SHIFTS.execSummary: Shift towards decentralized naval harassment and upgraded ballistic missile testing to bypass GCC integrated air defenses.
TACTICAL_SHIFTS.appendDetailedSection: Since Day 80, tracking data indicates a reduction in mass UAV salvos, replaced by targeted 'Shahab-4 Mod 2' deployments and fast-attack craft testing outer defense perimeters.

strategic_overview.execSummary: The ceasefire period (Day 49-65) ended abruptly, transitioning into an active Phase 3 on May 04. A notable ceasefire violation occurred in Omani airspace on May 06 (Day 68).
strategic_overview.appendDetailedSection: Phase 3 relies heavily on the newly formed GCDI network to mitigate asymmetric drone swarm attacks targeting coastal energy infrastructure, following the official collapse of negotiations.

tactical_shifts_phase3.execSummary: Since late May, Iranian forces have shifted to mixed-domain saturation strikes (simultaneous ballistic, cruise, and UAV elements) to stress GCC defense architectures.
tactical_shifts_phase3.appendDetailedSection: Day 80-100 analysis indicates a 40% increase in the use of depressed trajectory MRBMs paired with low-altitude UAV swarms, requiring split-second Terminal Phase Intercepts by Patriot and THAAD batteries.

tactics.execSummary: Since Day 100, Iranian forces have shifted towards low-altitude drone swarms to evade long-range radar networks, forcing GCC defenders to rely on point-defense systems.
tactics.appendDetailedSection: Emergence of Blackout Protocol Corridors and low-altitude ingress tactics.

D93-UPDATE.execSummary: Since Day 80, the conflict has seen an increase in asymmetric naval and drone operations by Iranian forces targeting GCC maritime infrastructure. GCC forces have coordinated 'Operation Gulf Shield' to secure shipping.
D93-UPDATE.appendDetailedSection: New deployments of Sayyad-4B systems along the Iranian coast indicate a shift towards strengthening defensive anti-access/area denial (A2/AD) networks.

MARITIME_SEC.execSummary: Maritime security operations have intensified with the launch of Operation Sentinel Resolve, aiming to protect commercial vessels navigating the Strait of Hormuz.
MARITIME_SEC.appendDetailedSection: Recent deployments include additional US 5th Fleet assets and integrated GCC naval patrols following the end of the ceasefire period.

adv_tactics_phase3.execSummary: Adversary tactics have shifted toward high-speed, low-altitude saturation strikes utilizing Fattah-2 HGVs alongside traditional Shahed swarms.
adv_tactics_phase3.appendDetailedSection: Observations from May 25 to Jun 02 indicate a 15% reduction in overall launch volume but a significant increase in the technical sophistication of the munitions employed.

tactics_evolution.appendDetailedSection: Recent engagements since late May indicate an increased use of Swarm-Evasion Profiles (SEP) by Iranian drone operators. This dynamic spreading behavior significantly increases the intercept calculation burden on regional air defense networks, necessitating overlapping radar coverage.

POST_CEASEFIRE_POSTURE.execSummary: Post-ceasefire shifts in air defense configurations reveal higher integration of automated CIWS and localized EW.
POST_CEASEFIRE_POSTURE.appendDetailedSection: Starting May 04, regional allied forces have shifted from kinetic-first defense to multi-layered electronic countermeasure deployments to preserve standard surface-to-air interceptors.

advanced_tactics_may.execSummary: Since late May, Iranian forces have shifted to combined drone-missile saturation attacks, testing the newly implemented GCC Desert Aegis protocol.
advanced_tactics_may.appendDetailedSection: The introduction of the Ashkan-3 missile variant has notably reduced early warning times for GCC interceptor batteries, requiring more integrated regional tracking data.

gcc_relations.execSummary: Significant divergence has emerged between Saudi Arabia and the UAE over the latter's direct retaliatory strikes on Iran, causing friction within the GCC.
gcc_relations.appendDetailedSection: Saudi Arabia reportedly complained to Washington about the UAE's strikes on Iranian targets, such as Lavan Island, fearing retaliatory attacks on its own energy infrastructure.

naval_domain_phase3.execSummary: Naval clashes intensified in late May (Day 90+) with increased IRGC asymmetric activities in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting joint GCC maritime patrols.
naval_domain_phase3.appendDetailedSection: Starting May 28, the use of fast attack craft and deployment of sea mines marked a shift towards maritime disruption, temporarily affecting localized shipping lanes before countermeasures were deployed.

adversary_tactics_phase3.execSummary: Since Jun 02, adversary tactics have heavily prioritized swarming techniques utilizing Zulfiqar-X munitions to bypass point defenses.
adversary_tactics_phase3.appendDetailedSection: Recent analysis indicates a significant increase in low-altitude drone incursions intended to exhaust interceptor stockpiles before primary ballistic missile launches.

tactical_shift_may26.execSummary: Since May 18, adversary tactics have shifted towards low-altitude, late-night drone swarms targeting secondary infrastructure.
tactical_shift_may26.appendDetailedSection: Recent engagements between May 18 and May 30 indicate a coordinated effort to test the GCC-Nets unified early warning system.

Day90_Escalation.execSummary: Post-Day 90 operational reviews indicate a shift towards Asymmetric Swarm Tactics (AST) to bypass conventional GCC radar nets.
Day90_Escalation.appendDetailedSection: Recent intelligence from May 28 to May 31 suggests the deployment of Sayyad-4B interceptor systems in the northern Gulf to provide anti-air cover for drone launch sites. GCC defense forces have adapted tracking protocols in response.

tactical_shift.execSummary: A definitive tactical shift observed since late May involves the pairing of USVs with aerial loitering munitions to overwhelm coastal defenses.
tactical_shift.appendDetailedSection: On May 29, defensive elements noted synchronized arrivals of air and surface threats, resulting in the establishment of the De-escalation Corridor to clear commercial vessels from engagement zones.

ad_network_density.execSummary: GCC air defense density parameters adjusted to reflect rapid redeployment of US THAAD and Patriot PAC-3 MSE batteries following the early May ceasefire violation.

day95_tactical_shift.execSummary: Entering Day 95, Iranian forces have transitioned from daily massed drone swarms to sporadic, high-altitude loitering munitions deployments and asymmetric naval mining operations near the Strait of Hormuz, testing the newly formed GCJOC air defense network.

appendDetailedSection.title: Phase 3 Shift to Maritime Threats
appendDetailedSection.content: Since the resumption of hostilities (Day 66), adversary tactics have heavily shifted towards Uncrewed Surface Vessels (USVs) in the Gulf of Oman, rather than purely airborne drone swarms. Joint GCC patrols (Operation Coastal Watch) have countered multiple vessel incursions between May 10 and May 30.

STRATEGY_PHASE3.execSummary: Phase 3 is characterized by deep-strike attempts utilizing maneuverable SRBMs and saturated loitering munition swarms intended to overwhelm terminal defense systems.
STRATEGY_PHASE3.appendDetailedSection: The deployment of Zolfaghar-E missiles suggests an adaptation to GCC THAAD and Patriot interception rates recorded during Phase 1.

cyber_infrastructure.execSummary: Iran has initiated a 'Digital Chokepoint' strategy, expanding attacks from physical shipping to subsea fiber-optic cables and regional AI data centers in the UAE and Bahrain.
cyber_infrastructure.appendDetailedSection: As of Jun 1, IRGC forces are demanding protection fees for subsea cable maintenance and launching drone strikes against digital infrastructure in the Gulf, signaling a significant shift in the conflict's asymmetric domain.

POST_CEASEFIRE_TACTICS.execSummary: Following the breakdown of the ceasefire on Day 66, hostilities resumed with a shift in tactics, utilizing Khaibar-X variants to test GCC defense networks.
POST_CEASEFIRE_TACTICS.appendDetailedSection: The resumption of active conflict on May 04 saw coordinated drone and missile strikes across multiple GCC states. Defensive grids have adapted by sharing early warning telemetry in real-time.

DEFENSE_POSTURE.execSummary: GCC integrated air defense systems have shifted to elevated alert status (Gulf Shield Phase III) as of late May, focusing on rapid drone swarm identification and interception.

tactical_shift_d80_plus.execSummary: Since May 18, IRGC forces have increasingly relied on mixed-vector saturation attacks launched simultaneously from Kish Island and mobile mainland launchers.
tactical_shift_d80_plus.appendDetailedSection: The introduction of the Zolfaghar-E SRBM alongside low-altitude drone swarms starting May 20 has severely tested the Gulf Shield Integrated Protocol.

tactical_shifts.appendDetailedSection: Recent data from late May (Day 90+) indicates a shift towards utilizing layered drone swarms over the Arabian Gulf, prompting the GCC to implement Distributed Sensor Mesh tactics to improve low-altitude detection rates.

air_defense_readiness.execSummary: GCC air defense coordination reaches peak integration with the operationalization of the GCC-JDIC on Jun 3.
air_defense_readiness.appendDetailedSection: The establishment of the GCC Joint Defense Intelligence Cell on Jun 3 allows for seamless sharing of tracking telemetry among member states, dramatically reducing target acquisition latency for incoming ballistic threats.

TACTICS_PHASE_3.execSummary: Following the ceasefire collapse, Iranian forces have shifted strictly to Coordinated Multi-Vector Attacks (CMVAs), utilizing hypersonic glide vehicles alongside conventional drone swarms to stress GCC air defense architectures.
TACTICS_PHASE_3.appendDetailedSection: Between May 08 and Jun 03, OSINT and GCC defense sources note a marked increase in the deployment of Fattah-2 platforms, often claimed via regional semi-official channels like Tasnim News Agency shortly after launch sequences.

kinetic_trends_phase3.appendDetailedSection: New weapon systems introduced post-Day 80 include the Kheybar-Shekan 2 MRBM, first documented on May 20. In response, GCC forces activated Operation Resolute Shield on May 23 to integrate regional sensor data and improve detection of advanced maneuverable reentry vehicles.

PHASE3_ESCALATION.execSummary: Introduction of Kheybar-Shekan 2 MRBMs by Iranian forces on Jun 02 marks a technological escalation, prompting the activation of the GCC Silent Shield Protocol.
PHASE3_ESCALATION.appendDetailedSection: The new MRBM variants have stressed interceptor batteries, requiring multi-layered strategies.

GULF_DEFENSE_UPDATE.execSummary: Post-ceasefire (Day 65+) escalations have prompted renewed defense posturing among GCC states, particularly regarding coastal infrastructure protection.
GULF_DEFENSE_UPDATE.appendDetailedSection: Recent intelligence indicates the deployment of Fattah-2 systems, requiring enhanced interoperability within the Operation Resolute Gulf framework.

adv-intel-d75-d96.execSummary: Intelligence reports from Phase 3 indicate a shift towards maritime hybrid warfare, specifically using DSD tactics. Gulf states have countered with Operation Desert Aegis, significantly increasing radar integration.
adv-intel-d75-d96.appendDetailedSection: The adoption of civilian vessel launch platforms requires updated ROE (Rules of Engagement) for coalition navies operating in the Strait of Hormuz.

tactical_shifts_day80_96.execSummary: Since late May, Iranian forces have shifted towards mixed-munition swarms (SST) utilizing newly introduced Zelzal-3B rockets against coastal infrastructure.
tactical_shifts_day80_96.appendDetailedSection: Observations from May 18 to Jun 3 indicate a reliance on volume rather than precision, testing the endurance of regional interceptor stockpiles. The integration of SST strategies has necessitated rapid redeployment of Point Defense systems in the northern Emirates.

TCT_06.appendDetailedSection: As of early June, Phase 3 of the conflict has seen Iranian forces increasingly utilize 'swarm' tactics combining Fattah-3 missiles with Shahed-136 drones to overwhelm layered defense systems.

phase_3_tactics.execSummary: Phase 3 operations increasingly feature multi-axis drone swarms attempting to penetrate layered coastal defense networks. The introduction of the Shahed-149 marks a strategic pivot toward higher-altitude approaches.
phase_3_tactics.appendDetailedSection: Recent interceptions between May 28 and Jun 09 indicate a shift from direct kinetic strikes towards ISR (Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance) probe flights aimed at mapping GCC air defense radar frequencies.

munitions_evolution.appendDetailedSection: Since mid-May, analysis of downed munitions indicates a shift towards Shahed-136B variants, prompting adjustments in GCC interception protocols under Operation Resolute Guardian.

PHASE_3_ESCALATION.execSummary: Phase 3 operations have seen a shift towards asymmetric drone swarms targeting secondary infrastructure, tested against the new GCC IEWS.
PHASE_3_ESCALATION.appendDetailedSection: The deployment of Shahed-149 drones marks a tactical evolution, focusing on depleting interceptor stockpiles.

EW_CORRIDOR_DOHA.execSummary: Increased electronic warfare jamming in the Al-Udeid corridor has severely degraded civilian GPS tracking in surrounding civilian sectors since late May.
EW_CORRIDOR_DOHA.appendDetailedSection: Reports confirmed on Jun 01 show localized GPS spoofing resulting in intermittent civilian aviation rerouting, corroborating shifting tactical emphasis toward spectrum dominance.

adversary_capabilities.appendDetailedSection: Recent intelligence and OSINT analysis from late May to early June 2026 (Days 85-104) indicate the introduction of Shahed-149 Gaza HALE drones into the Northern Gulf theater, expanding the adversary's ISR and strike loitering capabilities.

mil_intel_d97.execSummary: Phase 3 of the conflict is characterized by an increase in asymmetrical drone swarm tactics following the collapse of the mid-conflict ceasefire. Focus has shifted from ballistic salvos to low-altitude loitering munitions.
mil_intel_d97.appendDetailedSection: Recent intelligence indicates deployment of Arash-2 systems from forward operating bases, challenging GCC low-altitude air defense radars.

Tech_Escalation.execSummary: By early June, both sides demonstrated significant capability adaptations. GCC forces integrated interim directed-energy weapons (PEG) to handle loitering munitions, while Iranian forces debuted the Fattah-3 terminal-maneuvering RV to bypass traditional PAC-3 interceptions.

maritime_interdiction_phase3.execSummary: Since Jun 01, allied forces have detected a surge in Iranian AIS spoofing and unmanned surface vessel (USV) deployments, leading to the formal establishment of the Strait of Hormuz Interdiction Zone.
maritime_interdiction_phase3.appendDetailedSection: Recent intelligence highlights an uptick in asymmetric maritime threats as the conflict extends into its third phase. Specialized units have been deployed by Gulf nations to counter these threats, backed by intensive monitoring from international OSINT maritime tracking entities.

IRAN_DRONE_TACTICS_PHASE3.execSummary: Iranian forces have shifted from swarming tactics to low-altitude, terrain-masking single-drone incursions since mid-May.
IRAN_DRONE_TACTICS_PHASE3.appendDetailedSection: Analysis of late May trajectories indicates a deliberate adaptation to evade GCC radar networks, utilizing the mountainous terrain of the Musandam Peninsula before diving toward coastal targets.

BHR_CEASEFIRE_STATUS.execSummary: The fragile April ceasefire remains highly unstable, evidenced by fresh interceptions of ballistic missiles over Bahrain in early June.
BHR_CEASEFIRE_STATUS.appendDetailedSection: Despite ongoing bilateral peace talks between the U.S. and Iran, regional tensions persisted when a significant escalation saw three Iranian ballistic missiles targeting Bahrain intercepted on Jun 03.

late-may-escalation.execSummary: Phase 3 saw renewed missile exchanges starting on May 20 following the collapse of the ceasefire.
late-may-escalation.appendDetailedSection: The re-ignition of active hostilities has been characterized by swarm drone tactics combined with Zolfaghar-26 SRBMs.

adversary_tactics.appendDetailedSection: Recent deployments of the Shahed-149 platform beginning late May represent a notable shift toward HALE reconnaissance and strike coordination, requiring GCC forces to adjust PAC-3 and THAAD engagement envelopes.

POST_CEASEFIRE_RESUMPTION.execSummary: Hostilities resumed aggressively following the Day 49-65 ceasefire period. IRGC-N activity in the Strait of Hormuz has intensified.
POST_CEASEFIRE_RESUMPTION.appendDetailedSection: New Iranian sources such as Nour News and Tasnim have increasingly challenged GCC interception claims.

ADV-12.execSummary: Adversary tactics have shifted towards integrated strike packages combining low-flying UAVs with high-trajectory ballistic missiles.

AIR_DEFENSE.execSummary: Air defense networks integrated across GCC airspace resulting in higher intercept rates during Phase 3.
AIR_DEFENSE.appendDetailedSection: The establishment of the GACC has allowed cross-border tracking of incoming munitions, improving early warning systems.

tactical_shift_june.execSummary: Adversary forces have shifted from high-cost ballistic missile salvos to attritional warfare utilizing large-scale LMS (Loitering Munition Swarms) targeting critical nodes.
tactical_shift_june.appendDetailedSection: This shift aims to deplete GCC interceptor stockpiles while maximizing economic anxiety, particularly focusing on desalination plants and off-shore rigs.

KINETIC_UPDATE.appendDetailedSection: On Jun 05, coalition air defense networks fully integrated tracking for Khorramshahr-4 ballistic profiles, increasing High-Altitude Intercept (HAI) success rates over the Arabian Peninsula.

Day98_Strategic_Update.execSummary: Since Day 95, defense operations have seen a significant pivot towards enhanced coastal and low-altitude radar deployments across GCC states.
Day98_Strategic_Update.appendDetailedSection: Recent OSINT and official releases indicate a concentrated effort to close low-altitude detection gaps following recent escalations.

GULF_AIR_DEFENSE_PHASE3.execSummary: Post-ceasefire Gulf air defenses have shifted from static regional defense to integrated, multi-layered GCC coordinated interceptions to counter renewed Phase 3 hostilities.
GULF_AIR_DEFENSE_PHASE3.appendDetailedSection: Following the ceasefire collapse, the integration of radar telemetry between Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, and the UAE has reduced interception reaction times. Beginning on May 04, THAAD systems assumed primary responsibility for high-altitude ballistic threats, supported by continuous CAPs (Combat Air Patrols).

hypersonic_escalation_p3.execSummary: Since Phase 3 resumption, the conflict has seen the introduction of advanced maneuverable reentry vehicles.
hypersonic_escalation_p3.appendDetailedSection: The deployment of new missile profiles on May 23 required modified GCC air defense firing doctrines, specifically regarding PAC-3 MSE intercept geometries.

Kuwait-Jun3-Update.execSummary: On Jun 3, the fragile ceasefire was violated when Kuwait International Airport's Terminal 1 was struck by an Iranian drone and missile attack, resulting in 1 fatality and 63 injuries. This led to Kuwait expelling Iranian diplomats and a retaliatory U.S. strike on Qeshm Island.
Kuwait-Jun3-Update.appendDetailedSection: The Jun 3 strike on Kuwait International Airport caused significant disruption, grounding commercial flights. Iran denied responsibility, blaming a malfunctioning U.S. Patriot missile, a claim CENTCOM rejected as 'totally FALSE', backed by Kuwaiti CCTV footage.

maritime_escalation.execSummary: Since Day 80, maritime confrontations have spiked in the Strait of Hormuz, with the Zulfiqar Naval Militia employing swarm tactics against commercial shipping.

appendDetailedSection.title: Late May Maritime Escalation
appendDetailedSection.content: Following the breakdown of the Phase 2 ceasefire, IRGC naval elements increased fast-attack swarm tactics. Operations peaked between May 20 and May 30, leading to the deployment of 'Operation Silent Gulf' by US NAVCENT to counter drone telemetry.

june_escalation_kuwait.execSummary: The conflict escalated significantly in early June 2026. Following US actions to disable a blockade-running tanker (M/T Lexie), Iran launched a massive retaliatory wave of missiles and drones against Kuwait, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, and Dubai. A direct drone strike on Terminal 1 of Kuwait International Airport caused civilian casualties and severe structural damage, marking a dangerous expansion of target sets.
june_escalation_kuwait.appendDetailedSection: Furthermore, diplomatic strains between the US and GCC states emerged publicly, with Saudi Arabia and the UAE refusing airspace clearance for US retaliatory strikes to prevent wider economic fallout.

caoc_destruction.execSummary: On June 5, 2026, defense media revealed that the US Combined Air Operations Center (CAOC) at Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar was destroyed by Iranian ballistic missiles during the conflict's opening phase. While personnel had pre-emptively relocated to Shaw AFB in South Carolina preventing casualties, the loss of this multi-billion dollar strategic hub has forced a paradigm shift away from centralized forward-deployed headquarters.

phase3_swarm_tactics.execSummary: IRGC aerospace forces have shifted to highly compressed time-on-target windows in Phase 3, launching ballistic missiles concurrently with pre-positioned loitering munitions to saturate GCC radar trackers.

maritime_threat_evolution.execSummary: Maritime threat matrix expanded in June with the verified introduction of Zulfiqar-3 ASBMs, significantly elevating the risk to commercial shipping.
maritime_threat_evolution.appendDetailedSection: Recent intelligence from early June indicates Iran has actively deployed the Zulfiqar-3 ASBM. This variant features enhanced maneuverability during the terminal phase, complicating interception for standard SM-2/SM-6 effectors deployed by coalition forces.

tactical_shift_maritime.execSummary: Adversary forces have shifted toward maritime-launched drone swarms since mid-May to bypass mainland-focused early warning systems, complicating GCC naval operations.

kinetic_phase3.execSummary: Since May 18, Phase 3 operations have seen a shift towards high-altitude UAV deployments and targeted naval blockades.

maritime_blockade.execSummary: As of early June, US forces have disabled multiple vessels enforcing a maritime blockade, including the VLCC M/T Lexie on Jun 2, escalating retaliatory strikes on Gulf infrastructure.

june-escalation.execSummary: Following the April ceasefire, hostilities flared up in early June. A missile strike on Kuwait International Airport on Jun 03 caused civilian casualties, and a subsequent exchange on Jun 05 and Jun 06 saw US forces destroy Iranian radar sites, while Iran launched seven ballistic missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain.
june-escalation.appendDetailedSection: The ceasefire was broken by a deadly strike on Kuwait International Airport on Jun 03. Subsequently, on Jun 05 and Jun 06, US forces targeted Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites in Goruk and Qeshm Island. Iran retaliated with seven ballistic missiles towards Kuwait and Bahrain; six were intercepted by allied defenses.

phase3_tactical_shifts.execSummary: Following the collapse of the diplomatic ceasefire on May 04 (Day 66), Phase 3 operations have been characterized by rapid, decentralized swarm attacks (P3RO) countered by GCC structural defense adaptations.
phase3_tactical_shifts.appendDetailedSection: Strategic analysis from IISS and Defense News indicates that intercept rates have stabilized due to the new Swarm Munitions Intercept Concept (SMIC). Early warning sharing across the Hormuz Littoral Shield has mitigated infrastructure damage despite increased launch volumes.

maritime_denial_phase3.execSummary: Since Jun 01, adversary tactics have shifted from coastal bombardment to establishing deep-water exclusion zones.
maritime_denial_phase3.appendDetailedSection: IRGC Navy vessels have been actively enforcing the HEZ using fast attack craft and coastal missile batteries, marking a significant escalation in Phase 3 economic disruption.

threat_matrix_phase3.appendDetailedSection: On Jun 8, intelligence and OSINT sources confirmed the integration of Shahed-238B variants in strike packages targeting the Strait of Hormuz approaches. These variants demonstrate advanced evasive maneuvers compared to earlier iterations observed in Phase 1.

Phase3_Tactical_Shift.execSummary: Following the collapse of the ceasefire on May 04, IRGC forces shifted from land-based theater ballistic missiles to dispersed maritime drone swarms.
Phase3_Tactical_Shift.appendDetailedSection: Analysis of intercepts between May 04 and Jun 05 indicates a strategic shift. By deploying 'Ghost Swarms' from fast attack craft in the Gulf, adversary forces are attempting to reduce early warning times and bypass long-range THAAD/Patriot detection.

phase_3_escalation.execSummary: Phase 3 operations are defined by the introduction of Fattah-2 glide vehicles and persistent Shahid-149 drone deployments following the collapse of the Day 49-65 ceasefire.

CEASEFIRE_STATUS.execSummary: The Apr 08 ceasefire brokered by Pakistan remains in effect but is categorized as holding contested due to localized violations, such as the Jun 03 Iranian strike on Kuwait International Airport.
CEASEFIRE_STATUS.appendDetailedSection: Military activity has shifted from massive barrages to isolated tactical exchanges, requiring continuous U.S. and GCC air defense readiness and prompting retaliatory U.S. strikes on Qeshm Island on Jun 03.

adv-tactics-jun8.execSummary: Adversary forces have increasingly utilized 'Ghost Swarm' decoy tactics to probe air defenses during Phase 3 operations.
adv-tactics-jun8.appendDetailedSection: Recent engagements on Jun 8 indicate a shift towards mixed-payload swarms, further complicating integrated air and missile defense (IAMD) coordination.

tactical_evolution_d101.execSummary: Adversary tactics have shifted toward coordinated multi-axis drone swarms combined with ballistic missile feints.
tactical_evolution_d101.appendDetailedSection: Recent observations since Jun 2 indicate a tactical evolution. Swarm attacks are increasingly used to exhaust interceptor inventories before the launch of heavier munitions.

karrar3_deployment.execSummary: Introduction of Karrar-3 UCAVs has slightly reduced GCC interception rates from 94% to 89% for specific low-altitude profiles.
karrar3_deployment.appendDetailedSection: Electronic warfare adaptation is ongoing, with UAE's Al-Bariq system showing early success since its deployment on May 20.

tactical_update_phase3.execSummary: Phase 3 has seen a shift toward the use of advanced ballistic systems like the Fattah-2 by Iranian forces, prompting the GCC to implement 'Operation Iron Shield' for tighter, integrated intercept windows.
tactical_update_phase3.appendDetailedSection: Since Day 70, the establishment of the Coastal Exclusion Zone (CEZ) has reduced collateral maritime incidents. However, the introduction of hypersonic glide vehicles has required coordinated GCC radar tracking to maintain high intercept rates over hardened military targets.

PHASE3_HGV_THREAT.execSummary: The introduction of Fattah-2 HGVs marks a significant tactical escalation, forcing adjustments to terminal-phase interception protocols by integrated GCC air defense networks.

phase3_tactical_shift.execSummary: Adversary forces have shifted toward localized electronic warfare and deployment of longer-range UCAVs like the Shahed-149 to bypass overlapping GCC air defense networks.

phase3_escalation.execSummary: Following the breakdown of the ceasefire on May 04, adversary forces resumed sustained UAV and ballistic missile operations against GCC energy infrastructure and military installations.
phase3_escalation.appendDetailedSection: Phase 3 (Days 66+) has seen a strategic shift toward more complex, multi-vector swarms utilizing advanced drones like the Shahed-149.

strategic_overview_phase3.execSummary: Phase 3 hostilities have intensified with the deployment of Shahab-4 ER missiles and coordinated drone swarms, prompting Operation Coastal Shield by GCC states.
strategic_overview_phase3.appendDetailedSection: Recent intelligence from early June indicates a shift in Iranian tactics towards extended-range ballistic strikes and drone saturation attacks. The GCC's launch of Operation Coastal Shield reflects a coordinated regional response to secure key maritime choke points and intercept incoming threats earlier in their flight paths.

D85-102_UPDATE.execSummary: Hostilities resumed with localized drone swarm probing along the GCC coastline, primarily targeting UAE and Qatar airspace.
D85-102_UPDATE.appendDetailedSection: Since May 25, new loitering munition variants have been identified, prompting the activation of the Coastal Sentinel Grid across the Gulf.

STRAT_UPDATE_JUN9.execSummary: Phase 3 hostilities have escalated with the introduction of new anti-ship munitions and coordinated swarming UAV tactics. Coalition forces activated the Gulf Shield Framework on Jun 2 to improve interception rates.
STRAT_UPDATE_JUN9.appendDetailedSection: Day 90 to Day 102 observed a shift towards asymmetric maritime disruption, prompting the activation of new GCC defense frameworks.

tactics_update.execSummary: Phase 3 has seen a shift toward low-altitude, radar-evading drone incursions, prompting new joint GCC defensive postures.
tactics_update.appendDetailedSection: Since Jun 2, adversarial forces have increasingly utilized the Kaman-26 loitering munition. GCC responses include Operation Pearl Shield, focusing on enhanced coastal radar networking and localized interception capabilities.

intel-update-phase3-start.execSummary: Hostilities resumed aggressively on May 4 (Phase 3), with Iran deploying advanced hypersonic glide vehicles and enhanced electronic warfare to degrade GCC air defense coordination.
intel-update-phase3-start.appendDetailedSection: The introduction of Fattah-2 systems and widespread GPS spoofing significantly altered intercept probabilities during the opening week of Phase 3, necessitating a shift to multi-layered, autonomous interception protocols.

june_escalation.execSummary: After relative calm since the Apr ceasefire, direct fire resumed on Jun 6 and Jun 7 with Iran launching missiles at Israeli bases including Ramat David Air Base. On Jun 8, Israel struck the Karun petrochemical complex in Mahshahr. Hostilities paused by Jun 9 following US demands.

adv_tactics.execSummary: Since Day 75, Phase 3 strikes have demonstrated complex integration of Kheibar Shekan-2 MRBMs and Shahed drone swarms, prompting the activation of the GCC 'Operation Iron Shield' radar-sharing network.
adv_tactics.appendDetailedSection: The deployment of Kheibar Shekan-2 missiles highlights a shift toward evasive terminal trajectories. The GCC response, Operation Iron Shield, was formalized on May 20, linking UAE, Saudi, and Bahraini sensor grids to improve early warning and interception rates by an estimated 15%.

Day100_Intel_Summary.execSummary: As of Jun 9, advanced early warning systems show increased preparations for new drone swarm launches.
Day100_Intel_Summary.appendDetailedSection: Phase 3 active operations continue. The recent introduction of Khatam-2 tactics indicates a shift in adversary strategy.

PHASE3_RESUMPTION.execSummary: Following the collapse of the ceasefire on May 3, the conflict transitioned into decentralized, asymmetric strikes primarily targeting maritime assets.
PHASE3_RESUMPTION.appendDetailedSection: Day 68 (May 5) saw the first confirmed ceasefire violation involving an Omani interception of a rogue drone off the Musandam peninsula, followed by a steady increase in Swarm-D utilization.

tactical_shift_may.execSummary: Since May 20, a noticeable shift towards low-altitude multi-vector drone strikes has challenged regional defense networks.
tactical_shift_may.appendDetailedSection: The introduction of the Shahed-136B variant has stretched early warning systems, prompting the initiation of Operation Sentinel Strike for enhanced GCC coordination.

ADV_TACTICS.appendDetailedSection: Recent observations since Day 80 indicate a shift towards mixed-altitude saturation strikes combining low-flying UCAV variants with high-altitude ballistic profiles. This method is specifically designed to desynchronize GCC integrated air defense tracking layers and force higher interceptor expenditure.

AEROSPACE_DEFENSE.execSummary: Phase 3 has seen a shift toward countering low-altitude maritime drones and ballistic missile volleys targeting critical export infrastructure. Interception rates remain high (92%+) but debris fallout continues to pose persistent localized risks.

appendDetailedSection.title: Phase 3 Air Defense Shift (Jun 01 - Jun 10)
appendDetailedSection.content: GCC layered defense has increasingly relied on offshore early warning pickets to detect sea-skimming drones earlier. The introduction of localized C-RAM near key energy hubs has reduced debris impact in critical zones.

kinetic.execSummary: Since Jun 07 (Day 100), hostile actions have seen a qualitative shift towards coordinated drone swarms targeting secondary coastal infrastructure.
kinetic.appendDetailedSection: Recent intelligence indicates the deployment of advanced Shahed-149 drone units targeting desalinization and port facilities, prompting enhanced air defense patrols across the AGZ (Arabian Gulf Zone).

phase3_tactics.appendDetailedSection: Phase 3 (Day 95 onwards) has seen a distinct shift from saturated swarm tactics to high-altitude precision strikes utilizing Shahed-149 Gaza platforms, complicating early warning radar detection and forcing NATO allies to deploy higher-tier interception assets across the GCC.

diplomatic-status-day100.execSummary: Diplomatic efforts have centered on the Tehran Protocol as of early June, attempting to break the deadlock regarding the naval blockade.

kinetic_phase_3.execSummary: Phase 3 operations indicate a shift towards precision strikes targeting maritime choke points and critical coastal infrastructure.
kinetic_phase_3.appendDetailedSection: Recent intelligence from early June suggests ASBM deployments near coastal regions, increasing the threat to international shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz.

air_defense_adaptations.execSummary: GCC air defenses have shifted to a multi-layered integrated protocol (Operation Resolute Shield) to counter the introduction of maneuvering Kowsar-4 MRBMs.
air_defense_adaptations.appendDetailedSection: Following the introduction of the Kowsar-4 on May 25, UAE and Saudi defense forces noted a 15% drop in single-shot kill probability, requiring ripple-firing of interceptors. The May 28 activation of Operation Resolute Shield has pooled regional X-band radar data, restoring interception success rates to near 90% by Jun 08.

PHASE_3_ASSESSMENT.execSummary: The ceasefire established on Day 49 officially collapsed on Day 66, transitioning the conflict into Phase 3. The resumed hostilities are characterized by more sophisticated, coordinated drone swarm attacks against key infrastructure.
PHASE_3_ASSESSMENT.appendDetailedSection: Day 66 onward marks Phase 3, resulting in increased operational tempo for regional air defense networks.

weapon_systems.execSummary: Phase 3 has seen the introduction of Fattah-2 HGVs by adversary forces, prompting the GCC to implement Operation Iron Shield protocols.

drone_tactics.execSummary: Adversarial forces have increased the use of Asymmetric Naval Swarms alongside coordinated multi-vector UAV attacks during Phase 3, stretching local air and sea defenses.

ADV_TACTICS_JUNE.execSummary: Post-Day 80 adversary tactics reveal a shift towards low-altitude UAV swarming synchronized with GPS-spoofed ASBMs targeting Gulf shipping lanes.
ADV_TACTICS_JUNE.appendDetailedSection: Recent deployments of the Shadow-Net EW system alongside Zolfaqar-e-Basir missiles require updated intercept protocols by GCC naval task forces. Phase 3 engagements heavily prioritize electronic counter-countermeasures (ECCM) and shared target telemetry under Operation Swift Shield.

advanced_tactics.execSummary: Since Day 95, Iranian forces have shifted towards utilizing high-altitude drones and sea-skimming cruise missiles in multi-axis coordinated swarm attacks to stress regional air defenses.

mil_strategy.execSummary: Shift towards defensive maritime posturing observed as of Jun 13.

GULF_AIR_DEFENSE.protects: Abu Dhabi, Al Dhafra, critical infrastructure
Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Ruwais, Fujairah, Al Minhad
Riyadh, PSAB, Ras Tanura, Yanbu, Shaybah
Kuwait City, Ali Al Salem, Camp Arifjan
Strategic sites — cruise missiles, large rockets
Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Al Dhafra AB, Barakah NPP
Riyadh, Dhahran, Jubail, Yanbu, Ras Tanura
Kuwait City, Ali Al Salem AB

UAE-1.rawClaim: MoD cumulative issued Mar 1: B=132 intercepted/137 detected, U=195/209. Deaths: 1 Pakistani (debris nr Zayed Airport).
UAE-1.derivation: Day 1 = opening baseline; no prior day to subtract. Detected/intercepted split from MoD territory/sea breakdown.

UAE-2.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=165,C=2,U=541. Daily delta: B=33,C=2,U=346.
UAE-2.derivation: ΔB=165-132=33, ΔC=2-0=2, ΔU=541-195=346. Deaths cumulative 3 (Pakistani, Nepali, Bangladeshi).

UAE-3.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=174,C=8,U=689. 9 drones fell on territory.
UAE-3.derivation: ΔB=9, ΔC=6, ΔU=148 from cumulative delta.

UAE-4.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=186,C=8,U=812. FIRST BM to land on UAE territory.

UAE-5.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=189,C=8,U=941. Lowest daily BM (3).

UAE-7.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=205,C=8,U=1184. All 9 BM destroyed.

UAE-8.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=221,C=8,U=1305. BM surge — 16 det highest since Day 2.

UAE-9.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=238,C=8,U=1422. Highest daily BM (17 det).

UAE-10.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=253,C=8,U=1440. SHARP drone drop (18 from 117).
UAE-10.derivation: ΔB=9 (int), ΔU=35 det / 26 int. Leakers: 9 drones penetrated (25.7% fail rate). DISPUTE: Wikipedia attributes 141 injuries to this day — actually Mar 14 per MoD text. KT reports 117 injuries vs MoD 122.

UAE-11.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=262,C=8,U=1475. WORST DRONE PENETRATION: 9 of 35 got through.
UAE-11.derivation: ΔB=9, ΔU=35 det / 26 int. Deaths reach 6.

UAE-12.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=268,C=15,U=1514. CRUISE MISSILE ESCALATION — 7 new CM.
UAE-12.derivation: ΔC=7 (first since Day 3). Signals new weapon deployment.

UAE-15.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=294,C=15,U=1600. Fujairah bunkering hub fire.

UAE-16.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=298,C=15,U=1606. LOWEST DAILY VOLUME (10 total).
UAE-16.derivation: ΔB=4, ΔU=6. Capability degradation signal confirmed.

Qatar-1.rawClaim: MoD/MoI joint presser (issued Mar 1, covers Feb 28): 65 BM + 11 drones + 2 aircraft intercepted. 2 BM struck Al Udeid.
Qatar-1.derivation: Feb 28 vs Mar 1 timing ambiguity: strikes began late Feb 28, presser issued Mar 1 covering first 24h. Treated as Day 1.

Qatar-3.rawClaim: 7 BM + 5 drones + 2 Su-24 bombers ALL intercepted. Qatar F-15QAs shot down 2 Iranian Su-24MK.
Qatar-3.derivation: First QEAF air-to-air kill. QatarEnergy declared Force Majeure.

Qatar-10.rawClaim: CUM AS OF MAR 10: 151 missiles, 69 drones, 2 aircraft targeted. 143M/53D/2A intercepted.
Qatar-10.derivation: Most comprehensive Qatar total. No updated cumulative through Mar 16 — true total substantially higher.

Bahrain-1.rawClaim: BDF: ~45 missiles, ~9 drones intercepted. US 5th Fleet HQ Juffair DIRECTLY STRUCK.
Bahrain-1.derivation: DISPUTE: AP/Stars&Stripes (Mar 5) reported 75M intercepted (65 destroyed), 124D (88 downed, 36 LANDED). BDF uses 'intercepted and destroyed' — true penetration rate higher than BDF implies.

Bahrain-9.rawClaim: SITRA ATTACK: 32 civilians injured incl. 17yo girl, 2-month-old infant. BAPCO Force Majeure.

Saudi-3.rawClaim: Ras Tanura Refinery struck by drone → fire at 7:04am. 550k bpd shut.
Saudi-3.derivation: No Saudi cumulative issued — incident-based. Fragmented bulletins with overlap risk.

Kuwait-2.rawClaim: 6 US SOLDIERS KILLED at Port Shuaiba (drone struck TOC). Cum ~97 BM + 283 drones intercepted.
Kuwait-2.derivation: Day 2 Kuwait bulletin covers ~36h. Breaking Defense '97BM/283D' consistent with later FDD tallies.

Kuwait-9.rawClaim: Drone struck 2 KAFCO fuel tanks inside Kuwait International Airport; large fire, no casualties. Source: Arab Times citing DGCA via KUNA.
Kuwait-9.derivation: Date confirmed as Mar 8 (Day 9). Treated as U-High: Arab Times cites official aviation authority DGCA.

Kuwait-10.rawClaim: Two drones caused explosions in fuel tanks near Kuwait International Airport. Another drone struck Public Institution for Social Security building — material damage.
Kuwait-10.derivation: Single source (Arab Times). Treated as U-Med. Consistent with broader Kuwait attack pattern.

Oman-2.rawClaim: Port of Duqm: drone hit mobile worker housing unit injuring 1 expatriate; 2nd drone intercepted (debris near fuel tanks). Near Khasab: tanker Skylight 4 crew injured, 20 evacuated.
Oman-2.derivation: Muscat Daily local reporting. Two separate incidents. Treated as U-High for Duqm (worker name/employer cited); U-Med for Khasab (MSC relay).

Oman-3.rawClaim: Drone-boat strike on tanker MKD VYOM off Muscat killed 1 crew member; 21 crew evacuated. Duqm: fuel tank struck, no casualties. Salalah: drone fell near port, no losses.
Oman-3.derivation: MKD VYOM fatality: Muscat Daily confirmed, cross-referenced with MSC/maritime reporting. Duqm fuel tank: Muscat Daily. Salalah fall: Times of Oman.

Oman-12.rawClaim: Several drones were downed while others struck fuel tanks at Salalah Port. No human casualties recorded. Several tanks damaged. Source: ONA via Times of Oman.
